Name: Elder Law of Michigan
Description:
Elder Law of Michigan is a nonprofit organization whose mission is to advocate for, educate, and assist our target populations. While our services address the needs of many different people, we continue to target our services to older adults and persons with disabilities. For over 25 years, we have provided no-cost counseling on legal, pension, housing, nutrition, and benefits access. We also provide direct and collaborative partner assistance to organizations that work with our target populations. 

We receive financial and in-kind support from many sources, including the U.S. Administration for Community Living, Michigan Department of Health and Human Services, Michigan’s Aging and Adult Services Agency, the National Council on Aging, the Michigan State Bar Foundation, various community organizations throughout Michigan, volunteers, and donors. 

Elder Law of Michigan, Inc. was incorporated in 1990 and has provided award-winning programs and services for over 25 years. An all volunteer Board of Directors provides governance to the organization. Our management team has a combined 80 years experience in managing and serving in non-profit organizations. 

Additionally, Elder Law of Michigan is always interested in volunteers who want to join with others to bring justice, economic security, and safety to the lives of vulnerable families. Volunteers are important, and are a huge contributor to our success. We are dedicated to providing a service-learning environment for our volunteers. 

Opportunities to volunteer include:
- Research and report on legal issues
- Provide client advice and assistance
- Prepare correspondence
- Update publications such as internal legal resources, web content, and brochures.
Call us at 866.400.9164 or sign up online at www.elderlawofmi.org/volunteer.

Name: ElderWise Housing Solutions
Description:
Our mission is to give seniors and their families an easier way to sell a family home and, in doing so, helping to reduce any transitional burden that comes as one ages.  
We saw the stress that seniors and their families went through when it was time to transition out of a life long home.  Elderwise Housing Solutions was created to solve common problems families face when selling a family home.  In doing so we hope to help a family focus on spending more time with their loved one and helping them find the care they might need.

Our Easy Transitions Program helps solve typical issues seniors face when selling home.  Whether the home is outdated, damaged, has deferred maintenance, very messy or the senior needs to move quickly we can help.  We work with the senior and their family to purchase the home "as is" from them.  This allows for a much smoother transition from the home.
Here are some of the ways the Easy Transitions Program can help...
There is no need to update or fix problems with the home.  
Take the possessions that they want and leave the rest in the house.  We dispose or donate unwanted items for the family.
There is no need to clean the house...or keep it clean for closings.
There is flexibility in the closing.  For those families that need to move quickly we can close in 7 days.  
We allow a two week post close occupancy. This allows families to get the money from the sale to pay for care then return later to get any items that might be wanted.
There is also the certainty of the sale with not having to deal with the financing or other contingencies. 
We care for and guide seniors and their families through the process from start to finish.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Zelf aan Zet
Description:
Zelf aan Zet is een samenwerking van 4 zorgondernemers ondersteund door de Alzheimer Liga Vlaanderen.

Het is onze missie ervoor te zorgen dat mensen met een zorgbehoefte zorgeloos kunnen leven. Vandaag en morgen. Hiervoor willen we hen en hun mantelzorgers ondersteunen op verschillende domeinen.

Gezondheid blijft de belangrijkste factor voor een dynamisch leven met eigen keuzes. Maar er speelt zoveel meer als je langer zelfstandig wilt wonen. Ouder worden gaat gepaard met tal van uitdagingen. Ook mensen met een beperking staan voor tal van praktische en financiële uitdagingen.

Met dit initiatief willen we:
 Mensen bewust maken dat langer zelfstandig wonen haalbaar en betaalbaar is, mits de juiste ondersteuning op maat.
 Aan mantelzorgers kenbaar maken dat ze er niet alleen voorstaan.
 Zorg en welzijn actoren aangeven dat onze activiteiten aanvullend en complementair zijn en niet concurrentieel.
 Naambekendheid verwerven bij zowel onze doelgroep als bij de 'traditionele' professionals.
 De hand reiken naar iedereen die actief is rond ouderenwelzijn, want we geloven heel sterk in samenwerken en co-creatie.

Heeft jouw organisatie of bedrijf interesse in een infosessie, heb je een geschikte locatie om ons welkom te heten of heb je nog een heleboel vragen nadat je op onze website bent terechtgekomen? Aarzel zeker niet om contact met ons op te nemen!

We have and continue to experience a high level of growth in all of our programs. We are NDIS registered and offer numerous NDIS services including:

- Psychosocial Recovery Coaching
- Support Coordination
- Supported Independent Living (SIL)
- Short-term Accommodation (STA)
- Medium-term Accommodation (MTA)
- Respite
- Community Access 
- Assistance with Development of Daily Living and Life Skills
- Assistance with Travel and Transport 

Our team are highly trained and are committed to providing and encouraging ongoing personal and professional development of our staff. We utilise a highly person-centred approach in all support of our participants and in all of our programs. 

We have extensive experience supporting people with dementia, people who have a physical disability, and people who have a psychosocial disability. We have extensive experience supporting participants who are culturally and linguistically diverse. We also have extensive experience providing aged care supports.

Name: Voice for Kali Foundation
Description:
Kali was a girl with Cerebral Palsy. 

She was left at the mercy of others who refused to accept her uniqueness and the fact that people can be different.

Kali, unable to defend herself, speak for herself died a lonely death and was buried like a queen.

A queen who was left dirty while alive, left hungry sometimes and allowed to wander about in a world that had no respect for persons who are a little different. 

Yes she had no voice, but I have become her voice! 

To speak against discrimination of any form meted to Persons like her.

VFK works for a world where every Persons living with Disability is treated with respect and dignity. 

We are here to champion and model diversity, equity and inclusion.

Please kindly stay with us and help anywhere possible.

Thank you!

Name: Fondation Loisirs Pour Tous
Description:
Basée à Châtel-St-Denis, La Fondation Loisirs Pour Tous, créée en 2009, est active dans les cantons de Vaud et de Fribourg. Notre mission est de promouvoir l'épanouissement des personnes en situation de handicap en créant un environnement inclusif et en offrant des activités de loisirs enrichissantes qui favorisent le développement personnel, les rencontres sociales et le bien-être. 

Nous croyons fermement que chaque individu, indépendamment de son handicap, mérite d'avoir la possibilité de s'épanouir pleinement. Nous nous engageons à promouvoir l'inclusion en créant des espaces où chaque personne se sent accueillie, respectée et valorisée. Nous nous efforçons de mettre en place des activités de loisirs qui répondent aux besoins et aux intérêts spécifiques des personnes en situation de handicap. Nous croyons que le loisir est bien plus qu'une simple distraction ; c'est une occasion de développement et d'épanouissement personnel.

Nos activités sont conçues pour encourager le développement personnel des individus en situation de handicap, en leur offrant des opportunités de croissance, d'apprentissage et de dépassement de soi. Nous cherchons à stimuler leur confiance en eux, à renforcer leurs compétences et à favoriser l'émergence de leurs talents uniques. Nous croyons également au pouvoir des interactions sociales pour briser l'isolement et favoriser un sentiment d'appartenance.

Notre vision est de créer un monde sans barrières physiques, sociales et émotionnelles où chaque personne en situation de handicap a la possibilité de participer, de manière autodéterminée, à des activités de loisirs inclusives.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

We are passionate about person-centered care, meaningful relationships, and fostering independence. By creating engaging and tailored programs, we ensure that every individual receives the support they need to reach their goals, build friendships, and feel a true sense of belonging.

At Life Purpose Australia, inclusion isn’t just a value, it’s our purpose.

#EmpoweringLives #NDISProvider #InclusionMatters #LifePurposeAustralia #CommunitySupport #DisabilityInclusion

Dolphin Lifts is a trusted provider and installer of a wide range of mobility solutions, including stair lifts, home lifts, step lifts, hoists, and bathroom adaptations. Our mission is to help individuals maintain their independence and stay in the comfort and familiarity of their own homes for as long as possible, with confidence and peace of mind.

With our expert team of professionals, we offer tailored solutions to meet the unique needs and preferences of each individual. Whether it's a simple stair lift for easy access to different levels of your home, a home lift for added convenience and mobility, or a bathroom adaptation or wet room conversion to ensure safety and accessibility, Dolphin Lifts has you covered.

We understand the importance of maintaining a comfortable and secure living environment, which is why we prioritize your mobility and well-being above all else. Our top-quality products, combined with our exceptional customer service, ensure that you receive the highest level of satisfaction and support throughout your journey with us.

Choose Dolphin Lifts and experience the freedom, comfort, and confidence you deserve, all within the walls of your own home.

Name: Marco Polo Portal - NDIS Housemate Matching
Description:
Welcome to Marco Polo Portal - Innovating Inclusion and Choice in NDIS Housing.

At Marco Polo, we're on a mission to empower NDIS participants with unparalleled choice and control over their living situations. Our platform confronts the challenges of the disability housing market, offering a beacon of hope and progress.

Our Mission: Transform disability housing by enabling participants to "Choose Connections, Select Supports," helping providers "Boost Business, Vanquish Vacancies," and making navigators' work of "Connecting Care, Simplifying Share" easier. We aim to correct power imbalances, making choice and control a reality for every participant.

What We Offer:

For Participants: A platform to select housemates and providers, aligning with your preferences.
For Providers: A way to connect with participants, fill vacancies faster, and showcase ethical service.
For Navigators: A streamlined matching process, simplifying the task of finding suitable housing.

Why Choose Marco Polo?

Innovative Solutions: The first app designed for the NDIS community, rebalancing participant-provider dynamics.
Empowerment Through Choice: We champion the freedom of decision-making, ensuring participants lead their lives on their terms.
Commitment to Quality: Our platform features vetted providers to guarantee a safe, supportive environment.
Join us as we reshape the disability housing market into a more inclusive, empowering community. Marco Polo Portal - Empowering lives, one match at a time.

Opera con passione e professionalità rivolgendo cura, attenzione e rispetto alla persona valorizzando la sua unicità. Motore di tutta cooperativa sono: le persone che accoglie, i soci, i lavoratori, i volontari, gli amici e i sostenitori.

Name: Habile
Description:
Référent accessibilité,

La société Habile accompagne les professionnels dans la mise en conformité de leurs établissements et intervient également auprès des particuliers dans le cadre de l'adaptation de leurs logements. Notre objectif est de permettre à tous de vivre en parfaite autonomie, de leurs domiciles à leurs établissements recevant du public favoris, en passant par la voirie, les transports et les installations ouvertes au public. Nous proposons des solutions d'accessibilité non-stigmatisantes, adaptées à tous. 

Services aux professionnels : 
Nous accompagnons les Etablissements Recevant du Public souhaitant mettre en conformité leurs établissements, en réalisant des diagnostics ou audits. Nous leurs préconisons également du matériel adéquat, nous pouvons même réaliser les travaux d'aménagement et l'achat /la pose du matériel. 
Nous proposons également un service de consulting pour les cabinets d'architecte / Bureaux d'études Ingénierie afin de répondre à des problématiques d'accessibilité spécifiques. 

Services aux particuliers : 
Notre accompagnons les particuliers dans l'adaptation de leurs logements, en préconisant des aménagements adaptés à leurs difficultés du quotidien ainsi qu'à leurs budgets et au délai imparti. Nous proposons des services de conseil, de maîtrise d'oeuvre, d'achat et de pose de matériel adapté.

We promote the values of independence, community integration, personal choice, and self-advocacy for NDIS participants and support every effort in reaching their personal goals. We do everything with highest level of integrity, ensuring you and your family are treated with compassion, dignity and respect.

We services all Areas of Sydney - East, West , North and South of Sydney (Blacktown, Parramatta, Penrith, Strathfield, Redfern, Maroubra, Liverpool, North Sydney, Chatswood)

Our team comprises of staff, registered nurses, EIN who are experienced and have bubbly and happy nature to attaining highest level of care and excellence in what they do.

Name: Elder Services Resource Network - ESRN
Description:
ESRN is a networking and education group for professionals who work with seniors located in Palm Beach, Broward and Miami-Dade counties.  We raise funds throughout the year in numerous ways.  Funds are then used to provide grants to several not-for-profits serving seniors.

Our mission: To enhance and improve the lives of seniors in South Florida by identifying and linking resources through the networking of dedicated professionals and organizational philanthropy.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Technology for Ageing & Disability WA (TADWA)
Description:
TADWA's mission is to help older people, people with disability and their carers to do what is important to them.

Our occupational therapists and trade technicians work with thousands of people every year, listening to what is important and helping improve the quality of their lives. TADWA is a ‘team of teams’, encompassing Home Modifications, Recreation & Mobility, Technology & Computer Support, Custom Equipment and Therapy Services.

We're a not-for-profit charity registered under the ACNC and have been making a positive difference to the lives of Western Australians since 1984.

Description: Centre national de ressources au service de la personne épileptique et en situation de handicap rare, de son entourage et des professionnels.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Beyond Disability Services
Description:
We have been touched by a family member who has been impacted by disability who we have been advocating for on his behalf for the past 25 years. At times this can be a real struggle, especially when we consistently deal with services who are 'unable to help' or advise us 'he is not suitable'. We have made the decision to open up a Short Term Accommodation Respite facility to support families who care for a person with a disability like our family member who struggled to find suitable respite for their loved ones. Our background also entails working in the disability industry for the past 25 years so we understand the struggles that families face whilst trying to seek and utilise a trust worthy safe facility for their loved ones

We believe that all families that care for an individual living with a disability deserve a well-earned break from their caring role. We aim to provide a respite service that is run more like a holiday stay for your loved one and will cater to the needs of each individual so our clients will get the most out of life

Beyond Disability Services is committed to creating a safe comfortable environment where the individual will feel at home. We are dedicated to ensuring a service of excellence through a commitment and dedication to empowerment, inclusions and participation
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: La Passerelle surdicécité
Description:
La Passerelle est une structure ressources dédiée à l’autonomie et à l’inclusion des personnes en situation de surdicécité

Cette association Loi 1901 s’inscrit dans une dynamique d’innovation sociale. Elle a été fondée à Toulouse en 2023 pour répondre aux besoins non et mal satisfaits sur le territoire afin d’améliorer la qualité de vie des personnes concernées : 
•	Isolement social
•	Perte d’autonomie dans la vie quotidienne
•	Manque d’accès : aux droits, aux soins et à des ressources adaptées
•	Manque d’accessibilité des Etablissements Recevant du Public (ERP)

La Passerelle apporte une réponse globale à cet handicap méconnu et complexe à travers 3 pôles complémentaires : 

1. Un Groupe d’Entraide Mutuelle (GEM) : Un espace convivial pour favoriser l’entraide, l’inclusion et le pouvoir d’agir
2. Un Pôle Ressources & Expertises : Un dispositif d’évaluation, de conseils et de formation pour faciliter les parcours de vie et améliorer les pratiques
3. Un Pôle Action Autonomie : Un accompagnement individualisé par des professionnels spécialisés pour renforcer l’autonomie au quotidien
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Wisdom Senior Care
Description:
Founded by Carolyn Thurston in 2006. Wisdom Senior Care’s mission is dedicated to helping seniors live happily and comfortably in their own homes. Put simply, our vision is to change the way families view aging, by bringing personalized care programs that empower elders while enabling close time with their loved ones.

Our care providers bring companionship, personal attention, transportation, Alzheimer's care, respite care, and more to your home. Always outstanding in their uniforms and caring for others, our staff is known as the “purple people” and they’re loved in every community they’re in.

Why are we in demand? 

As 11,200 Americans reach senior age each day, Wisdom Senior Care stands as a leader in the franchising and healthcare industry, bringing sustainable solutions for families who don’t want their elders to spend their last years in a far-off community home, and entrepreneurs who want to support them. Our program is one of the fastest growing franchises in the US.

Who should work with us? 

Becoming a franchisee with us means you’re entering a phase of your professional career that surpasses an average 9-5. Not only are you getting the opportunity to fill a much needed gap, full of compassion, in your area, but you’re going to: 

Achieve better work-life balance through becoming more flexible with your time.
Becoming the boss and not constantly reporting or working for someone else’s success. 
Building a business that can lead to true financial freedom. 
Growing the business, enough where it becomes a family ‘legacy’ that you can pass down to your kids.   
Uncapped income. Your success is in your own hands. The more efficient and strategic you are, the more you will make. 
Learning from the best professionals the senior case space through our seasoned network of leaders, franchisees, and training/ support resources. 

If you’re curious to learn more, you can reach us at info@wisdomseniorcare.com or visit https://wisdomseniorcare.com/
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: BJ Care Services PTY LTD
Description:
Who We Are

Established in 2018, BJ Care Services is a trusted NDIS provider specialising in supporting individuals with complex behaviours. With over 30 years of combined experience in the disability sector, our directors lead a passionate team dedicated to helping participants achieve greater independence, well-being, and a higher quality of life.

Who Do We Cater To:
We provide disability support to individuals of all ages across Melbourne and Victoria, including children, adults, and families. Our services are tailored to those with complex behaviours of concern and unique needs, ensuring that each participant receives the support required to achieve their goals and live independently in the community.

What We Do
At BJ Care Services, we offer a wide range of tailored services to meet the unique needs of every participant. Our services include:
 STA and Respite Care
 Supported Independent Living (SIL)
 Specialist Disability Accommodation
 Emergency & Short-Term Accommodation
 Mid-Term Accommodation
 Person-Centred Education
 Social & Community Participation

We provide person-centred care that focuses on individual goals and aspirations, ensuring each participant receives the support they need to thrive.

Why Choose Us?
What sets us apart is our commitment to delivering high-quality, individualised care and support. Our team understands the unique challenges faced by those with complex behaviours and works tirelessly to provide solutions that foster independence and community inclusion.
 Person-Centred Approach – Tailored support plans that prioritise the individual's needs
 Experienced Leadership – Our directors bring 30 years of expertise in the disability sector
 Reliable & Trusted – Fully registered NDIS provider adhering to the highest industry standards

Get In Touch
Need help with disability support services or navigating the NDIS? Connect with BJ Care Services today and let us support your journey to independence.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Communauté 360 Loiret
Description: Handicap : des solutions près de chez vous
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: EDIA Social
Description:
EDIA es una empresa de impacto social positivo, que presta un servicio especializado en la atención de personas en situación de dependencia y en protección de menores. Se trata de una compañía sin ánimo de lucro fundada en la comarca de El Andévalo (Huelva) hace 20 años, cuyos beneficios revierten en los territorios en los que opera. 

La empresa desarrolla su actividad profesional en la prestación de servicios sociales para garantizar el bienestar de las personas. 

Dispone de una amplia experiencia en ayuda a domicilio y gestiona centros especializados de atención asistencial para mayores y para quienes se encuentran en situación de dependencia, así como de acogimiento residencial de menores. Igualmente, imparte talleres de educación en valores para la infancia. En 2023 ha puesto en marcha un servicio de catering para promover el bienestar social mediante una alimentación de calidad.

EDIA Social, con sedes en Villanueva de los Castillejos (Huelva) y en Almuñécar (Granada), tiene como principal prioridad prestar un servicio profesional de la máxima calidad que sitúe a las personas en el centro, con el fin de garantizar su calidad de vida. 

En este sentido, mantiene como valores la igualdad, la equidad, la sostenibilidad y la transparencia en su gestión.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: iCOMMUNITY AUSTRALIA
Description: At iCOMMUNITY, we support individuals with disabilities in exercising choice and control, achieving their goals, and becoming active community members. With our experienced and professional support workers, we think outside the box to provide the proper support at the right moment, creating moments that matter as we break down barriers and strive for a more inclusive society.

Name: Ryan’s Gifts of Advocacy, Inc
Description: Our foundation provides services and textbook scholarships to college/vocational students who have a disability or disorder.  Prospective homeless college students who have never known anything but a transitory lifestyle often strongly believe education provides a way out of their current situation.  Whether you help through monetary donations or spreading our mission through word-of-mouth, thank you.

Name: Faaborg Rehab Technic ApS
Description:
Faaborg Rehab Technic er eksperter i at skabe specialtilpassede hjælpemidler til børn og voksne. 

Vores mission er at hjælpe personer med at overkomme fysiske vanskeligheder gennem specialudviklet rehabiliteringsudstyr, så de får større glæde i hverdagen.

Faaborg Rehab Technic blev grundlagt i 1984 af Niels Erik Johansen, og blev særligt kendt på at udvikle Faaborg Liften. Siden har vi udviklet og forhandlet en bred vifte af hjælpemidler fra lifte og cykler til kørestole, badekar og vægte. Alt produktudvikling og produktion foregår i tæt dialog med vores kunder, hvor vores fokus er stabilitet, enkel og funktionel betjening, høj service og lang levetid.

Alle vores produkter er gennemtestet af eksterne testinstitutter (HMI og Tüv) og CE-mærket i overensstemmelse med gældende standarder. Firmaets grundlægger og ejer har igennem flere år deltaget i det internationale standardiseringsarbejde for rehabiliteringsudstyr. 
Faaborg Rehab Technic henvender sig til flere kundegrupper og leverer bl.a. totalløsninger og specialudviklede løsninger til: 

- Hospitaler/Hospices 
- Kommuner (Depoter - Sundhedsplejen) 
- Private og offentlige institutioner
- Læger og klinikker 
- Fysioterapeuter
- Dyrlæger

Læs mere om os på https://faaborg-rehab.dk/

Name: Association CRAPS 64
Description:
Depuis 1984, l’association CRAPS gère des structures médico-sociales dédiées à l’accompagnement de jeunes en situation de handicap dans les Pyrénées-Atlantiques. Grâce à une approche centrée sur les besoins de chacun, le CRAPS agit pour une société plus inclusive en proposant les solutions les plus adaptées.
Pour répondre aux besoins spécifiques de chaque jeune, le CRAPS gère des dispositifs complémentaires, chacun pensé pour offrir un accompagnement adapté et favoriser leur épanouissement.

- DITEP - Dispositif Institut Thérapeutique Éducatif et Pédagogique :  Un accompagnement personnalisé pour les jeunes de 0 à 20 ans en situation de handicap. Ce dispositif favorise leur inclusion sociale et scolaire, en collaboration avec les familles, les établissements scolaires et les structures de droit commun. Les interventions s’articulent autour de trois services de proximités implantés sur Pau et Mourenx.

- Dispositif d’Appui : Face aux délais d’accès au DITEP du CRAPS, ce dispositif aide les parents dont leur enfant est inscrit sur la liste d’attente à trouver des solutions pratiques et des ressources adaptées en s’appuyant sur le milieu ordinaire.

- EMAS Béarn et Soule : inclusion des élèves en situation de handicap, en intervenant sur les circonscriptions de Pau, Orthez, Oloron-Sainte-Marie, et une partie de la Soule.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Nourished Not Deprived
Description:
An NDIS and Medicare Provider of Dietetics, Occupational Therapy, Speech Pathology, Psychology & Support Work

Nourished Not Deprived was born from lived experience - from knowing what it feels like to fall through the cracks, to fight for care that feels human, and to wish there was an easier way to be supported.

That’s why we created NND. To make sure no one else has to go without the help they deserve.

We’re a community of Allied Health and Support Work professionals who lead with heart, understanding, and purpose. Our mission is to make accessing care feel easy, safe, and empowering - whether that’s through Dietetics, Occupational Therapy, Speech Pathology, Psychology, or day-to-day Support Work that’s guided by clinical expertise.

Every one of our Support Workers is trained and supervised by our Clinical Manager, an Occupational Therapist with over a decade of experience - because quality care starts with understanding the why behind what we do.

From Telehealth to home visits, after-hours care to weekend sessions, we meet people where they’re at - across every corner of Australia. Accessibility shouldn’t be a privilege; it should be the standard.

At Nourished Not Deprived, we don’t just deliver services - we build a community where participants, families, and practitioners all thrive. When our team feels supported, our clients feel it too.

Because everyone deserves to be nourished, not deprived.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Knight & Day Disability Support Services
Description:
Knight & Day Disability Support Services is a local South Australian family owned & operated NDIS disability support provider servicing the northern Adelaide region. Our dedicated team is committed to ensure all our participants receive the highest quality care to enable them to remain in peace and happiness. Our dedicated team of careers can help you to maintain and regulate your day-to-day life and where you have every possibility to be your own and live an independent life.

Knight & Day Disability Support Services is a new and innovative South Australian Disability Support Provider, we provide support for individuals and their families from the age of 7 to over 65 years of age with all disabilities.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: FECPA - Fondation des Etablissements Cantonaux pour Personnes Agées
Description: La FECPA, Fondation des Etablissements Cantonaux pour Personnes Agées, regroupe 7 institutions pour personnes âgées représentant plus de 400 résident.e.s et 700 employé.e.s. Elle est formée de 6 homes médicalisés (Home médicalisé Les Arbres, Home médicalisé Le Clos, Home médicalisé Les Charmettes, Home médicalisé De l’Ermitage, EMS La Sombaille, Home médicalisé Le Temps Présent), 1 pension (Pension Le Logis) et 15 appartements avec encadrement (Les Vignes du Clos), ainsi qu’une administration centrale. Le FECPA assure tous les types de mission que prévoit la planification médico-sociale du canton de Neuchâtel pour ainsi accueillir dans les meilleures conditions et avec un encadrement adapté les personnes âgées.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Núcleo DISCA
Description:
Con un abordaje multi y transdisciplinario, la agenda de DISCA se centra en el estudio de los procesos y mecanismos a través de los cuales las personas con discapacidad se transforman en ciudadanas. 

En tres años abordaremos cómo se construye y negocia la ciudadanía en los ámbitos de 
a) la participación política, 
b) el ejercicio de la sexualidad y reproducción y 
c) el acceso a la salud. 
Cada uno de estos ámbitos constituye una línea de desarrollo del Núcleo. 

Una cuarta línea de trabajo se centrará en los desafíos de utilizar metodologías inclusivas y participativas en investigación en discapacidad.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: BASScare Aged Care Services
Description: Since the 1950's BASScare's sole purpose has been to provide high quality, all-encompassing care for older Australians in our community. We are committed to every stage of the journey. From the moment a little assistance is required, our extensive, industry-leading services are there, adjusting and adapting to evolving needs and desires. Our members come to us because they want to be part of a community who'll be with them through every step of life's latter stages. Once you enter the BASScare community, you'll have the support you need for the rest of your life.

Description: Client Care First (CCF) is a registered NDIS service provider. We provide support to people with disabilities and their families in the Illawarra and Shoalhaven regions.

Name: The Wisdom Club
Description:
The Wisdom Club is a sanctuary where eldercare is redefined through compassion, expertise, and community. 

Inspired by the belief that aging is not a limitation but an opportunity to thrive, TWC is driven by the vision of six founding members to create a comprehensive facility or SoHo house for senior citizens—a space where they wouldn’t feel alone. 

When conceptualizing our eldercare ecosystem, the goal is to create more than just a service; it is to build a community that celebrates every life experience. 

At our first state-of-the-art facility in Vile Parle, Mumbai, we blend medical expertise, personalized care, advanced technology, and meaningful engagement to foster a life of purpose and joy. 

Families trust us to care for their loved ones as our own, ensuring a seamless balance of health, happiness, and well-being.

For more enquiries, please send us an email on twc@thewisdomclub.com.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Advocacy for Disability Access and Inclusion
Description:
Advocacy for Disability Access and Inclusion (ADAI) is a not-for-profit, independent, community-based organisation providing free, professional, & confidential advocacy support to people living with disability, their families & carers across South Australia. With over 35 years of experience, ADAI has a proud history of standing alongside individuals and families to improve rights, inclusion, and wellbeing, we offer short-term, issue-specific advocacy to help individuals navigate services, resolve concerns, and uphold their rights. Our services include things like: support with the NDIS, navigating complex systems, housing, education, employment, health, referrals &  access to essential services.

ADAI also offers a range of interactive, accessible and free workshops, designed to build knowledge, confidence & capacity, strengthen self-advocacy skills, and empower people with disability. These sessions explore real-life issues such as navigating services, self-advocacy skills, budgeting 101, skills for employment, mental health & wellbeing, and more. Our workshops are always tailored to the needs of attendees and delivered in a supportive, inclusive and accessible environment. If you would like to request a specific topic, please fill out the form at: https://forms.gle/hq7wr5ZBtM4Taint9

Need support? Our team works across both metropolitan & regional South Australia, including outreach to rural & remote communities; you can come by, call, or connect, we'll be here. 

  (08) 8340 4450
  advocacy@advocacyfordisability.org.au


You don't have to do it all alone, we're with you.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Maya VR
Description: Maya VR is een innovatief bedrijf dat virtual reality-technologie inzet om de ouderenzorg te verbeteren. Het biedt een VR-platform dat is ontworpen om niet alleen entertainment te bieden, maar ook om therapeutische en educatieve ervaringen te ondersteunen. Het aanbod, MayaHome, integreert VR in de dagelijkse zorgtaken, variërend van wondverzorging tot emotieregulatie, wat zorgverleners helpt tijd en energie te besparen. Door de inzet van VR draagt Maya VR bij aan de verlichting van de werkdruk voor zorgpersoneel in een snel vergrijzende markt.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Plateforme de répit PR57
Description:
La plateforme de répit s’adresse à tout aidant s’occupant d’une personne atteinte d’une maladie neurodégénérative, en perte d’autonomie ou atteinte d’un handicap.
Les missions de la Plateforme d’accompagnement et de Répit s’inscrivent dans les 4 axes de la Stratégie Nationale pour soutenir et accompagner les aidants de personnes en situation de handicap et en perte d’autonomie. 
La mission générale de la PFR57 est de s’appuyer sur une démarche citoyenne, solidaire et inclusive, pour prévenir le risque d’épuisement des aidants non professionnels de tout aidé en proposant des prestations d’accompagnement, de soutien et de relais accessibles, adaptées ou adaptables à leurs besoins et attentes et à leur situation géographique, économique, sociale, familiale et professionnelle.
En effet, la plateforme d’accompagnement et de répit a pour missions de :  
-	Répondre aux besoins d’information, d’écoute, de conseils, de relais et de formation des proches aidants pour les conforter dans leur rôle d’aidants et les accompagner dans leurs démarches administratives dans une logique de proximité,  
-	Participer au repérage des besoins et attentes des proches aidants et du binôme aidant-aidé,  
-	Favoriser la mise en relation du couple aidant-aidé vers diverses prestations de répit ou de soutien (à domicile, en ESMS, vers des activités de loisirs et de vacances y compris en milieu ordinaire),  
-	Favoriser le maintien de la vie sociale et relationnelle de la personne en situation de handicap et de son aidant et lutter contre le repli ou l’isolement du proche aidant ou du binôme aidant-aidé.  
 Au travers d’une écoute attentive, l’évaluation des besoins et des attentes de l’aidant et du binôme aidant-aidé vise à apprécier l’opportunité des actions d’information, de soutien, de formation et des prestations de répit proposées et disponibles sur le département.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: BDMS Community Services BDMSCS, Registered Service Provider Since 2019
Description:
BDMS Community Services provides caring assistance to people who live with a disability. Our organisation was established in 2019, and our team has more than 20 years of direct community experience. We work across the disability and aged care sectors, with our friendly and highly motivated team specialising in dementia care.

Our organisation was established in response to the growing demand for disability solutions. With a lack of high-quality services and congruent care packages, people with needs weren't getting the support they deserved. We work hard every day to support people in need, with our team partnering with care recipients and their families to find solutions that work.
At BDMS Community Services, we help people to identify needs, set goals, and find pathways towards achievement. We want people to live their best possible life, and we believe everybody can reach their potential. Whether it's satisfying the activities of daily living, advancing independence, or participating in the local community, we help people to move forward and live life with confidence.

Our qualified and experienced team delivers consistent 24/7 care for all disability-related issues. We're proud to work with a diverse range of cultures and are happy to provide support at any level of disability or stage of life. At BDMS Community Services, we deliver exceptional community-based care with a smile.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Le Clos du Nid de l'Oise UNIVI Handicap
Description: Fondée en 1967 sous l’impulsion de l’abbé Oziol, notre association n’a eu de cesse de développer son offre et sa capacité d’accueil en préservant ses valeurs bienveillantes et sa mission historique : assurer l’éducation, les soins spécialisés et l’insertion sociale d’enfants, d’adolescents et d’adultes porteurs d’un handicap mental. Aujourd’hui, notre devoir est de préserver intacte l’action d’utilité sociale engagée depuis 56 ans, d’en garantir les valeurs et de poursuivre encore le développement de nos activités. De nombreux défis attendent les acteurs du handicap dans les années à venir. L’évolution des prises en charge et la forte hausse du nombre de personnes à accompagner d’ici 2030 en sont deux illustrations. Notre association répondra présente pour relever ces défis, à vos côtés.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: DSX - Disability Services Exchange
Description:
Unified Advocacy
A single, powerful voice with Government, using verified data to influence policy on pricing and quality.

Radical Transparency
Members commit to public self-assessment, giving participants clear, ethical choices for the first time.

Shared Strength

Participant-Led Governance
Participants hold a real, technology-enabled voice in DSX's direction, ensuring we stay true to our mission.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Bowhead Corporation
Description:
Bowhead Corp is an adaptive mobility company designing innovative adaptive bikes, wheelchairs, travel wheelchairs, and mobility solutions for people with disabilities, aging adults, and individuals with mobility challenges.

Our mission is simple: mobility without limits. We build durable, intuitive equipment that supports independence, confidence, and active living—whether for everyday use, travel, or recreation.

Bowhead serves individuals, families, caregivers, and adaptive programs, while partnering with corporations and organizations committed to accessibility and inclusion. Through sponsorships and collaborations, companies can help expand access to adaptive mobility solutions for individuals and community programs.

We believe mobility is freedom—and inclusive design can unlock opportunity at every stage of life.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Coastal Aged and Disability Care
Description:
Coastal Aged and Disability Care (CADC) is more than just a service provider—we are a dedicated team committed to making a real difference in the lives of individuals who need support. We believe that everyone, regardless of age or ability, deserves to live a fulfilling, independent, and dignified life.

With a compassionate and highly experienced team, we provide Supported Independent Living (SIL), Community Access, In-Home Support, and Personal Care tailored to each individual's unique needs and goals. Our focus is on building meaningful relationships with our clients, ensuring they receive not just the right support, but also the respect, care, and empowerment they deserve.

At CADC, we understand that true care goes beyond just providing services—it’s about listening, understanding, and being there every step of the way. We take pride in fostering a community where individuals feel safe, valued, and supported to live life on their own terms.

Our Mission is to leverage technology and community resources to inform families, reduce emotional barriers and ease the process of rightsizing in order to build better relationships and reduce tension while aging.

Day in and day out, everything we do is aimed at helping people of all ages live their best life!

Our History
Systems Unlimited was founded as a nonprofit in 1971 by a group of parents who wanted to ensure those we care for can live in safe, comfortable houses locally and be taught skills that allow them to live as independently as possible. We are proud to live that mission everyday for the last 50+ years! 

Our Staff Sets the Gold Standard
Our diverse, creative, and caring staff is nearly 1,000 strong. They are passionate about helping people of all ages live as happy, healthy, and independently as possible by providing direct care that is personalized and accredited. We believe in building one-on-one relationships with those we care for. Our organization and people are proud to set the gold standard in Eastern Iowa and beyond. 

Employment Opportunities
Whether you are searching for an entry level direct support position, supervisory, or management opportunities, there is no greater place to look than Systems Unlimited! Here, you can build a career that will have a direct impact on hundreds of people’s lives. Reach out about our position openings and see how you can help people live their best lives!

Name: Ability Range
Description:
Navigating the complexities of daily life with delays, disabilities, or neurodivergence presents unique challenges. Many NDIS participants struggle to develop essential skills and maintain daily routines, leading to feelings of frustration and dependency. For NDIS service providers, delivering tailored support that meets these diverse needs while staying aligned with NDIS guidelines and optimizing business operations can be equally challenging.

 Addressing Unmet Needs
Unaddressed issues can prevent NDIS participants from achieving their full potential, resulting in less fulfilling, less independent lives. NDIS service providers may also miss opportunities to enhance their services and increase revenue streams, limiting their ability to make a meaningful impact.

 Challenging Misconceptions
There is a common misconception that traditional support methods are sufficient for all NDIS participants and that digital solutions are too complicated or impersonal. In today's digital age, the right app can provide personalised, accessible, and effective support, enhancing daily lives and elevating service delivery for providers.

 Introducing Ability Range
Ability Range is an innovative app designed to transform the NDIS experience. Our app empowers NDIS participants by supporting skill development and enhancing daily routines. It offers a comprehensive profile of each participant, detailing their interests, likes, hobbies, and moods, allowing for truly personalised care. 


For NDIS participants, it’s a pathway to greater independence and a more fulfilling life.

For NDIS service providers, Ability Range adds opportunities to enhance their services and increase revenue streams.

Ready to stay ahead of the curve and be part of the future today?
Discover how our app can enhance your business and add an extra revenue stream. 

 Contact Us: 0383759667
 Email Us: hello@abilityrange.au

Description:
Welcome to BSDAP, your partner in revolutionising behaviour support. Proudly built by Aussie Behaviour Support Practitioners for Aussie Participants, BSDAP is dedicated to enhancing the quality of life for participants through personalised, evidence-based support. Our program puts individuals at the heart of their journey, ensuring they control their data & choose their providers.

Participant-Owned, NDIS Funded: Subscriptions are NDIS-funded, enabling participants to own their data & select providers who meet their needs.

Aussie Innovation: Designed by Aussies for Aussies, BSDAP understands NDIS regulations & participant needs.

Person-Centred Approach: Personalise each participant's profile & enhance plans, practice, & reporting with real-time, evidence-based data collection & analysis.

Smart Data, Smart Decisions: Our free mobile app allows stakeholders to track & analyse behaviour patterns in just 20 to 60 seconds, making data-driven decisions easy.

Efficient Use of NDIS Funds: BSDAP is free for practitioners & companies, optimising SBIS funding & saving hours per participant, allowing focus on quality support & care plans.

Deeper Insights: Go beyond frequency, duration, & intensity to understand behaviour, helping identify root causes & effective strategies.

Collaborative Environment: Align support teams & build a community around each participant, fostering a supportive network that ensures everyone is informed & working together for better outcomes.

Implementing Provider Access: Grant access to critical data, including Restrictive Practice data, to ensure transparency & alignment in support strategies.

Support Staff Education: Equip team members with the knowledge to understand behaviour support nuances & person-centred insights, enhancing their ability to deliver high-quality care.

Privacy & Security: We prioritise robust data security & compliance with NDIS regulations & Australian Privacy Laws, ensuring peace of mind for you & your participants.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Support Network / Family Members:

Participants:

CCC complies with all Queensland and Commonwealth Government requirements for the delivery of quality and safe disability support services.

Organisational Values - 

Choice – We value individual choice and support growth

Trust – We honour trusting relationships and build great health outcomes

Partnership – We work in partnership with individuals and their support networks and eliminate power imbalances.

Hope – We always move forward with hope and never give up.

Empowerment – We promote self-determination and capacity building.

Name: Innovative Community Care
Description:
Welcome to Innovative Community Care, a pioneering provider of cutting-edge care solutions dedicated to enhancing the lives of individuals with disabilities and the elderly. With our unwavering commitment to innovation, we strive to revolutionise the care industry by delivering compassionate and personalised support that empowers our clients to lead fulfilling and independent lives.

At Innovative Community Care, we understand the unique challenges faced by individuals with disabilities and the elderly. Our team of experienced professionals are dedicated to designing and implementing innovative care programs that prioritise individual needs, promote inclusivity, and foster a sense of dignity and autonomy.

Through our comprehensive range of services, we aim to address the diverse needs of our clients, encompassing physical, emotional, and social aspects of care. We leverage the latest technologies, research, and best practices to develop tailored solutions that optimise well-being, mobility, and overall quality of life.

What sets us apart is our holistic approach, which recognises that every individual is unique and deserves personalised care. Our compassionate caregivers and skilled professionals work closely with clients and their families to create customised care plans that align with their specific goals and preferences. We believe that by promoting independence, self-expression, and a sense of belonging, we can foster a vibrant and supportive community for our clients.

Innovative Community Care also places a strong emphasis on collaboration and partnerships within the care ecosystem. We actively engage with healthcare professionals, advocacy groups, and local communities to stay abreast of emerging trends, share knowledge, and contribute to the advancement of care practices. By fostering these collaborations, we ensure that our clients benefit from the most advanced and effective care solutions available.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Our Senior Living Solutions team has gone through the experience of caring for our own parents through years of dementia, life-altering illnesses, and just plain old age. Let us help you navigate through this stage of aging parents. Please call us at 770-847-8471.

Vi stræber efter en høj neuropædagogisk og sundhedsfaglig kvalitet. Derfor uddanner vi løbende vores tværfaglige teams så vi fastholder vores nysgerrighed og faglighed indenfor området. Dette er også planen med vores vikarer, så de løbende også bliver klædt på.
Vi lader os inspirere af nyeste viden på hjerneskadeområdet. Vi udfordrer os selv med kollegial supervision samt eksterne supervisorer. Det gør vi fordi, det har vores borgere krav på. Vi gør det også fordi vi skal bevare vores energi, faglighed og varme i arbejdet.
Hos os kan vi fordybe os i arbejdet og tiden til faglige snakke er en del af hverdagen.
Sidst men ikke mindst så har vi det sjovt imens 
Vi har løbende brug for nye kollegaer.
Kontakt Bitten Tobberup på mail : btsw@syddjurs.dk

Name: Mentor Healthcare
Description:
Mentor Healthcare has provided over 100,000 hours of service to clients of diverse abilities. We have helped our clients grow their confidence, learn new skills, improve their fitness, transform their diet and become better equipped to engage with the world around them.

Mentor Healthcare grew out of a passion for fitness, nutrition and people. Since 2019 Mentor Healthcare has grown to one of Adelaide’s leading home-grown NDIS providers of allied health and disability support. Our mentors and practitioners come from a wide range of backgrounds – experienced health practitioners, professional athletes, domestic violence case managers, personal trainers and even a professional dancer. We require two qualities in our staff: empathy and leadership. With these core qualities, and our ongoing training, we believe we have cultivated the best team of disability service providers in Adelaide.

We are growing rapidly and constantly seeking passionate mentors and allied health professionals. Please see our Jobs page or contact us directly to join a fantastic team of committed and fulfilled professionals.

Name: Hervé Transports Services
Description:
HTS Mobilité | Transport inclusif et accompagnement bienveillant pour tous 

Nous sommes une entreprise à taille humaine, née d’un croisement unique entre expertise professionnelle et expérience de vie personnelle, au service d’une mobilité réellement inclusive et humaine.

Spécialisés dans le transport et l’accompagnement des personnes à mobilité réduite (PMR), des seniors, mais aussi de tout public, nous plaçons l’humain au cœur de chaque trajet.

 Plus qu’un simple transport, nous proposons un accompagnement complet, du domicile jusqu’à la destination finale : rendez-vous médicaux, événements, sorties personnelles, trajets professionnels, activités sportives ou déplacements longue distance.

Fondée par Hervé, professionnel formé en TPMR, VTC, auxiliaire ambulancier et diplômé en transport & logistique, HTS Mobilité est née d’une histoire de vie forte : celle d’un frère ayant grandi aux côtés de sa sœur en situation de handicap, en fauteuil depuis sa naissance.

Ce double regard – le vécu et la formation – nous permet d’offrir un service différent, accessible, rassurant et respectueux, en cassant les codes souvent associés au handicap, pour promouvoir l’autonomie, la liberté et la dignité de chacun.

 Basés à Ménitré, nous intervenons dans tout le Maine-et-Loire (49) et proposons des trajets ponctuels ou réguliers, adaptés aux besoins du quotidien comme aux situations exceptionnelles.

Engagés localement, nous collaborons avec des partenaires comme Cap Adapt, Egoïstes Ensemble ou Team Handuo, pour soutenir des initiatives qui mêlent inclusion, mobilité et solidarité.

Parce qu’être accompagné, c’est plus que se déplacer.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

-	La résidence Gallois-Lallier de Villeblevin comprenant 20 résidents
L’équipe est composée, d’un médecin coordonnateur (40%), d’un cadre de santé, d’un psychologue (40%) d’une psychomotricienne et des différents intervenants médicaux et paramédicaux extérieurs (médecins traitants, kiné, pédicure- podologue,) et d’une équipe soignante (AS et ASH) dédiée à l’accompagnement quotidien des résidents.
L’établissement comprend également les équipes administratives, techniques, hôtelières restauration et animation.

Name: ABC Behaviour Support
Description:
ABC Behaviour Support is an NDIS Registered Specialist Behaviour Support Provider. We work with people 7– 65 years of age who are NDIS funded with ‘capacity building’ supports and have ‘Improved relationships’ stated in their NDIS plan.

People centric 
The value of ABC is the people. We represent a collective depth of experience from a range of diverse professional backgrounds. Each professional is appointed for their unique personal qualities which best fit their team and the organisation. Every person; professional, parent, practitioner and participant, contribute to a range of perspectives which inform person centred supports. 
 
Change 
ABC plans for change. We are continuously improving the business model and workflows to provide best practice supports and meet participant needs. We encourage creative thinking and challenge the status quo. We will adapt quickly and consider each situation as an opportunity. Our prime function is to plan for behaviour change. 
 
Practical 
ABC people explore, practical, common sense and simple methods to improve participant quality of life and reduce behaviours of concern. Each strategy should just make sense to implementing providers and consider environmental factors. 
 
Strengths based 
ABC is strong because we invest time and resources into individual strengths. We capitalise on talent and focus on positive experiences to accelerate growth. 
 
Data

Name: Wilhelmiina Palvelut Oy
Description:
Koti Wilhelmiinassa

Jos ikäsi puolesta tarvitset elämääsi lisää turvaa ja huolenpitoa, Wilhelmiina tarjoaa sinulle hyvän kodin. Wilhelmiinassa on koteja erilaisiin tarpeisiin, itsenäisestä asumisesta ympärivuorokautiseen hoivaan.
Wilhelmiinan kodit sijaitsevat Helsingin Pikku-Huopalahdessa ja toukokuussa 2023 avautuvat Wilhelmiina Tenholan yhteisöllisen palveluasumisen ja ympärivuorokautisen hoivan uudet kodit.

Töissä Wilhelmiinassa

Hyvän arjen kokemukset ja elämykset muodostavat perustan Wilhelmiinan toiminnalle - vain osaava ja hyvinvoiva henkilöstö voi tarjota hyvää ja laadukasta palvelua asiakkaille. Satsaamme toimintamme kehittämiseen osallistumalla innovatiivisiin hankkeisiin ja kehittämällä jatkuvasti henkilöstömme osaamista. Meille on tärkeää pitää hyvää huolta henkilöstömme työhyvinvoinnista.

Wilhelmiinassa työskentelee monialainen ammattilaisten joukko - lähi- ja sairaanhoitajia, hoiva-avustajia, fysio- ja toimintaterapeutteja, erikoislääkäreitä, ravintola- ja hallintohenkilökuntaa, vapaa-ajanohjaaja sekä alihankintasopimuksella psykologi ja sosiaalityöntekijä. 

Visionamme on Enemmän näköistäsi elämää. Visio on arkemme punainen lanka, jonka ympärille rakentuu kaikki tekemisemme.

Toimimme myös yhteisellä arvopohjalla. Arvomme ovat:
1. Arvostamme sinua
Jokaisen ihmisen kunnioittaminen ja ihmisistä välittäminen ovat toimintamme keskiössä. 
2. Onnistumme yhdessä
Vaikutamme alamme tulevaisuuteen. Vastaamme ennakoiden toimintaympäristömme ja ihmisten tarpeiden muutoksiin.
Ideoimme, kokeilemme ja opimme avoimin mielin uutta.
3. Uudistamme ja uudistumme
Yhdessä toimien saamme monipuoliset voimavarat ja osaamisen käyttöön.
Opimme toisiltamme ja autamme toisiamme onnistumaan. 
Yhdessä olemme vahvoja ja menestymme toiminnassamme.
Iloitsemme yhdessä saavutuksistamme.
4. Olemme luottamuksesi arvoisia
Rakennamme luottamusta avoimuudella, osaamisellamme ja tekemällä sen, minkä lupaamme. 

Omistajamme on Miina Sillanpään Säätiö.

All Eskleigh services can be accessed through the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), the Motor Accidents Insurance Board (MAIB), Disability Support for Older Australians Program (DSOA) or can also be funded privately.

While much has changed in the disability support sector over the last 70 years, Eskleigh is still not-for-profit and still providing the best in care to Tasmanians right across the state.

I work with the individual to build the skills and confidence to achieve their goals for independent travel. That movement may be learning to get to find their way around their home for a young child, learning your way around kinder, school or another education setting, or learning to cross roads, plan travel routes and catch public transport into the city as a child's independence grows. 
For adults it can be learning to use a mobility aid and travel in different ways to what they may have in the past and building confidence to do that independently if they have lost vision or learning new and more complex travel routes for those who already have the skills in other environments.

Description:
The goal of this program educate and encourage individuals in Washington and other nearby counties with developmental disabilities, such as Autism, Down Syndrome, traumatic brain injuries and other rare genetic disorders, to participate in the game of ice hockey. Our organization encourages the continued development of dependability, self-confidence, improved concentration, improved balance and motor skills, and personal accountability and socialization. The goal of our board of directors and coaches is to create a fun but structured environment for our players in order to provide a positive experience for them and their families. Nos aînés doivent pouvoir compter sur un accompagnement simple, fiable mais surtout humain.

Name: שווים בתעסוקה – מפעל הפיס
Description:
שיעור המועסקים בקרב אנשים עם מוגבלויות בישראל הוא 40% בלבד, כמחצית מהשיעור באוכלוסייה הכללית. המצב אף החמיר בעקבות משבר הקורונה, שפגע בענפים רבים במשק.
פרויקט "שווים בתעסוקה" של מפעל הפיס, "ידיעות אחרונות-ווינט" ואתר "שווים", ופרויקט "עובדים ללא הגבלה" של רשת 13, הפיס ו"שווים" שמו להם למטרה לשנות את הסטטיסטיקה העגומה הזו ולעודד מעסיקים לשלב יותר אנשים עם מוגבלויות בחברות ובארגונים שלהם.

הפרויקטים לעידוד תעסוקה החלו לפני כשנה עם פתיחת קורסים לחניכים עם מוגבלויות ב"ידיעות-ווינט" (עיתונות ויח"צ) וברשת 13 (טלוויזיה ושיווק דיגיטלי). במקביל, יצאה קריאה דרך כלי תקשורת אלה לחברות עסקיות להצטרף ליוזמה. 
מייקרוסופט ישראל הייתה הראשונה לפתוח בוט קאמפ טכנולוגי, ואחריה רשת מלונות פתאל השיקה קורס בישול, ואז סיסקו הצטרפה ועכשיו פרטנר וקליקאיט ועוד ועוד. כתוצאה מההכשרות האלה עשרות אנשים עם מוגבלויות כבר מצאו עבודה.

בינתיים, חברו לפרויקט גם נשיאות המגזר העסקי (דרך הפורום הכלכלי חברתי), שהחליטה לרתום את המעסיקים הגדולים בישראל לנושא, וגם המדינה, באמצעות קרנות הביטוח הלאומי. משהו מתחיל לזוז.

למה פתחנו את הדף הזה? כי ידענו שכאן אתם נמצאים: בעלים של חברות, מנכ"לים, סמנכ"לי כוח אדם, אחראים על גיוון תעסוקתי. האנשים שאנחנו רוצים לשכנע לפתוח עוד הכשרות ולהכניס עוד אנשים עם מוגבלות למעגל העבודה והחיים הישראלי. אז בואו תצטרפו להתחלה של מהפכה ותפתחו את הדלת ואת הלב לעובדים עם מוגבלות.

אנחנו מזמינים ארגונים שמעוניינים להצטרף לפרויקט ההכשרות לפנות אלינו, ולהשפיע יחד איתנו.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Description: Epiphany Care provides disability support services and supports participants to live independently. Services include NDIS plan management and Coordination of support, Accommodation, and Housing, Supported Independent Living (SIL): Mental Health Services, Clinical Service with Behavioural Support, and a Registered Nurse. We have Day Programs and support clients with Community Participation. Our team works with all levels of disability

Name: Open Futures Disability Services
Description:
Open Futures is a leading disability provider dedicated to empowering individuals with disabilities and fostering inclusive communities. We are committed to offering comprehensive support services that enable individuals to thrive and reach their full potential.
At Open Futures, we work closely with individuals, families, and communities to develop personalized plans that promote independence, enhance well-being, and foster social inclusion. Our team of experienced professionals provides a wide range of programs and services to address our clients' unique needs and aspirations.
Our service offerings encompass accommodation assistance, daily living skills training, employment support, social and recreational activities, and advocacy services. We tailor our services to meet the specific needs of each individual, recognizing their unique abilities, goals, and aspirations.
Open Futures prioritizes respect, dignity, and choice. We foster a culture that embraces diversity and celebrates the strengths and contributions of all individuals. Through person-centered planning and a holistic approach, we empower individuals to make informed decisions, exercise control over their lives, and actively participate in their communities.
With a focus on quality and continuous improvement, Open Futures adheres to the highest standards of service delivery. We invest in ongoing training and professional development for our staff to ensure they are equipped with the latest knowledge and skills. Our commitment to excellence is reflected in our accreditation and compliance with industry regulations and standards.
Join us on our journey towards an inclusive and accessible future. Whether you are an individual seeking support, a family member, a community partner, or a passionate advocate for disability rights, Open Futures welcomes your collaboration. Together, we can create a society where everyone can fully participate, contribute, and thrive.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Les Pérégrines SA
Description: Notre mission : «Nous accompagnons les seniors et les personnes dépendantes dans un cadre sécurisant, en veillant à préserver leur bien-être, leur dignité et leur lien social. Avec chaque résident, reconnu dans son unicité et expert de son vécu, nous co-construisons, en collaboration avec ses proches et un réseau de partenaires, un projet de vie permettant d’ajuster en continu les soins et le cadre de vie à ses besoins et aspirations.»

Entreprise régionale, Oval'Access intervient sur les départements du Vaucluse, des Bouches du Rhône, du  Var et des Alpes de Hautes Provence.

CARING FOLKS AUSTRALIA is a company that was established with the sole aim of offering disability services that puts the participant as a priority in creating opportunities, promoting independence and progression in a safe and non-discriminative environment.

Name: Fondation fribourgeoise en faveur des personnes en situation de handicap mental et IMC (FAH-SEB)
Description:
Fondée en 1983 dans le canton de Fribourg, la Fondation FAH-SEB se consacre à l'accueil et à l'accompagnement d'adultes en situation de handicap mental et de handicap moteur cérébral (IMC). Sa mission est de leur offrir un cadre de vie agréable, favorisant le développement de leurs compétences et leur épanouissement.
Pour cela, la Fondation s'appuie sur deux sites principaux : le foyer "La Colombière" à Misery et le home "Le Linde" à Tinterin. Les équipes, composées de professionnels qualifiés, notamment des éducateurs spécialisés, assurent au quotidien le bien-être des résidents, grâce à un accompagnement personnalisé et adapté à leurs besoins.
La Fondation FAH-SEB s'engage à respecter la dignité et les droits de chaque personne accueillie, à promouvoir son autonomie et son inclusion sociale, et à valoriser ses compétences et ses potentiels. Elle travaille en étroite collaboration avec les familles et les proches aidants, et assure une gestion transparente et responsable de ses activités.
Dans le cadre de sa mission, la Fondation stimule les capacités cognitives, crée un environnement chaleureux et bienveillant, et encourage l'autonomie et l'inclusion sociale des personnes qu'elle accompagne.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Going Places Orientation & Mobility
Description:
At Going Places Orientation & Mobility we offer O&M assessment and training to both children and adults of all ages. 

Our developmental O&M services focus on children learning concepts about their world through exploration and play. We encourage children to have agency in the way they move by assisting families and care givers to set up regular routines that foster experiential learning and self-determination, as children work to achieve their goals. 

Our services to adults focus on using the discovery approach to O&M to enhance a learners conceptual understanding of the environment to build life long travel skills. We partner with your to individually tailor training programs around your goals, session frequency, current skill level and preferred learning/teaching style.

We also offer accessibility training in the use of smartphones to increase your ability to connect with others and use these devices for increased independent travel. 

Programs are aimed to offer rewarding experiences to encourage increased independent travel and progression toward your goals. We believe O&M should be fun, challenging and engaging. 

Services are funded through the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) from the Improved Daily Activity area of the plan.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Description: Many Hands' mission is to build and sustain quality care, housing, learning and activities for those with a disability while educating the next generation of service professionals using a Lifesharing model.

Name: Rescare Homes Trust
Description:
Established in December 1994, Rescare was set up to represent the wishes of parents and the relatives of some of the people with an intellectual disability who were previously living at Mangere Hospital. Their wish was for their loved ones to move from an institution to a ‘community within a community’ so that friendships would be kept and new ones made.  The families wanted the new community to have a strong sense of family, whanaungatanga and belonging.

As a result, in August 1995, Rescare Homes Trust opened on our Weymouth site with eight homes. This was followed, in February 1996, by the opening of four homes for 74 adults with an intellectual disability at the Flat Bush site (formally known as Chapel Road).

Today, with 20+ homes, 117 residents and a number of vocational services for both residents and non-residents, and more than 180 staff including a CEO and a capable management team, Rescare Homes Trust is a lifestyle choice for people with an intellectual disability.

Each person is supported with a full and varied life while enjoying the benefits of living in a secure, gated community that has well exceeded the dreams of its founders.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Gilani Mobility Australia
Description:
The Gilani Mobility team has a passion and drive to provide independence solutions offering our clients the opportunity to continue with an active lifestyle. We have an extremely diverse team allowing us to offer holistic solutions for our clients. This team consists of Engineers, Product Specialists, Clinicians and Customer Service Representatives. The knowledge and expertise that our skilled team can offer ensures that our client's individual needs are being met and the process of accessing Assistive Technology (AT) is a stress-free journey. We offer demonstrations and trials of our AT products at our clinical room, your preferred therapist centre or any suitable location that meets the demands of each individual involved in this assessment process. The journey does not finish with Gilani Mobility after a purchase, we are here to offer ongoing support to our clients with our qualified after sale team and technicians.

Our Services

1. Wheelchair Hire and Hire Before You Buy Option
2.Community Transport for Private and NDIS funded
3. Mobility Equipment Repair & Service
4. Accessible Home Modifications and Elevated Solutions
5. Accessible Vehicle Modifications
6. Occupational Therapy Assessment

The Gilani team is committed to ensure our clients have access to a range of high-quality Mobility equipment & Assistive aids to improve their independence and lifestyle.

Our key priority is to ensure our client's needs and expectations are being met at all times. This starts from the top with our Founder Ray Gilani along with our International Business Manager Craig Legender who have a combined 25 years of experience working in the care sector.

We are here for you through every step of your journey from clinical assessment to after sales support so PLEASE DO NOT HESITATE TO CONTACT US to see how one of our dedicated Gilani team members can assist you.

Name: WHEELMOVE

673 / 5000
Getting around in a manual wheelchair is complicated, but wanting to get off the beaten track can quickly become hell: muscle fatigue, injuries, need for cumbersome or expensive solutions to get there.

WheelMove allows People with Reduced Mobility to access new activities like everyone else thanks to a transportable system that is easy to use, affordable and can be installed on any manual wheelchair.

WheelMove thus bring autonomy, comfort and new opportunities to wheelchair users.

WheelMove is a company currently being formed, following incubation phase at Poclain.

Name: Centre de Support aux Personnes Handicapées (CESPHA)
Description:
CESPHA : Est un organisme à but non lucratif qui a pris naissance le 16 juillet 2016, cette association est conçue pour des gens qui vivent avec une déficience physique, mentale, visuelle, et atteintes la surdité. Elle fonde son action sur l’absolu respect de la personne humaine. Elle entend promouvoir la dignité et la citoyenneté des personnes en situation de handicap, de dépendance, de perte d’autonomie ou de difficultés sociales en œuvrant à la fois pour leur complet épanouissement individuel et leur meilleure inclusion  à toute forme de vie sociale.
Elle vise non seulement à adapter la cité aux enfants, adolescents, adultes en situation de handicap de dépendance, de perte d’autonomie ou de difficultés sociales en contribuant le plus possible à la mise en œuvre des conditions matérielles et humaines de leur accueil dans un cadre de vie ordinaire, mais aussi à modifier la perception qu’en a l’ensemble du corps social. Elle entend, en conformité avec les valeurs fondatrices de la laïcité, aboutir à leur pleine reconnaissance tant humaine : même et égale dignité, que sociale : citoyenneté à part entière.
Fonde son action sur les principes dans le respect de la Déclaration Universelle des Droits de l'Homme approuvée par l'Assemblée Générale des Nations Unies le 10 décembre 1948 et la Convention relative aux droits des personnes handicapées adoptée par l'Assemblée générale des Nations unies le 13 décembre 2006 et la convention relative aux droits des personnes Handicapées(CRDPH).
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Id-Dar tal-Providenza
Description:
Id-Dar tal-Providenza was founded by Mons. Michael Azzopardi in 1965 with the aim of offering a home with a warm and family-like environment for persons with disabilities who for some reason or another cannot stay living with their family. Since than Id-Dar tal-Providenza has supported its residents to participate in and enjoy the quality of life within the community.

Today Id-Dar tal-Providenza is made up of five residences: Villa Monsinjur Gonzi, Villa Papa Giovanni and Villa Papa Luciani. It also includes three other residences, called Żerniq in Siġġiewi, Dar Pirotta in Birkirkara and Akkwarell in Qawra.

At the moment, Id-Dar hosts approximately hundred persons between the ages of fourteen and eighty years. It also offers respite services on a regular basis to families of persons with disabilities, thus offering them support to enable the disabled family member to continue to live within their family.

Some of the residents spend their day by attending activities outside the home namely day centres. Few others have a job within the home or outside the home.

Three and a half million euro per year are required for the running of the Homes. Most of the money goes for the wages of about 200 workers who support the residents in their daily needs. About 200 other highly committed volunteers also give their support on a regular basis.

Id-Dar Tal-Providenza, which is built on faith in Divine Providence, relies to a very large extent on the generosity of its benefactors and the public donations it receives to fund the high level of care it provides.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

 Nos valeurs: Reconnaissance de la personne, Engagement, Ouverture

Name: Innov'age Alsace
Description:
Innov’age c’est un programme d’action pour stimuler les innovations et expérimentations pour favoriser le bien vieillir en Alsace. Initiée en 2016 par des acteurs du Bas-Rhin la démarche couvre maintenant l’ensemble de l’Alsace. Depuis 2016 ce sont pas loin de 1 000 participants qui ont pris part à des évènements d’intelligence collective et de réseautage (associations, entreprises, collectivités, enseignants/chercheurs...).
Pus d'info et inscription à la lettre d'information : contact@innovage.alsace / 06 79 29 33 18

Innov'age, c'est :

# Club Innov'age : une rencontre par trimestre entre entreprises, associations, collectivités et enseignants-chercheurs. Pour développer votre veille et votre réseau, chaque rencontre se fait autour d'une présentation et d'échanges entre participants.

# Atelier Innov'age : une rencontre par mois autour d'un projet précis ou d'une problématique transversale à plusieurs projets. Par petit groupe, nous échangeons pour aider à trouver des solutions.
Experts Innov'age : c'est un réseau qui rassemble l'expertise des partenaires institutionnels.

# Accompagnement Innov'age : ces accompagnements sont attribués à des structures à la suite de programmes de type Hackathon ou Sprint.

# Annuaire Innov'age : annuaire des membres de la communauté afin de faciliter les échanges et les prises de contact entre participants.

# Lettre d'information Innov'age : restez informé en vous abonnant à la lettre d'information. Vous y retrouverez les prochains événements, des vieilles thématiques, des appels à projets et des actualités des membres de la communauté.

Innov'age est une initiative de la Collectivité Européenne d'Alsace, co-animée avec l'Adira, le Cep Cicat, France Active Alsace et OpenCare Lab.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Fondazione Oggi e Domani ETS
Description:
La Fondazione Oggi e Domani, promossa da Fondazione Cariparo e da 19 famiglie, è aperta a tutte le famiglie con figli con disabilità ed ha lo scopo di promuovere l’inclusione sociale delle persone con disabilità, creando con le stesse, sin dalla più giovane età dei figli, percorsi che consentano loro di esprimere al massimo le proprie attitudini e potenzialità, avendo il focus specifico sulla gestione degli aspetti riguardanti il “Dopo di Noi”.

La Fondazione Oggi e Domani partirà dal progetto di vita della persona con disabilità, ovvero dall’immaginare per lei un futuro possibile e compatibile con le sue abilità. Nella preparazione del progetto di vita verranno coinvolte, oltre alla famiglia e alla persona con disabilità, anche le istituzioni (in primis ULSS e servizi sociali dei comuni), l’amministratore di sostegno se nominato, gli operatori degli enti del terzo settore che supportano la famiglia, nonché la rete informale della comunità di appartenenza.

Il progetto di vita si concretizza con l’elaborazione di una strategia articolata che coinvolge una pluralità di soggetti impegnati nell’erogazione dei servizi desiderati e nel costruirne la sostenibilità economica mobilitando contributi da diverse provenienze: famiglia d’origine, Istituzioni, Enti pubblici e privati, associazioni, persone fisiche donatrici e comunità del territorio.
La Fondazione Oggi e Domani privilegerà la sua azione nello sviluppo della rete sociale a sostegno dei fragili, della loro protezione giuridica, dell’inclusione lavorativa, della loro autonomia abitativa e della gestione dei loro patrimoni destinati dalle famiglie o dai donatori.
