Name: Espaço Ikaria Centro-dia para Idosos
Description:
No Espaço Ikaria Centro Dia para Idosos, acreditamos que a terceira idade é uma fase preciosa da vida que merece ser aproveitada ao máximo. Somos um Centro Dia especializado em proporcionar um ambiente acolhedor e estimulante, onde idosos independentes ou semi-independentes podem passar o dia desfrutando de atividades prazerosas, convívio social e cuidados dedicados. Nossa equipe dedicada está comprometida em oferecer qualidade de vida, estímulos cognitivos, troca de experiência e atenção individualizada, para que cada um possa viver com alegria, prazer e aprendizado.

Atendimento durante o dia: Oferecemos um local seguro e confortável para idosos passarem o dia, enquanto suas famílias estão ocupadas. Eles desfrutam de um ambiente inclusivo, cercado por profissionais experientes e atenciosos.

Atividades prazerosas: Promovemos uma variedade de atividades recreativas, culturais e terapêuticas para manter nossos idosos engajados e entusiasmados. 

Isso inclui com destaque: Pilates, Jogos Interativos, Estimulação Cognitiva, Fisioterapia Preventiva, Dança Senior, Hortoterapia, Cinema, Arteterapia, Musicoterapia, Música Ambiente, Bingo, Aula de Dança Livre e festas Temáticas.

Convívio social: Estimulamos a interação e o convívio social entre os idosos, criando um ambiente de apoio e amizade. 

Organizamos eventos e celebrações especiais para fortalecer os laços e criar memórias duradouras.

Cuidados personalizados: Nossa equipe altamente capacitada oferece cuidados individualizados, levando em consideração as necessidades e preferências de cada idoso. Garantimos a administração adequada de medicamentos, alimentação balanceada e assistência atenciosa em todas as atividades diárias.

No Espaço Ikaria, estamos comprometidos em fornecer um ambiente amoroso e enriquecedor, onde os idosos podem se sentir valorizados, apoiados e felizes. Junte-se a nós e descubra um lugar onde o envelhecimento é celebrado e cada dia é vivido com alegria.

Name: HomePlace Living Supports
Description:
HomePlace is an organisation built on the belief that every individual has the right to a secure home and active participation in the life of the community. Established in 1989, HomePlace was formed by a group of parents who were seeking to secure an independent future for their intellectually disabled adult children 

These parents wanted to feel confident and assured that their children, who they had cared for until adulthood, could move into their own home, and be well supported. Today, this sentiment is just as strong as it was at HomePlace’s inception, with the organisation offering personal and individualised disability support.

We work in partnership to provide support that is personalised to our participants’ wants and needs and moving them towards a more meaningful life.

HomePlace’s organisational objectives in accordance with our Constitution are:

- To establish, manage and maintain a range of accommodation support services for adults living with intellectual and cognitive disabilities.
- To support the rights of people with an intellectual or cognitive disability to have a quality of life that is comparable to those living without disability.  

As an organisation, HomePlace values being:

- A trusted companion
- Genuinely useful and helpful
- Respectful
- Resilient

Name: Réseau LCA 84
Description:
Suite à un traumatisme crânien, un accident vasculaire cérébral… les blessés et leurs familles se retrouvent isolés et démunis pour appréhender une situation complexe et inattendue qui provoque très souvent une grande rupture dans la vie des personnes et de leur entourage. 
Le Centre Ressource Départemental sur le Traumatisme Crânien et la lésion cérébrale animée par le SAVS SAMSAH de l’Etablissement Public Saint Antoine à l’Isle sur la Sorgue, l’Association des Familles de Traumatisés Crâniens de Vaucluse (AFTC 84), la Clinique Korian Les Cyprès (Montfavet-Avignon) et le cabinet juridique Victima Expert (Avignon) partagent le constat de la nécessité de créer du lien entre les différents acteurs de l’accompagnement des blessés et de leur famille. Aussi, ils associent leurs compétences pour animer une dynamique de réseau dans le Vaucluse et hors département entre les différents acteurs professionnels, institutionnels, associatifs pouvant proposer aux blessés et leur famille un accompagnement adapté dans les différents domaines de la vie des personnes.

Les objectifs de ce réseau :
-Favoriser la connaissance des séquelles et conséquences d’une cérébro-lésion acquise,
-Favoriser la prise en compte adaptée des besoins spécifiques des personnes cérébrolésées,
-Favoriser la diffusion d’informations sur les ressources et dispositifs institutionnels, libéraux et associatifs existants,
-Faciliter l’accès des personnes aux soins, aux prestations médico-sociales et aux droits, 
-Favoriser la continuité et la pluridisciplinarité du parcours d’accompagnement des blessés et de leur famille : aspects médicaux, administratifs, juridiques, sociaux, professionnels….
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Cap'école
Description:
CAP’école est une association loi 1901 qui a pour mission de promouvoir et renforcer l’école inclusive pour les enfants porteurs de handicap et/ou à besoins spécifiques.
CAP’école a été fondée en 2016 par des parents d’élèves en situation de handicap à la Seyne-sur-mer, et rayonne aujourd’hui dans tout le département du Var.
Nos actions sont menées selon des valeurs d‘égalité et de respect de la dignité de tous. Pour nous, l’école inclusive doit bénéficier à tous les enfants, avec ou sans handicap, par une mise en contact au quotidien. La nouvelle génération pourra ainsi mieux concevoir la présence de personnes porteuses d’un handicap ou à besoins spécifiques à leurs côtés, dans tous les milieux de vie.
En tant que membre de la FNASEPH, CAP’école s’engage pour le droit à l’éducation et à la scolarisation pour tous, quel que soit le handicap.

« La constitution de notre pays affirme le principe de l’égalité pour toutes les personnes. Aucune déficience, aucune maladie, aucun trouble particulier, aucun degré d’atteinte ne saurait justifier d’exception à cette règle. En conséquence, les enfants, adolescents (…) en situation de handicap ont droit à une vie sociale, une éducation, une scolarisation (..) conformes à leurs aspirations et à leurs besoins, dans le droit commun (…), avec des moyens adaptés. » Charte FNASEPH, Grandir et vivre en société.

Qu’est-ce que l’école inclusive ?

C’est accompagner et compenser le handicap pour que chaque enfant ait une scolarisation de qualité de la maternelle au lycée.

C’est considérer chaque enfant comme particulier.

C’est croire en la capacité de tous.

Name: Generations Aging Advisors
Description:
At Generations Aging Advisors, we advocate for seniors and guide families through the maze of aging.

Do you ever worry that something might happen to your elderly parent or loved one, and you wouldn’t know who to call?  We show up in moments like these and guide families to make good decisions.  

If you ever want to try it, the process is really simple.  First, we have a call to find out more about your elderly loved one, what concerns you have, and so on.  Second, if it feels like a good fit, we will recommend a membership level for you, and help get you set up so that we become an emergency contact for your loved one so you have peace of mind.  Third, in order to provide regular support, we can schedule a regular weekly or monthly meeting with you or your loved one to check in and go over anything that might have changed.

Without this advocacy, we have seen there simply are not enough health care workers to provide your loved one with the support they need.  Often families are forced to make quick and uninformed decisions.  Making a bad decision can be very costly financially, medically and emotionally for your family.

Our members have peace of mind that in case of an emergency their loved one will be in the hands of a compassionate and experienced expert. You can focus on being there for your loved one while we take care of the advocacy.

Check out our website and follow us on Facebook:
www.GenerationsAging.com
www.Facebook.com/GenerationsAging

 Mettre en œuvre toute action destinée à favoriser l'épanouissement des publics accompagnés, dont les personnes en situation de fragilité en raison de leur état de santé ou de leurs besoins en matière d’accompagnement social ou médico-social, et à assurer leur intégration dans les différents domaines de la vie.

Entretenir l'esprit d'entraide et de solidarité entre les intéressés.
Assurer dans la durée le suivi effectif des personnes accompagnées après la disparition de leur famille.
L’association Althéa a pour vocation d’accompagner l’ensemble des publics de l’économie sociale et solidaire, dont les personnes en situation de handicap dans la visée d’une société plus inclusive.

Name: Friends Day Centre
Description:
About Us

Friends Day Centre is a Non-Profit Organisation with humble beginnings. In 1959 Lorna Ingram started a care and learning facility for her son, David and 4 other children who had special needs. The original facility was a small building the size of a double garage.

From those simple beginnings, Friends Day Centre has grown into a care facility for up to 120 learners with severe and profound disabilities. Those receiving treatment are from ages as young as 2 years old and the facilities have expanded over the years to 10 classrooms, a physiotherapy room, a jacuzzi for hydrotherapy, outdoor swimming pool and a sensory room. More than 30 staff members attend to the needs of the learners and 3 buses provide transport for around 40 learners.  

Friends Day Centre, operates from 08h15 to 14h00, Mondays to Fridays during Western Cape school terms and  is situated on the grounds of Alexandra Hospital in Maitland, Cape Town. The Centre provides appropriate education, personal skills development and an array of therapies for learners. The Centre accommodates learners from different communities, suffering from various and / or multiple disabilities, including cerebral palsy, spina bifida and epilepsy, among others. The aim is to enhance their quality of life, encourage a degree of independence and give them the chance to live a relatively normal life. The Centre provides parents with the peace of mind that their special needs child is being cared for during the workday.

Name: Smart Disability Accommodation
Description:
At Smart Disability Accommodation, our vision is to provide exceptional accommodation services to NDIS participants, revolutionizing their lives through cutting-edge technology. With a firm commitment to the Specialist Disability Accommodation Design Standards, we strive to construct dwellings from the ground up, integrating life-changing features that make a real difference.

Our flagship project involves the construction of a state-of-the-art group home, featuring four participant bedrooms designed to cater to individuals requiring high physical support. However, our dedication to inclusivity means that our high physical support design encompasses all the elements of fully accessible homes, welcoming participants from various support needs.

Located at 15 Moa Street, Norlane VIC, our dwelling enjoys a prime location with convenient access to essential amenities. Situated within walking distance of the newly opened hospital at Barwon Health North (since 2020) and the upcoming Northern Aquatic and Community Hub (set to complete in early 2024), our participants will benefit from easy access to healthcare facilities and recreational activities.

Our commitment to leveraging smart home technology is at the heart of our design. Through voice and app-controlled features, including lighting, blinds, fully automated doors, and other devices, we aim to empower individuals with disabilities, transforming their daily lives. By harnessing the power of the Internet of Things devices, we ensure that these technologies are not just enjoyable but also serve as vital tools for individuals with hearing/speech impairments or limited mobility, allowing them to control their environment effortlessly.

Join us on our journey as we build our first Smart Disability Accommodation Dwelling, setting a new standard for inclusive living. Together, we will unlock endless possibilities and redefine accessibility for individuals with disabilities.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: National Council for Persons with Disabilities
Description:
The National Council for Persons with Disabilities (NCPD) in Fiji provides a range of services to support and empower people with disabilities. Some of these services include:

Advocacy: NCPD advocates for the rights of people with disabilities in Fiji and works to ensure that their voices are heard in policy and decision-making processes.

Accessibility: NCPD provides guidance and support to ensure that public spaces, buildings, and transport are accessible for people with disabilities.

Information and referral: NCPD provides information and referrals to people with disabilities to help them access services and support in their communities.

Training and capacity building: NCPD offers training and capacity-building programs for people with disabilities, their families, and service providers to increase their knowledge and skills.

Employment support: NCPD works to increase employment opportunities for people with disabilities and provides support to help them succeed in the workplace.

Assistive technology: NCPD helps people with disabilities access assistive technology, such as mobility aids, communication devices, and hearing aids, to support their independence and participation in daily life by connecting them with Mobility Service Providers in Fiji 

Community development: NCPD works with communities to promote the inclusion and participation of people with disabilities in community life.

Overall, the NCPD plays a critical role in promoting the rights, inclusion, and empowerment of people with disabilities in Fiji.

NCPD overseas 35 + affiliated disability organizations and continues to work with each one of them to better the lives of Persons with Disabilities in Fiji
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Association à but non lucratif (loi 1901) reconnue d’intérêt général. 
Elle a pour vocation d’accompagner les familles et de prendre en soin les personnes atteintes de troubles cognitifs et comportementaux.

Pour se faire, Nous accueillons toutes personnes souffrant de pathologies neurodégénératives, telles que la maladie de type Alzheimer, la maladie de Parkinson, la démence à corps de Lewy, les démences fronto-temporales, la maladie de Korsakoff. Mais aussi les démences vasculaires ou encore l’angiopathie amyloïde et toutes autres pathologies apparentées…

L’association œuvre pour permettre à la personne malade de rester le plus longtemps possible à son domicile. C’est pourquoi nous accompagnons autant le patient dans le maintien de son autonomie que la famille dans la compréhension de la maladie et dans la mise en place des aides humaines, techniques, administratives, sociales et financières.

La plateforme de répit « Les Pensées des Aidants » est un service d’accompagnement et de soutien pour les aidants familiaux.
Elle a pour but de soutenir l’ensemble des aidants familiaux qui accompagnent une personne âgée en perte d’autonomie quelle que soit sa maladie. Les objectifs sont de :
- Soutenir les proches aidants individuellement.
- Apporter de l’information pour aider à faire face à la prise en soin d’une personne souffrant de toutes pathologies entrainant une perte d’autonomie.
- Orienter vers les solutions de répits pour que l’aidant puisse bénéficier d’un temps libre réparateur.
Cela permet de prévenir les risques d’épuisement et de réduire la dureté d’accompagnement qui s’installe au quotidien.

- On-site Medical Center, staffed with physicians focused on the benefits of geriatric wellness. As well as an onsite Dentist's office.

- A comprehensive list of services!
- A variety of Residency options designed to suit one's individual situation.

The Village is a leading senior living destination for over 25 years.

Only Allegria Village combines a location convenient to all of Detroit and Dearborn's major cultural and entertainment venues.

Name: TASSK
Description:
TASSK is an Aboriginal owned business initiated by Taye Williams, an Anangu/Noongar man. This was a direct result of his own experiences as not only an Indigenous person looking for employment but also one having a disability. Taye was born with Cerebral Palsy but this has never stopped him from achieving his goals. Unfortunately when he began looking for work he encountered many stereotypes and hence TASSK was born.

TASSK aims to improve outcomes for all Indigenous people and give them the best job search skills and opportunities to enter the workforce and be successful. It also aims to make workplaces more culturally inclusive. Through knowledge better relationships and outcomes can result.

Bajo un enfoque dinámico, buscamos desarrollar intereses, valores , eficacia, roles, hábitos y habilidades, valorizando el “propio deseo” humano, el cual brinda la oportunidad de sentirse miembro único y un sentimiento de pertenencia, con participación activa e individualizada en el desarrollo del plan de trabajo y organización de la rutina.

La dinámica organizacional es el funcionamiento de una “casa” con sus propios valores, hábitos y normas, contando con espacios dinámicos y multifuncionales que brindan la posibilidad de crear diferentes alternativas de ambientes de trabajo, permitiendo la participación en diferentes roles, según lo requiera su plan de trabajo.

Not only can we help you find the perfect home for your loved one, we also act as your advocate and expert concierge before, during and after move-in.  Some say the best part is that our services are free!  We've done all the legwork when it comes to finding the best place that will cater to individual care needs, fits the budget, location and has the best reputation with qualified management and caregivers who provide the highest quality care for your loved one.  We can often negotiate better rates for you versus you trying to negotiate on your own.  We make every attempt to identify benefits you may qualify for such as the Veterans Aid and Attendance benefit or the Assisted Living Waiver program.  

As an advocate and industry expert, we work to ensure you never have an adversarial relationship between your loved one and those caring for him/her.  Let us help you navigate this process by giving your loved one the best outcome possible.

L'Arche la Merci fait partie de la Fédération de L’Arche en France, reconnue d’utilité publique. La Fédération Internationale des Communautés de L’Arche regroupe l’ensemble des 153 communautés de L’Arche dans le monde.

La mission de L'Arche est de :
> Faire connaître le don des personnes ayant une déficience intellectuelle qui se révèle à travers des relations mutuelles, sources de transformation.

> S'engager dans les cultures respectives et travailler ensemble à construire une société plus humaine.

Description:
There are only a handful of Aboriginal owned and managed NDIS service providers across Australia. As a result, for most Aboriginal people, the only way to acquire NDIS services is to seek non-Aboriginal service providers that may not be culturally appropriate. We aim to deliver quality, genuine, and transparent services to Aboriginal communities, and other people living with disabilities. 

Bookyana strives to ensure that we deliver culturally appropriate services to all participants under the Aboriginal NDIS, Department of Child Protection (DCP), and Exceptional Needs Unit (ENU).

Our strategy of staffing employs Aboriginal men and women in core positions, ensuring that our Aboriginal participants receive the greatest possible cultural standard.

Our people come from different walks of life and diverse backgrounds. We value an environment that is inclusive, safe, and respectful to all employees and participants.

Description:

Our founder has over 40 years experience providing power chair and mobility scooter solutions, while the rest of our team includes some of the most experienced, knowledgeable and understanding people in the industry.

We’re dedicated to helping you get mobile, so you can enjoy life to the fullest. A registered NDIS supplier, Power Mobility offers an extensive range of mobility products to assist those with limited or restricted movement, including:

Electric wheelchairs
Motorised mobility scooters
Lift chairs (electric recliners)
Walking aids and frames
Healthcare
Bathroom aids

Unlike many retailers of power chairs and other mobility equipment, we offer expert after-sale service and repairs, both onsite and via our convenient mobile service. Power Mobility provides a wide selection of MASS approved mobility aids and we are also a DVA Accredited Supplier.

Name: Artisan Mind
Description:
Artisan Mind is an organization formed out of the love of Christ and the deep desire to share His love through art and creativity. Whether our engagement is focused on art observation, painting, spiritual programs, or other life-enriching opportunities, all walks of faith are welcome. Many clients request art-based programs while others prefer faith-based support. In all accounts, our inspiration comes from the same place and our goal is to meet people right where they are, support their quality of life and engage them in meaningful ways.

OUR MISSION

We are committed to honoring the dignity of individuals living with physical and cognitive challenges by nurturing their strengths, hope, and joy through creative and spiritually engaging experiences.

OUR VISION

A world where faith, hope, and love flourish abundantly for people of all abilities.

WE SEEK TO:

 - Provide creative experiences that unlock imagination in a way that helps people living with physical or cognitive change stay connected or find connection to a sense of meaning, purpose, and value.
 - Enrich lives by reminding people living with memory loss of their inherent value and identity given by God.
 - Strengthen relationships, renew hope, and restore peace in a faith that is bigger than physical or cognitive challenges, or a dementia diagnosis.

Name: Oakley Home Access-New Haven County
Description:
Oakley Home Access is a leading Home Improvement team specializing in Fall Prevention, Mobility, Accessibility, and Aging-in-Place solutions. Our team includes Certified Aging-in-Place Specialists (CAPS), licensed Occupational Therapists and Physical Therapists, and experienced Home Remodeling experts — all dedicated to meeting you with “where you are” - helping individuals and families live safely, independently, and comfortably at home.

We proudly serve a wide range of community members, from children with special needs to older adults recovering from injury or seeking to maintain independence as they age. Whether adapting homes for temporary recovery or planning long-term accessibility, Oakley Home Access provides tailored solutions to meet each client’s unique needs.

Our most common home modifications include:
	•	Stair Lifts
	•	Wheelchair Ramps
	•	Grab Bar Installations
	•	Tub-to-Shower Conversions (Tub Cuts)
	•	Stairway Handrails
	•	Custom Entrance Railings
	•	Walk-In Showers
	•	Bathroom Accessibility Upgrades
	•	Adaptive Equipment for improved home mobility

At Oakley Home Access, we deliver trusted services and believe everyone deserves a home that supports their lifestyle, enhances safety, and promotes confidence every day.

Name: Stichting Samen tegen eenzaamheid
Description:
Stichting Samen tegen eenzaamheid is opgericht met de missie om een inclusieve gemeenschap te creëren, waarin eenzaamheid onder senioren wordt aangepakt. Geïnspireerd door de kracht van muziek hebben wij het radiostation Feel Good 60's 70's gelanceerd, dat elke dag de warme klanken van de jaren zestig en zeventig uitzendt. Wij geloven dat muziek het maatje is dat naast je op de bank zit en de kans biedt om mensen samen te brengen en hen te motiveren om de eerste stap te zetten naar sociale interactie.

Bij onze stichting richten we ons op drie belangrijke doelgroepen: luisteraars van ons radiostation, senioren van 55 jaar en ouder die zich eenzaam voelen, en toeleiders - de mensen die een vertrouwensband hebben met senioren zoals een werkgever, een buurtje of een verenigingsgenoot. Met een team van betrokken vrijwilligers bieden wij een luisterend oor en verwijzen we door naar ondersteuning bij hulpvragen en informatie over lokale evenementen en activiteiten die sociale verbinding stimuleren.

Onze aanpak is gebaseerd op samenwerking met maatschappelijke partners zoals welzijnsorganisaties. Door deze contacten kunnen we in onze programmering en podcasts aandacht besteden aan een breed scala aan ondersteuningsdiensten die mensen helpen hun eenzaamheid te doorbreken. Wij organiseren ook zelf evenementen, campagnes en initiatieven die uitnodigen tot ontmoeting en verbinding, zodat mensen ondersteuning vinden, nieuwe vriendschappen kunnen sluiten en sociale netwerken kunnen uitbreiden.

Bij Stichting Samen tegen eenzaamheid zetten we ons in voor een samenleving waar iedereen zich gehoord en verbonden voelt. Met ons motto "Samen in de klanken, samen uit de eenzaamheid" geloven we dat we door het delen van muziek en informatie samen de eenzaamheid kunnen verlichten en nieuwe perspectieven kunnen bieden.

Volg ons voor updates over onze activiteiten, evenementen en om te zien hoe wij samen een verschil maken in de levens van velen.

Name: V-Hygie
Description:
How to act before a fall?

        Thanks to touwi's advanced AI-Core technology, Docky, along with its Moovy, tracks and analyzes residents' movements to identify "get-up" events and instantly alerts caregivers to act before the risk of a fall!
	Simultaneously, within the resident’s room, Docky can activate its integrated nightlights and provide verbal reassurance that the care team has been alerted.

The V-Hygie system is a groundbreaking solution! Designed by skilled healthcare professionals in long-term care facilities, it uniquely alerts caregivers to potential nighttime falls without requiring infrastructure modifications or additional installations.

Vanwege onze sociaal maatschappelijk rol worden wij door gemeente Eindhoven financieel ondersteund. Verder zijn wij afhankelijk van de bijdragen van onze leden, sponsoren en donateurs.

Name: Our Cottage LLC
Description:
At Our Cottage, we believe that care should never feel clinical or impersonal. That’s why we’ve created a new standard in assisted living — one that blends compassionate support with the warmth and familiarity of home.

We operate boutique-style assisted living homes designed for seniors and adults who require additional support with daily living or memory care. Unlike large facilities, Our Cottage is intimate by design: each residence accommodates no more than 6–8 residents and maintains a 1:4 caregiver-to-resident ratio to ensure meaningful, attentive care at all times.

Our homes are thoughtfully remodeled for safety, beauty, and comfort — featuring private or semi-private rooms, beautifully landscaped yards, inviting common areas, and peaceful bedrooms. We provide 24/7 care, home-cooked meals, curated activities, medication management, and coordination with home health and hospice providers when needed. We also welcome pets and work closely with families to customize every detail to each resident’s unique needs.

We serve both seniors and younger adults living with early-onset Alzheimer’s, Parkinson’s, or other medical conditions that require long-term residential support. Many of our families come to us in moments of transition — following hospital discharge, a diagnosis, or when home care is no longer enough. We understand how emotional that process can be, and we walk alongside each family every step of the way.

Whether you’re exploring options for a loved one or yourself, Our Cottage offers something different — a model rooted in dignity, trust, and heart. Our homes are located in Plano, North Dallas, and surrounding communities, and we are actively expanding throughout DFW.

We invite you to learn more about what makes Our Cottage truly feel like home.

Description:
Dandelion House was started by Daniel and Natalie Hill. They shared a vision of a more person-centred approach to disability support, with a stronger focus on fostering relationships between those needing care and their families.

Our mission at Dandelion House is to enable our clients to work towards greater independence and live their best life. It is our goal to be a model provider of person-centred support and give our clients the ability to participate in social activities at whatever level they are comfortable with.

We are proud to provide our services in over 30 homes across Newcastle, the central coast and Dubbo, as well as organise engaging community activities for people with disabilities that enable them to have a life full of meaningful relationships.

Name: Apex Care Services
Description:
About Us
Apex Care is a leader in complex care housing solutions, providing safe, supportive, and empowering environments for individuals with high-care needs. Our approach is rooted in person-centered care, ensuring that participants receive tailored support that fosters autonomy, well-being, and long-term success.

Our Approach
Person-Centered Care: We collaborate with participants, families, and health professionals to develop customized care plans.
Comprehensive Care Plans: Tailored support for medical and daily living needs, including wound care and specialized health management.
Quality Housing & Tenancy Management: Secure, well-managed homes that enhance community integration.
Emergency & Maintenance Support: Rapid response for urgent repairs, ensuring safe and functional accommodations.
Skilled & Trained Staff: Ongoing training equips our team with the expertise needed for complex care.
Continuous Improvement: Participant feedback drives service enhancements.
Regulatory Compliance: We adhere to NDIS Quality and Safeguards standards, ensuring exceptional service.
Our Unique Selling Proposition
Apex Care specializes in Supported Independent Living (SIL) homes designed for complex care participants. With an in-house therapy and nursing team, we facilitate a smooth transition from institutionalized settings to independent living, fostering growth in a homely, nurturing environment with professional support readily available.

Types of Accommodation
Specialist Disability Accommodation (SDA): Purpose-built homes with tailored environments and on-site medical support.
Supported Independent Living (SIL): Housing that balances autonomy with essential life skills coaching and 24/7 support.
Medium Term Accommodation (MTA): Transitional housing offering structured programs to promote independence.
Short Term Accommodation (STA): Temporary housing providing respite and engaging therapy-led activities.
Emergency Accommodation: Rapid-response housing for urgent care needs
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Compassion Wings
Description:
Compassion Wings Pty Ltd

Compassion Wings is dedicated to delivering exceptional, client-centered care in Adelaide's North East, including Tea Tree Gully, Salisbury, Port Adelaide, and Playford. Our mission is to provide tailored support that enhances the quality of life and promotes independence for our clients.

Our Services Include:

Advanced Nursing Care: We offer specialized care for complex health needs, such as advanced wound care, stoma care, and tracheostomy management, ensuring the highest standards of clinical practice.

Assistance with Daily Life: Our support includes help with personal care, hygiene, and daily living tasks, aimed at maintaining client dignity and independence.

Personal Hygiene Support: We assist with grooming, bathing, and dressing, providing respectful and comfortable care.

Community Participation: We encourage and support clients to engage in social, recreational, and community activities, enhancing their inclusion and overall well-being.

Why Choose Us:

Under the leadership of Director Desha Karanjit, a nurse with 13 years of experience, Compassion Wings is distinguished by its commitment to high-quality, nurse-led care. Desha’s expertise drives our dedication to personalized care, ensuring our services meet the highest standards and effectively address the unique needs of each client.

Our Commitment:

Local and Consistent Support: Our support workers are strategically located within a 30-minute radius of clients, ensuring reliable, consistent care without additional travel charges.

Experienced Leadership: Our team benefits from Desha’s extensive nursing experience and leadership, which fosters a culture of excellence and continuous improvement.

Personalized Care Plans: We create customized care plans tailored to each client's specific needs, allowing for flexibility and responsiveness to changing circumstances.

At Compassion Wings, we are passionate about making a positive impact through compassionate & high-quality care.

Name: Adaptathon Bordeaux
Description:
L’Adaptathon Bordeaux vise à distinguer des projets ayant pour finalité l’inclusion dans l’emploi des personnes en situation de handicap. 

C’est un dispositif associatif, porté par des amis de Joël Solari, dont l'objectif est de permettre l’émergence de solutions innovantes face aux difficultés d’accès et de maintien dans l’emploi des personnes en situation de handicap : solutions, dispositifs, outils... susceptibles de répondre à des besoins, non satisfaits, des personnes en situation de handicap pour accéder ou se maintenir en emploi. 

Cette 4ème édition est aujourd'hui lancée avec un appel à projets dont le calendrier est le suivant :
- Date limite de candidature : 15 juillet 2024
- Sélection par le comité Adaptathon : été / rentrée 2024
- Remise des prix : 22 novembre 2024 à l'Hôtel Mercure Bordeaux Mériadeck  

Des prix seront attribués aux lauréats, avec une dotation financière.

Name: Dementia Care Solutions
Description:
My journey began in music therapy and has evolved across 25+ years in senior care—as a caregiver, care coordinator, administrator of a stand-alone dementia community, and memory care director. I help spouses and family members of people living with dementia feel empowered and confident in caring for their loved ones—without feeling overwhelmed or isolated—so they can support their loved one AND take care of themselves during one of the most difficult journeys a family can face.

Through my Alzheimer's Disease and Dementia Care Seminars, I provide the education and tools to gain certification (Certified Dementia Practitioner - CDP) that professionals and family caregivers need to deliver compassionate, informed care. I've walked alongside countless families through this journey, and I'm here to help you navigate it with knowledge, dignity, and hope.

Ready to deepen your dementia care expertise? Join my next Alzheimer's Disease & Dementia Care Seminar. Visit Dementia-care-solutions.com or message me directly to register.

My services include:
- 1:1 Consultation
- Alzheimer’s Disease & Dementia Care Seminar
- Training
- Support Groups
- Expert Legal Advice

My certifications Include:
-Alzheimer's Disease and Dementia Care Trainer
- Certified Dementia Practitioner
- Personal Care Home Administrator
- Assisted Living Administrator
- Older Adult Daily Living Administrator
- Board Certified Music Therapist

Name: Fondazione Historie Onlus
Description:
Fondazione Historie nasce nel 1978, quando un gruppo di giovani volontari decidono di rispondere alle richieste della cittadinanza dando un valido seguito agli strascichi della legge Basaglia che in quello stesso anno aveva decretato la chiusura degli ospedali psichiatrici in favore di un’integrazione sociale reale. Ad oggi gestisce, fra Villafranca di Verona e Valeggio sul Mincio, due comunità alloggio e cinque appartamenti protetti, tre centri diurni con finalità di laboratori artistici e un'azienda agricola che produce ortaggi e zafferano.

La Mission di Fondazione Historie è "Le abilità sono un valore", principio che si declina in positive relazioni sociali, un impegno che dia appagamento, un luogo dove trovare amici. 

La Vision è atta a riconoscere a minori, adulti e anziani con disagio, disabilità o fragilità - comunque determinata - e alle loro famiglie, il diritto a essere felici e a vivere una vita bella e di qualità. Con il metodo del “fare assieme”, Fondazione Historie si impegna per favorire la costante crescita delle persone che segue, con processi innovativi e operatori qualificati. Giorno dopo giorno, il lavoro viene ideato nei contenuti, nel metodo, nella valutazione e nel coinvolgimento, per creare socialità e cultura sulle diversità.

Fondazione Historie finanzia le sue attività con contributi pubblici e privati e con una costante raccolta fondi su progetti confidando nella generosità responsabile di molti cittadini. Tutte le raccolte hanno come destinazione: il sostegno e l'innovazione dei servizi offerti a persone e famiglie; l'acquisto di attrezzature e impianti sicuri e moderni; il miglioramento degli ambienti per garantire il conforto e il rispetto delle nome sulla sicurezza.

Nous accompagnons aussi bien les structures médico-sociales (IME, foyers de vie) que les familles et individus souhaitant explorer leur potentiel créatif dans un cadre bienveillant et accessible.

Ensemble, bâtissons un monde plus solidaire et créatif.

#Inclusion #ArtsEtHandicap #InnovationSociale #Solidarité #CréativitéPourTous

Name: Pro Auxilium
Description:
Chez Pro Auxilium, nous sommes engagés pour simplifier l’accès aux droits des personnes âgées en Suisse.
Spécialisés dans l’accompagnement administratif pour l’obtention de l’allocation pour impotent AVS, nous offrons une solution unique, rapide et humaine, aussi bien pour les particuliers que pour les professionnels du médico-social.


Notre plateforme web : une première en Suisse

Pro Auxilium propose la seule plateforme suisse dédiée à la simplification des démarches d’allocation pour impotent AVS (API).
Conçue pour être intuitive et accessible, elle permet à chacun (particulier, proche aidant ou professionnel) de remplir une demande d’API pas à pas, grâce à un système guidé, clair et sécurisé.
1/ Autonomie : complétez votre demande simplement, à votre rythme, sans connaissances administratives préalables.
2/ Gain de temps : la procédure est pensée pour réduire les délais de constitution des dossiers.
3/ Sécurité : vos données sont protégées et vos dossiers conformes aux exigences AVS.

Cette plateforme transforme une démarche complexe et mal connue en un processus fluide et accessible, pour tous



Notre Offre

1 Pour les particuliers :
Accompagnement personnalisé, à domicile ou par téléphone, pour constituer un dossier complet et optimisé en 48 à 72 heures.

2 Pour les établissements médico-sociaux (EMS, CMS, hôpitaux...) :
Simplifiez la constitution des dossiers d’API avec notre plateforme intuitive. Rapide, guidé et sans complexité.

3 Pour les associations, fondations et partenaires du secteur social :
Partenariat sur mesure pour sécuriser et accélérer les démarches de vos bénéficiaires.



Pourquoi choisir Pro Auxilium ?

 Expertise gériatrique unique en Suisse dans la gestion des demandes d’allocation pour impotent AVS.
 Service 100% transparent, avec un accompagnement guidé et des tarifs clairs.




 Visitez : www.proauxilium.ch
 Appelez-nous : 021 219 77 01

Name: ComfyGO Mobility Schweiz
Description:
WER WIR SIND?
ComfyGO Mobility ist ein führender Hersteller hochwertiger, benutzerfreundlicher Mobilitätsprodukte. Wir entwickeln nachhaltige Produkte, die mit der neuesten Technologie ausgestattet sind und die Bedürfnisse und Erwartungen unserer wertvollen Kunden voll und ganz berücksichtigen. Wir stehen stolz hinter jedem Produkt, das wir verkaufen, und verpflichten uns, allen unseren Kunden einen erstklassigen Kundenservice zu bieten.

Unsere Aufgabe
Unsere Mission ist es, Lösungen zu schaffen, die das Leben der Menschen einfacher und angenehmer machen. Wir bieten erstklassige Mobilitätsprodukte, die es unseren Kunden ermöglichen, ein besseres und einfacheres Leben zu führen und aktive Mitglieder der Gesellschaft zu werden.

Unsere Vision
unsere Vision; Ziel ist es, eines der führenden Unternehmen zu sein, das die innovativsten, qualitativ hochwertigsten, leichtesten, faltbarsten, langlebigsten und preisgünstigsten Mobilitätsprodukte auf dem Mobilitätsmarkt anbietet.

Wir bieten Franchises in der ganzen Schweiz an. Kontaktieren Sie uns, um die Vorteile der amerikanischen ComfyGo- und britischen QuinGo-Unternehmen zu nutzen.

Name: APRAIH
Description:
L’Association APRAIH (Association Pour Recherche Animation Insertion Handicap) a été fondée en 1981 au sein du service de MPR du CHU de Nantes.

Elle a historiquement pour vocation : 

- De financer du matériel d’adaptation et de recherche autour du handicap (notamment auprès des patients du CHU de Nantes). 

- De proposer des activités sportives, culturelles et de loisirs aux patients hospitalisés. 

- De favoriser la réadaptation, et la réinsertion sociale des personnes en situation de handicap.

La force de cette Association, est d’être constituée de professionnels du handicap, ainsi que de personnes en situation de handicap (hospitalisées ou anciennement hospitalisées), dans le but de remplir conjointement les missions de l’Association.

L’association possède un véhicule (minibus) adapté, qu’elle met à disposition du personnel d’animation, pour tout déplacement de loisirs, ou administratif (préfecture, lieu de scolarisation en vue d’une re-scolarisation), afin de faciliter les déplacements des personnes à mobilité réduite. 

Elle met également à disposition un appartement dit adapté (en sous location), pour faire le relais entre l’hospitalisation et le retour à domicile, dans un contexte parfois difficile de recherche de logement adapté.

Notre Association est œuvre pour la réadaptation et l’intégration des personnes en situation de handicap en étant présente dès le début d’hospitalisation auprès d’eux, et en les accompagnant jusqu’à leur sortie d’hospitalisation et au delà.

Name: Silver Caregivers Co-operative
Description:
Silver Caregivers Co-operative Limited (SCCL) is a social enterprise that was set up in July 2013. It envisions to better the quality of life of caregivers through Workshops, Courses, Talks, as well as to become a one-stop centre of information to better support their important roles as caregivers to their loved ones. SCCL is currently the leading Psycho-Social Course Provider for caregivers in Singapore. SCCL is registered under the Singapore National Co-operative Societies Act.

Research has found that caregivers tend to neglect their own health care appointments and eat poor-quality diet among caregivers who provide assistance with basic activities of daily living (ADLs). This has bearing on their health status in the long term.

Caregivers are also at high risk of fatigue and may lose their motivation to care for their loved ones due to the demands of their caregiving roles.

SCCL champions the cause of caregivers, especially the sandwiched group (refers to caregivers who are not within the bottom 5% of the economic ladder), by empowering them with relevant holistic skill sets.

Through collaboration with various organizations, it hopes to provide a series of workshops, seminars and public forums to support and prepare them socially and mentally to be effective caregivers.

Name: AMAT 47
Description:
L'AMAT est un acteur engagé dans l'inclusion sociale et professionnelle des personnes en situation de handicap. 



Notre mission est de favoriser l'autonomie et l'intégration des individus grâce à des services adaptés et un accompagnement personnalisé. 


À l'AMAT 47, nous croyons fermement en la dignité, en l'égalité et en la valorisation des talents. Nos valeurs fondamentales guident chacune de nos actions :


1. Inclusion : Nous nous engageons à créer un environnement où chaque individu, quelles que soient ses différences, trouve sa place.

   

2. Solidarité : La force de notre association réside dans notre capacité à travailler ensemble, en soutien mutuel, pour le bien-être de tous.


3. Autonomie : Nous mettons tout en œuvre pour favoriser l'autonomie des personnes que nous accompagnons, en leur offrant les outils nécessaires pour s'épanouir.


4. Respect : Chaque personne est unique. Nous respectons les choix, les aspirations et les besoins de chacun, en adoptant une approche centrée sur l'individu.


Nos Services


Nous proposons une gamme variée de services pour accompagner les personnes en situation de handicap :


- Établissement et Service d'Aide par le Travail (ESAT) : Nos 2 ESAT offrent des activités professionnelles adaptées, tout en favorisant l'épanouissement personnel et social des travailleurs.


- Service d'Accompagnement à la Vie Sociale (SAVS) : Ce service aide les personnes à développer leur autonomie dans la vie quotidienne, en les soutenant dans leurs démarches administratives, sociales et professionnelles.



Notre Équipe


Notre équipe est composée de professionnels passionnés, engagés et formés pour répondre aux besoins spécifiques des personnes en situation de handicap. Psychologues, éducateurs spécialisés, travailleurs sociaux, etc collaborent pour offrir un accompagnement de qualité, basé sur l'écoute et le respect.

Name: LAPLA'J Formation
Description:
Pour rendre les parcours de santé accessibles aux personnes en situation de handicap, on travaille sur plusieurs fronts avec l'association LAPLA'JH. Voici comment on aborde les défis :

- Faciliter l'intégration professionnelle : On assiste les entreprises pour qu'elles puissent comprendre et répondre aux besoins spécifiques des personnes handicapées, en adaptant les postes de travail à leur profil.

- Construire des ponts entre les secteurs : On crée des liens entre le monde professionnel, les institutions et les personnes en situation de handicap pour élaborer des projets adaptés et pertinents qui répondent aux défis de la vie quotidienne de ces individus.

Pour réussir ces missions, on doit constamment développer des compétences essentielles telles que :

- Leadership et gestion d'équipe : Diriger et motiver une équipe engagée pour l'accessibilité et l'inclusion.
- Compétences en communication : Communiquer efficacement avec divers groupes d'intérêt, y compris les bénéficiaires, les entreprises, et les institutions.
- Expertise en adaptation de l'environnement de travail : Conseiller les entreprises sur les meilleures pratiques pour adapter les postes de travail.
- Sensibilisation à la diversité et à l'inclusion : Comprendre profondément les besoins des personnes handicapées et promouvoir l'inclusion à tous les niveaux.
- Capacités de négociation et de médiation : Négocier et médier entre -différents acteurs pour assurer des solutions bénéfiques pour toutes les parties.
- Innovation et créativité : Développer des approches novatrices pour surmonter les barrières à l'inclusion.

Ces compétences nous permettent non seulement de mener des initiatives significatives mais aussi d'inspirer et de faciliter des changements positifs pour une meilleure intégration des personnes à capacités variées dans la société.

Name: West Coast Positive Behaviour Support & Allied Therapy
Description:
At West Coast PBS and Allied Therapy, we are dedicated to enhancing the quality of life for individuals living with disabilities or mental health, as well as the families and support networks surrounding them. Our commitment lies in the delivery of personalised, trauma-informed care that fosters inclusion and access to essential services.



Our Services

West Coast PBS and Allied Therapy operate under the NDIS registration groups 0128 and 0110 and offer bespoke services in Perth to Mandurah, and the South West. Our registered early practicing, core, proficient and advanced behaviour support practitioners conduct initial assessments, identify and report on restrictive practices, attend quality assurance panels upon request, and create safety response and interim behavior support plans, functional behaviour assessments and comprehensive behaviour support plans, among a wide range of other services tailored to participant needs. Our allied therapy team focuses on improving mobility and facilitating participation within the home and community, with services available in the Perth Metro area, Busselton, and Dunsborough.



Our Philosophy

We believe in a human rights approach, committed to reducing and eliminating practices that restrict the rights and freedoms of individuals with disabilities. Through collaboration with NDIS service providers, we strive to offer individualised services grounded in evidence-based and strengths-focused practices. Our mission is to empower individuals, enhancing their independence and well-being, while fostering a supportive environment for all. 

Our aim in becoming a registered NDIS provider is to deliver ongoing support that is reliable and offer continuity to our participants, establishing ourselves as a trusted local resource.

Why do we do what we do? Simply put, to improve lives and create an inclusive society where everyone can thrive.

Name: Disability Support Services
Description:
Welcome to Disability Support Services, where we are dedicated to transforming the professional landscape for individuals with disabilities. We believe that everyone deserves equal opportunities to thrive in their careers, and we are committed to providing comprehensive support and resources to empower individuals with disabilities to reach their full potential.

At Disability Support Services, we understand the unique challenges faced by individuals with disabilities in the workplace. Our team of experts is passionate about fostering an inclusive and accessible environment, breaking down barriers, and promoting diversity and inclusion across industries.

Through our wide range of services, we offer personalized assistance to individuals with disabilities, helping them navigate the job market, develop essential skills, and connect with employers who embrace diversity. Our dedicated team provides guidance on resume building, interview preparation, career counseling, and ongoing professional development.

We also collaborate closely with employers, promoting disability awareness and assisting them in creating inclusive workspaces. Our consulting services help organizations implement best practices, reasonable accommodations, and policies that foster an inclusive culture, ensuring that individuals with disabilities can thrive and contribute their unique talents.

Join us in our mission to create a world where disability is not a barrier to success. Together, we can build a more inclusive and diverse workforce, where every individual has the opportunity to excel and make a meaningful impact. Name: Play Positive
Description:
A small yet diverse team with a focus on empowering players to find their tribe, Play Positive was founded in 2019 in response to an ad on a Facebook group seeking a GM for a young man with autism. 

This led our founder Dylan, who had already been studying the positive impacts of D&D in therapy, connecting with the family and running our first ever group.

From there, Play Positive grew and continues to seek innovative strategies to support neurodivergent individuals and their families to live their best, most fulfilling lives. 

We would not be who we are without our trauma-informed approach, which is at the core of our therapeutic sessions. This approach embodies hope and optimism that recovery is possible whilst ensuring our work does not creature further harm. Key components of our sessions include safety, trust, clear boundaries, player empowerment and ongoing choice.
With most of our group sessions online, they are accessible Australia wide using Discord.

Name: ACCOMATE
Description:
Accomate is a disability accommodation platform linking providers with users and stakeholders. Navigating the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) to find suitable accommodation can be daunting. www.Accomate.com.au is a dedicated platform that simplifies this process by connecting NDIS participants with the right accommodation providers. This blurb explores the services offered by Accomate.com.au, the benefits for both participants and providers and how it addresses challenges in the NDIS accommodation space. Accomate.com.auspecialises in listing and connecting NDIS participants with Specialist Disability Accommodation (SDA), Supported Independent Living (SIL), and respite care providers across Australia. The platform streamlines the search process, making it easier for participants and their families to find suitable housing that meets their unique needs and preferences.

Benefits for NDIS Participants
Simplified Search Process: Comprehensive listings and advanced search options.
Access to Quality Providers: Detailed information and user reviews ensure high-quality providers.
Personalised Support: Understanding eligibility criteria.
Convenience and Accessibility: photos and map integration for easy exploration.

Benefits for Providers
Increased Visibility: Listing services on a dedicated platform.
Targeted Marketing: Connect with NDIS participants and stakeholders.
Streamlined Communication: Easier coordination of services and addressing questions.
Business Growth: Connect with the right participants and stakeholders.

The Benefit of Using Accomate.com.au

Accomate.com.au is a valuable resource for both NDIS participants and providers. The platform simplifies the search process by offering comprehensive listings, detailed information, and advanced search options and helps participants find suitable accommodations. For providers, Accomate.com.au offers increased visibility, targeted marketing, and opportunities for business growth.

We are a community choir for people living with dementia and related memory loss, alongside their care partners. Through shared musical experiences, we foster joy, purpose, and meaningful connection while reducing isolation and challenging the stigma often associated with Alzheimer’s and dementia.

Our sessions are designed to be engaging, inclusive, and uplifting, culminating in a celebratory concert that highlights not just performance, but community, confidence, and shared accomplishment.

Second Verse Choir exists to remind us that while memory may change, the ability to connect through music does not.

Name: Changing Spaces Massachusetts
Description:
Many individuals living with disabilities and medical conditions do not have access to a safe, private, and dignified place to utilize the restroom in public. Baby changing tables are too small for older children and adults, but universally designed changing tables accommodate everyone. Volunteers across the U.S. joined together to create change and make our communities more inclusive and accessible for all.

Our Goals:

Spread Awareness within our communities about the lack of appropriate changing facilities for older children and adults with the medical need to use incontinence supplies

Advocate for height-adjustable adult size changing tables in public restroom facilities 

Connect with sports arenas, large assembly spaces, large stores, parks, etc., to encourage them to install universal changing tables 

Educate and train new advocates 

Introduce Legislation in our cities and states in collaboration with local and state government leaders

 Conditionnement & logistique – Préparation de commandes, mise sous pli, routage

 Blanchisserie & entretien des locaux – Nettoyage professionnel et respect des normes d’hygiène
 Restauration & autres services – Adaptation à des projets spécifiques

Notre engagement : offrir un travail de qualité optimale, tout en garantissant un impact social fort à nos partenaires. Nos équipes sont encadrées par des professionnels qualifiés, assurant fiabilité, rigueur et performance.

Pourquoi travailler avec nous ?
 Un engagement sociétal fort : en collaborant avec l’APFA, vous participez activement à l’inclusion des personnes en situation de handicap.
 Une productivité prouvée : nos travailleurs offrent des résultats comparables au milieu ordinaire, selon les retours de nos clients.
 Un accompagnement continu : nous assurons un suivi et un encadrement personnalisé pour garantir le bon déroulement des missions.
 Des solutions adaptées : nous nous ajustons aux besoins spécifiques de chaque entreprise.

Faites de votre engagement une valeur ajoutée pour votre entreprise. Rejoignez-nous dans cette démarche responsable et performante !

Name: Aroha Health care
Description:
Aroha Health Care is a dedicated and emerging disability support provider focused on bridging gaps in current services across New Zealand. Our experienced leadership team is passionate about delivering high-quality, individualized support to tāngata whaikaha (people with disabilities), including those with intellectual disabilities, autism, and physical disabilities. Rooted in the Waikato region, we are growing our operations with a strong commitment to expanding access to tailored, one-to-one supports that promote community inclusion, independence, and wellbeing across Aotearoa.

Guided by the principles of manaakitanga (care), whanaungatanga (connection), and kotahitanga (unity), we work closely with whānau and communities to empower individuals to live lives of dignity, choice, and full participation. With a flexible, culturally responsive, and holistic approach, we provide 24/7 support options designed to strengthen abilities and nurture independence. Our vision is a society where tāngata whaikaha(people with disabilities) have equal rights, real choices, and the opportunity to thrive — because at Aroha Health Care, we believe everyone deserves to live the life they choose. Whakakaha i ngā Pūmanawa — Strengthening Abilities Together.

Als kleines, aber hochmotiviertes Unternehmen haben wir uns auf Treppenlifte und Lifttechnik spezialisiert und verfügen über langjährige Erfahrung in diesem Bereich. Wir wissen genau, wie wir Sie mit hochwertigen und dennoch erschwinglichen Produkten begeistern können. All das bieten wir Ihnen in Verbindung mit exzellentem Kundenservice und freundlicher Unterstützung.

Nicht nur unsere Preise sind unschlagbar, sondern auch die Qualität unserer Produkte ist herausragend. Wir haben es uns zum Ziel gemacht, unseren Kunden Mobilität zu bieten, die sie glücklich macht, und das zu attraktiven Preisen.

Seit unserer Gründung im Jahr 1995 haben wir hart daran gearbeitet, langfristige und vertrauensvolle Beziehungen zu unseren Kunden aufzubauen. Unsere Kunden stehen für uns immer an erster Stelle und wir setzen alles daran, ihnen bestmöglichen Service und Support zu bieten.

Name: Community Carers Nsw Pty Ltd
Description:
At Community Carers NSW, we believe that anything is possible when individuals are empowered with compassionate support, choice, and respect. Established in 2018 and led by founder Annette Hanneman, who brings over 28 years of experience in the disability sector, we have built a reputation for delivering high-quality, personalised care for individuals and families across Greater Sydney, including the Sutherland Shire, St George, and Western Sydney regions.

As a registered NDIS provider, we offer a comprehensive range of services designed to support the diverse needs and goals of our participants. From personal care, community access, and domestic assistance to specialised community nursing and behavioural support, our team is dedicated to fostering independence, comfort, and a sense of belonging.

Our unique approach prioritises:
Participant Empowerment: Helping individuals achieve their goals and feel confident in their daily lives.

Experienced, Compassionate Team: Our skilled team includes registered nurses and highly trained support workers who understand the nuances of complex needs.

Community Connections: We believe in the power of community partnerships, working closely with support coordinators and local services to create a strong network of care.

Holistic Support for Families: From temporary respite care at our scenic Kurnell facility to emergency accommodation upon hospital discharge, we support both participants and their families through life’s transitions.

Our Mission: To create a safe, inclusive environment where everyone has the support they need to live a full, independent, and meaningful life.

Our Vision: To be a trusted leader in disability support, empowering lives through high-quality care, strong community ties, and dedicated partnerships.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Groupe Ravel
Description:
Le Groupe Ravel accompagne les personnes âgées et en situation de handicap dans l’adaptation de leur logement pour retrouver confort et autonomie. Nous proposons une solution clé en main, de l’étude des besoins à la réalisation des travaux, en passant par la recherche et l’obtention des meilleures aides financières, notamment MaPrimeAdapt’, qui peut couvrir jusqu’à 70 % des coûts.

Notre mission est simple : permettre à chacun de vieillir chez soi en toute sécurité, sans contrainte financière excessive. Trop peu de personnes savent qu’elles sont éligibles à des subventions pour financer leurs aménagements. C’est pourquoi nous prenons en charge l’intégralité des démarches administratives, en collaboration avec les mairies, associations et institutions, pour garantir un accès simplifié aux aides disponibles.

Nos engagements :
 Évaluation personnalisée : une analyse complète pour proposer des solutions adaptées aux besoins spécifiques de chacun.
 Accompagnement financier : recherche et optimisation des aides pour réduire au maximum le reste à charge.
 Travaux sur-mesure : mise en œuvre d’aménagements sécurisés et adaptés (douches à l’italienne, monte-escaliers, rampes d’accès…).
 Suivi après installation : une assistance continue pour garantir la qualité et la durabilité des aménagements réalisés.

Parce que l’accessibilité ne doit pas être un privilège, le Groupe Ravel agit pour offrir à tous un cadre de vie adapté et sécurisé. Ensemble, construisons un monde plus inclusif.

Our motto, “People, Community, and Culture”, reflects our commitment to uniting diverse communities by breaking linguistic, religious and cultural barriers.

We specialise in supporting culturally and linguistically diverse (CALD) participants, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities and Muslim individuals, particularly those transitioning from incarceration or navigating the justice system. Our services are tailored to empower participants to live independently and achieve their personal goals.

Why Choose Zyma Care
 Culturally Inclusive – Respecting diverse backgrounds and traditions
 Holistic Approach – Addressing mental, social and emotional wellbeing
 Qualified Team – Compassionate and professional support workers
 Community Focused – Creating strong inclusive networks of care

At Zyma Care, we do not just provide support. We empower lives and strengthen communities. Join us in making a difference

Name: MORE for Moo
Description:
Our purpose
The primary purpose of our association is to empower individuals with disabilities by providing meaningful employment opportunities, fostering an inclusive workplace environment, and promoting social and economic integration.

Our objectives

Employment Opportunities: To create and sustain a variety of job opportunities tailored to the skills and abilities of individuals with disabilities, ensuring fair wages and inclusive work conditions.


Workplace Inclusion: To cultivate an inclusive workplace culture that values diversity, provides necessary accommodations, and promotes the professional development of employees with disabilities.


Skills Development: To offer training programs and skill development workshops that enhance the employability and career advancement of individuals with disabilities.


Advocacy and Awareness: To advocate for the rights and capabilities of individuals with disabilities in the broader community and to raise awareness about the benefits of an inclusive workforce.


Support Services: To provide support services such as job coaching, mentoring, and counseling to help employees with disabilities achieve and maintain employment.


Partnerships and Collaboration: To collaborate with businesses, government agencies, and other organizations to create more opportunities and remove barriers for the employment of individuals with disabilities.


Research and Innovation: To engage in research to identify best practices and innovative approaches in employing individuals with disabilities and to share these findings with the broader community.


Community Engagement: To foster a sense of community and belonging among employees with disabilities through social activities, networking events, and peer support groups.

Synerkare | The Unincorporated Collective Exposing the Truth Behind Elder Abuse

We are not a corporation. We are a reckoning.

Synerkare is a disruptive, people-powered collective built by families, former caregivers, whistleblowers, and truth-tellers who are done staying silent. We exist to expose the systemic abuse, preventable neglect, and silent crimes being committed against aging Americans—right now, every single day.

This is not just advocacy. This is war against the silence that allows predators, broken systems, and unchecked institutions to keep operating behind closed doors.

We are igniting investigations, publishing hard truths, and building a trusted platform where families, professionals, and businesses can align for one urgent cause: protecting the lives and dignity of our seniors.

We are Synerkare.

Follow us. Join us. Or help us expose the rest.

Dagelijks bieden wij professionele en persoonlijke dagbesteding aan volwassenen met een zorgvraag: mensen met niet-aangeboren hersenletsel, mensen met een lichamelijke beperking, mensen met een vorm van dementie, ouderen.

Onze locatie aan de rand van Hapert biedt volop ruimte: twee zorgboerderijen, een kinderdagverblijf, belevingstuin, echte boerderij en een natuurgebied met rolstoelvriendelijke paden.

In deze groene, rustgevende omgeving kunnen onze gasten ondanks hun beperkingen genieten van het leven en van elkaar. Goede dagbesteding helpt hen langer thuis te wonen en biedt verlichting voor mantelzorgers.

Description:

Name: Made To Stay
Description:
Our mission is to provide a non-profit membership organization for senior members of McCracken County to enable them to remain in their own homes by providing services which they may not be able to provide for themselves due to age or health. The senior will be required to purchase a membership which will give them access to the services of the organization. The organization will be made up of senior Members, Volunteers, and staff. Services depend on Volunteer availability and include such things as: transportation, small home repairs,
yard work, social events, friendly calls and visits to check on well-being, and making referrals to other service providers as needed.

Made to Stay, a membership organization, is now in its 7th year of serving seniors 55+ and disabled adults in the Paducah/McCracken County area.  We are giving seniors in our area the opportunity to remain in their own homes and enjoy a better quality of life.  It is thrilling to be a part of such a worthy endeavor.

Made to Stay has served over 44 members weekly in our 6 years of service.  We have volunteers and staff who have filled more than 3,500 requests for services, spent more than 5,500hours, and driven over 46,000 miles serving them.

Volunteers are vital to our organization.  Without them, Made to Stay, would be unable to serve its members.  Our volunteers would all agree that serving our members is a very rewarding experience.  We invite you to consider joining us in this adventure.  Please visit our Volunteers Page on this website.

 Notre mission ?

Name: Affirming Futures
Description:
Built on passion and integrity

AFFIRMING FUTURES is a registered NDIS provider,
with services to support people with disabilities aged 17-65. Our priority is you.


Our Team

The founding members of AFFIRMING FUTURES have extensive knowledge and experience in working with people with disabilities and mental health conditions.

AFFIRMING FUTURES provides innovative solutions to address the barriers and difficulties that people with disabilities and their families experience, improving their quality of life through a wide range of services including in-home support, mentoring programs, short term accommodations, Supported Independent Living, support coordination and positive behaviour support.

AFFIRMING FUTURES puts relationships first to support those providing care, to protect the dignity of those receiving it, and to enhance the quality of life for both participants and their families.

AFFIRMING FUTURES provides professional, reliable, flexible and creative services and solutions to instill hope and shape futures:


Inclusion

We strive to provide an environment that supports people with a disability to have a fair go, with equal opportunity for engagement and social inclusion in the community.
Understanding

We work with the individual to understand their specific needs and goals, creating tailored supports based on when and how the individual would like them to be delivered.

Support

We offer a range of support options for individuals, giving more choice and control to make informed decisions about who they would like to work with, how they want to be supported, and where they want to be supported.

Encouragement

We encourage and support individuals to participate in activities and programs in line with their aspirations and goals to promote positive change in their life.

Name: Fyve Health
Description:
Fyve Health was born from Fyve Labs, our incubator dedicated to creating transformative product companies at the intersection of technology and human potential.

Our first venture, Fyve Health, is pioneering tools that redefine how individuals and families manage well-being. At the core of this journey is our flagship product: Tessa.

Tessa is an AI health companion designed to support aging populations and their families.
- What it does: Listens to and monitors the daily patterns of aging loved ones.
- Why it matters: Provides families with actionable insights that enable better, ongoing care decisions.
- Impact: Tessa helps close the gap between healthspan and lifespan, ensuring independence, dignity, and proactive care.

Name: Bocho
Description: Bocho is a cutting-edge assistive technology device that utilizes head tracking to enable people with motor impairments to interact with digital devices. It is designed to provide a more intuitive, efficient, and natural way of using computers, tablets, smartphones, and other devices without the need for traditional input devices such as a keyboard or mouse. With Bocho, users can control the cursor, type, browse the internet, play games, and perform other tasks entirely through head movements. Bocho has the potential to improve the lives of millions of people worldwide, allowing them to access the digital world and participate more fully in society.

Name: Team & Co - Mon Coach Adapté
Description:
TEAM & Co, Mon Coach Adapté trouve sa source dans une double lecture. Le terme TEAM, anglicisme signifiant « Equipe », et surtout dans l’abréviation de « Together Everyone Achieves More » qui pourrait se traduire par « l’union fait la force ».

TEAM & Co, Mon Coach Adapté est une société à vocation commerciale qui œuvre dans un secteur innovant pour mettre en relation des sportifs nouvellement formés et des personnes en situation de handicap désireuse de pratiquer une activité sportive.

TEAM & Co, Mon Coach Adapté souhaite opérer un changement de regard sur l’inclusion, le sport et le handicap.

TEAM & Co, Mon Coach Adapté a pour ambition de permettre à tout son écosystème, d’apprendre de soi, d’apprendre aux autres et d’apprendre des autres.

TEAM & Co, Mon Coach Adapté porte un plaidoyer public en faveur des bienfaits de la pratique d’activités physiques et sportives pour tous et anime le réseau des entités intéressées à ce sujet.  Son action doit concourir à bâtir un monde où les personnes en situation de vulnérabilité ont accès à toutes les activités sportives, de bien-être et de détente, pensées pour les personnes valides ; un monde où le sport s’adapte aux personnes qui le pratiquent sans distinction ni discrimination.

Notre société propose : 
- des prestations de consultant, de conseil et de service, en faveur de l’accompagnement sportifs adapté de toutes personnes en situation de vulnérabilité;
- des prestations de consultant, de conseil et de service auprès de toutes entreprises quel que soit leur domaine d'action, des particuliers et de tout organisme public, privé, médico-social, ou social quelle qu'en soit la structure juridique, en faveur d’une mise en relation entre des coachs formés et des personnes en situation de vulnérabilité.

Team & Co Mon Coach Adapté est donc une plateforme de mise en relation permettant le décloisonnement de différents mondes pour une émulation mutuelle.

Name: L'Arca Società Cooperativa Sociale
Description:
La storia de L’Arca Società Cooperativa Sociale affonda le radici nell’associazione Genitori di Ragazzi Disabili del Seprio, fondata a Tradate nel 1989 dal dottor Renzo Imperiali. Da qui, nel 2007, nasce la cooperativa sociale di tipo A, impegnata nella gestione di servizi socio-assistenziali ed educativi per i disabili del territorio.
La nostra offerta formativa diurna si divide tra un Centro Socio Educativo, dedicato a bambini e ragazzi, e un Centro Diurno Disabili, pensato per giovani adulti. Siamo attivi, inoltre, nel campo dell’assistenza scolastica e domiciliare, delle prestazioni riabilitative individuali (e degli interventi specialistici per minori con autismo. Ogni anno ci occupiamo di più di cento ragazzi provenienti da circa 35 Comuni nelle province di Varese e Como.

Name: Kwebbi
Description:
Eenzaamheid onder ouderen is een groeiend probleem. Technologie kan helpen, maar voelt voor veel mensen nog onpersoonlijk of ingewikkeld. Daarom introduceerden wij KWEBBI: een zachte, vriendelijke, interactieve papegaai die ouderen op een natuurlijke manier gezelschap biedt.

KWEBBI combineert kunstmatige intelligentie, spraakherkenning en empathische communicatie om dagelijks warmte en aandacht te geven. Geen ingewikkelde instellingen, geen technische drempels alleen een slim, zacht en gezellig maatje in huis.

Met KWEBBI kunnen ouderen eenvoudig praten over dagelijkse vragen, maar ook een persoonlijk praatje maken. KWEBBI toont uit zichzelf interesse, stelt lieve vragen en herkent wanneer iemand even stil is. Daarnaast biedt hij herinneringen, rustige geluiden en een gevoel van aanwezigheid precies wat veel ouderen missen.

Ons doel is helder: ouderen elke dag een gevoel van gezelschap, betekenis en gezelligheid bieden.
KWEBBI is meer dan technologie. Het is een warm maatje met een groot hart.

Name: First Choice Care Solutions
Description:
First Care Choice Solutions | Responsive Nursing & Disability Support

At First Care Choice Solutions, we understand that life can change in an instant. When it does, having the right support—delivered with care, compassion, and urgency—can make all the difference. That’s why we’re here, day or night, to respond when you need us most.

Our team of experienced nurses and dedicated support workers is committed to providing timely, person-centred care without the usual delays or roadblocks. From everyday assistance to complex clinical support, we make it easier to live well at home with the right help by your side.

Care That Feels Personal, Reliable, and Just Right  
 A real person answers your call—even at 3 AM  
 Access to specialist nursing support at support worker rates  
 Fast response for urgent needs, thoughtful planning for long-term care  
 Hospital-at-home options when staying in is the better choice  
 Less paperwork, no call centres, and no repeating your story  
 Consistent staff who understand your preferences and build real rapport  
 Support workers matched to your personality and care goals  

Our services include:  
- High-Intensity Support  
- Spinal Cord Injury Support (TAC clients included)  
- Neurological & Mental Health Care  
- Aged & Palliative Support  
- Diabetes & Medication Management  
- Catheter, Bowel & Wound Care  

Families find peace of mind knowing their loved ones are looked after with kindness and respect. Health professionals appreciate our attention to detail and clinical reliability. Support coordinators trust us to follow through—no chasing, no delays.

At First Care Choice Solutions, you're more than a case file. You're a person with a story, goals, and strengths.

Name: Marakuda
Description:
Marakuda is a South Australian NDIS provider that helps people build the support team that fits their life — not ours. Whether it’s personal care, mentoring, or supported living, we empower participants to shape how their support looks, feels, and grows. From the start, you’re in control: you choose who supports you, how you want to live, and what you’re working toward. We’re just the backup — sourcing, vetting, and supporting the right people to help you thrive.

Our teams are trained in the Good Lives Model (GLM) and Social Role Valorisation (SRV), with a focus on real-world outcomes like relationships, community roles, and confidence. We keep things transparent, values-led, and outcome-focused — supporting participants and families who want to take charge of their support and build something that works long-term. If you're ready to mentor, motivate, or innovate — we’re ready to help make it happen.
