Name: TOGETHER.AUDIO
Description:
Es gibt Momente im Leben, die alles verändern. 
So auch, wenn Lieblingsmenschen unerwartet in den künstlichen Tiefschlaf gelegt werden. 
Nichts stimmt mehr - die Verbindung, die zu diesen Menschen bis dato bestand, beginnt zu verschwinden.
Auch bei Angehörigen von unter Demenz leidenden Menschen entsteht im Laufe der Krankheit dieses Gefühl.
 
Wir - TOGETHER.AUDIO - komponieren gemeinsam mit den Angehörigen BONDING MOMENTS - VERBINDENDE MOMENTE für die Patienten, um den befürchteten Verlust dieser Verbundenheit aufzuhalten.
Mit der Lieblingsmusik des Betroffenen und durch Geschichten aus dem gemeinsamen Leben, erzählt von der vertrauten Stimme des Lieblingsmenschen, wird das Gefühl der Verbundenheit und Nähe für beide wieder hergestellt und verstärkt. 

In der Aufwachphase der Tiefschlafpatient:innen unterstützen BONDING MOMENTS ebenso, um das Gefühl des Alleinseins zu minimieren.

Es ist wissenschaftlich erwiesen, dass vertraute Klänge, wie Stimme und Musik, zu einem positiven Verlauf der Genesung beitragen können.

Die persönliche Erfahrung und die individuelle Begleitung jeder Produktion zeichnet uns aus:
vom Beratungsgespräch, dem BONDING MOMENTS RATGEBER, über die Aufnahme bis zum fertigen Produkt. 


Wie funktioniert TOGETHER.AUDIO?

Wir bieten ein persönliches Gespräch zur optimalen Vorbereitung für die Aufnahme der Botschaften, der BONDING MOMENTS.
Mit Hilfe des BONDING MOMENTS RATGEBERS, der mit Erfahrungsberichten und Tipps gefüllt ist, haben Sie einen wertvollen Begleiter in den Händen.

Es gibt mehrere Möglichkeiten, eine Botschaft aufzunehmen:
- direkt im Tonstudio
- zu Hause bei den Angehörigen
- per Sprachnachricht oder Videokonferenz

Unser TOGETHER.AUDIO Toningenieur bearbeitet Ihre Botschaft und unterlegt diese mit der Lieblingsmusik Ihres Angehörigen.
Diese BONDING MOMENTS werden dann auf ein von uns zur Verfügung gestelltes Abspielgerät übertragen, welches durch ganz spezielle, weiche Kopfhörer auffällt.

Name: Blanchardstown Center for Independent Living
Description:
For many disabled people, Independent Living can best be achieved by the employment of Personal Assistants.

Independent Living is not just about daily living. It is about self-determination, control, choice and the real freedom to make decisions Independent.

Who Are We
“BCIL It is a grassroots user-led organisation for people with disabilities with the main aim of ensuring that all people with disabilities achieve Independent Living, choice and control over their lives and full participation in society as equal citizens.“

Mission Statement
The mission of the center for independent living is to bring about real change in the lives of people with disabilities in order to achieve true independent living. We will work to ensure that the principles of independent living, choices, rights, empowerment and control are intrinsically rooted in the thinking, policies, strategies and actions of all stakeholders.

Core Values
Our core values are underpinned by the philosophy of Independent Living and all activities are aimed towards promoting: independence, options, rights and empowerment.

Our Volunteers
The aim of our Volunteer Service is to reduce isolation by matching volunteers to people with disabilities to accompany them on social events/appointments promoting inclusion and to empower people with disabilities to become active citizens within their community.
Our Volunteer service is continuing to grow at a steady pace.

If you want to get involved to make a real difference, we can talk through.

Nothing About Us Without Us

Name: RAWR Support Services
Description:
RAWR Support Services is a registered provider under the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS). Our priority is to connect and meet the needs of NDIS and aging participants across the Toowoomba, Warwick, and Stanthorpe regions.

RAWR is built on a passion for giving back to the community. The organisation supported its first participant in 2021 — who remains with RAWR today — and has since grown to support approximately 175 participants while employing around 85 staff.

RAWR's leadership team, comprised of professionals from diverse backgrounds and expertise, ensures RAWR’s services remain best practice, innovative, and tailored to individual needs.

At RAWR Support Services, we pride ourselves on delivering high-quality, genuine care to vulnerable people of all ages. Our collaborative approach empowers participants to build independence, increase opportunities, and enhance social participation, while working closely with the National Disability Insurance Agency (NDIA) to ensure funding and support are accessible to people with disability and My Aged Care for aging individuals in the community.

Our Vision
Our vision is to create, connect, and support inclusive communities where people with disability and aging individuals are empowered to live enriched, meaningful lives—both locally and beyond.

Our Mission
At RAWR Support Services, our mission is to empower people with disability and aging individuals by providing personalised support, strengthening meaningful connections, and fostering inclusive communities. We are dedicated to enhancing quality of life through compassion, collaboration, and a commitment to accessible opportunities for all.

RAWR’s Core Values

Respect: We honour the dignity, culture, and individuality of every person.
Attitude: We choose positivity, resilience, and respect—because how we show up shapes everything. A positive mindset fuels growth, teamwork, and success.
Willingness - learn and lead by example
Resilience: courage in change.

At Respite Haven, we create safe, comfortable, and empowering environments where individuals can recover, gain independence, and achieve their goals through tailored support, life-skills training, and social engagement programs.

Our Mission
To provide holistic, high-quality respite and supported accommodation that empowers individuals to live independently, develop essential life skills, and engage with their communities in a meaningful way.

Our Vision
To become Australia’s leading provider of mental health respite and independent living solutions, setting new standards in quality, innovation, and participant-centred care.

Name: Floroma Old Aged Home
Description:
Established in 1953, Floroma Old Aged Home exists to provide safe accommodation for golden oldies not catered for by the ‘for-profit retirement village’ industry.  It was originally established for the aged from Florida, Roodepoort and Maraisburg (and that’s where the name came from – FLOrida ROodepoort MAraisburg).  However, over the decades this geographic focus was expanded to include people from all over South Africa.
•	Floroma is registered as an NPO with the NPO directorate, with the registration number 002 011 NPO.
•	The independent living section consists of 74 cottages, 11 of which can accommodate couples.  The rest are for single people.
•	The Frail section can accommodate up to 66 people.  Residents in Frail get three good, healthy, tasty and nutritious meals per day, teas and coffees, daily laundry and 24/7 access to professional nursing support.
•	In addition to the above, various activities are facilitated under our active aging programme, while our Social Worker provides counselling, as needed. 
Floroma is very serious about its Not-For-Profit status and we strive to keep the cost of accommodation  as low as possible, while trying to make it a positively magical place to live in!

Name: Senifone
Description:
Bij Senifone geloven we dat technologie moet versterken—niet overweldigen.

Onze visie is eenvoudig: technologie die ouderen vrijheid, controle en gemoedsrust geeft—zonder complexiteit.

We bouwen aan een vertrouwd ecosysteem van apparaten en diensten voor ouderen en hun naasten. Van eenvoudig te gebruiken smartphones tot een companion app en SOS-noodondersteuning (binnenkort beschikbaar): elk product is ontwikkeld met één doel – mensen veilig, verbonden en zelfstandig houden.

Ons DNA is gebouwd op vier pijlers:

Veiligheid – altijd bereikbaar, hulp binnen handbereik.
Eenvoud – intuïtief ontwerp, zonder frustratie.
Vertrouwen – stabiele technologie, ondersteund door service.
Waardigheid – moderne, premium oplossingen zonder stigma.

Maar Senifone gaat verder dan producten. Wij geven vorm aan de toekomst van seniorenzelfstandigheid via digitale inclusie. Of het nu gaat om gemoedsrust voor families of zelfstandigheid voor ouderen: Senifone is altijd dichtbij.

Senifone – Altijd Dichtbij en Verbonden.

-- 

At Senifone, we believe technology should empower—not overwhelm.

Our vision is simple: technology that gives seniors freedom, control, and peace of mind—without complexity.

We’re building a trusted ecosystem of devices and services for seniors and their loved ones. From easy-to-use smartphones and tablets to a companion app and SOS emergency support (coming soon), every product is created with one purpose: to keep people safe, connected, and independent.

Our DNA is built on four pillars:
Safety – always reachable, help at hand.
Simplicity – intuitive design, no frustration.
Trust – stable technology backed by support.
Dignity – modern, premium solutions without stigma.

But Senifone goes beyond products. We are shaping the future of senior independence through digital inclusion. Whether it’s peace of mind for families or independence for seniors, Senifone is always near.

Senifone – Always Near and Connected.

Un événement UNIFIED permet aux personnes en situation de handicap de participer de manière active aux activités sportives proposées et favorise la cohésion sociale entre les personnes avec et sans handicap ce que MYrun4help permet depuis sa première édition.

Person Centred respecting and recognising your individual qualities, values and beliefs. treating you with dignity understanding you, your experiences and goals, maintaining confidential and giving you responsibility to decide what you think is best for you. WE are committed to a regional, collaborative pooling of resources with business, municipalities, society and science. Name: Harmony House Community Care & Services
Description:
“We founded Harmony House so that elderly people and those living with disabilities can get care and support as individual as they are”

The Harmony House story began in 2019 with a conversation between our two founders.

One has a family member living with a disability and the family wanted to ensure that despite this, they would live an independent and fulfilled life. One of the challenges was that the level of care being provided was sub-optimal, coupled with the knowledge that their NDIS plan wasn’t being used to its best extent.

That conversation was the spark that became the fire of an idea – to find a care provider able to do things better, and to give MORE.

More support
More empowerment
More connection
More independence
More value from NDIS plans

This is how Harmony House was born.

We believed that with our experience and genuine understanding of care needs, we could do better than other providers. A few years later, we are proud of the growth of Harmony House, and the level of care and support we continue to provide. We are now proud to service both the disability and aged care sectors.
We would love to welcome your loved one into the Harmony House family.

Our members enjoy access to social connection, personal support services, educational programs, and lifelong learning opportunities designed to reduce isolation and promote healthy, independent aging. Volunteers provide rides, home assistance, tech support, companionship, and much more, proving every day that we can operationalize love and caring through community action.

Voted Benicia’s #1 Senior Care Service for 2025, Carquinez Village is part of the national Village Movement, a growing network of grassroots organizations transforming the experience of aging. Together, we’re building a future where aging is celebrated, not feared, and no one faces it alone.

Description:
Na Regenera Homecare, estamos comprometidos em fornecer cuidados excepcionais e apoio compassivo para aqueles que estão em transição ou precisam de cuidados em casa. Nossa equipe dedicada de profissionais altamente capacitados está aqui para ajudar a garantir uma transição tranquila e fornecer cuidados personalizados no conforto do seu lar.

Nossos serviços abrangem uma variedade de necessidades, desde assistência pós-cirúrgica e gestão de medicamentos até cuidados de enfermagem especializados. Com uma abordagem centrada no paciente e foco na recuperação e bem-estar, nós nos esforçamos para garantir a máxima qualidade de vida para todos os nossos clientes.

Nós da Regenera Homecare, entendemos a importância de um cuidado atencioso e personalizado, e estamos comprometidos em proporcionar um ambiente seguro e acolhedor para todos os que atendemos.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Casa Emmaus Trieste
Description:
Casa Emmaus Residenza di 3° livello per non autosufficienti autorizzata al funzionamento ai sensi della DGR 144/2015 è gestita direttamente dall’ente privato senza fini di lucro Comunità Educante Soc. Coop. Sociale Onlus, cooperativa di tipo A nata nel 1980.  
Attualmente Comunità Educante gestisce in forma diretta la Residenza "Casa Emmaus" (aperta nel 1995 con 48 posti e successivamente ingrandita tra il 2000 e il 2001 fino agli attuali 122 posti) e i Centri Diurni “A Casa Mia”. Nel 2011 viene attivato un primo centro diurno autorizzato nel rispetto della normativa regionale vigente, inizialmente di 5 posti, incrementati gradualmente fino a dicembre 2018, con l’autorizzazione di due centri diurni da 30 posti ciascuno. Attualmente entrambi i centri diurni sono specificatamente autorizzati dalla regione per accogliere persone affette da demenze. 
Tutti i posti, sia residenziali che semiresidenziali, sono convenzionati con l'Azienda Sanitaria Giuliano Isontina.
Attualmente in Casa Emmaus lavorano 110 persone nelle diverse mansioni, tutte assunte direttamente dalla cooperativa, che non ha ritenuto di appaltare all’esterno alcun servizio (compresa la cucina che è gestita internamente da cuochi dipendenti).
L’elemento principale su cui si fonda l’erogazione dell’assistenza è il lavoro di equipe. In ognuno dei 4 nuclei della residenza e nei centri diurni lavora infatti un’equipe multiprofessionale formata da un coordinatore (educatore professionale, infermiere o dirigente di comunità), dagli infermieri, dagli operatori di assistenza, dal fisioterapista e dall’animatrice. Nel corso degli ultimi anni, i servizi si sono arricchiti di nuove professionalità come il terapista occupazionale o il tecnico della riabilitazione psichiatrica, che lavorano prevalentemente con persone affette da demenze, e il laureato in scienze motorie, che supporta le attività motorie.

Description:

We will continually strive to work in partnership with the various waterways authorities and other relevant agencies to achieve this. We also want to enable our members to connect with and support one another.

Our aim is to raise awareness of the fact that there are people aboard boats with a variety of accessibility needs on our inland waterways. There are actually more people living on boats who identify as having a disability than the national average! The Accessible Waterways Association also wants to break down barriers, and influence public attitudes and behaviours toward and around disabled people.

Name: CareForTom Foundation
Description:
As a beacon of unwavering support for all families, we are dedicated to guiding you through the intricate landscape of elder care and services with clarity and compassion. Our commitment to providing unbiased assistance ensures that every family's journey is met with understanding and respect, empowering you to make informed decisions for your loved ones.

The Foundation’s first project, CareForTom.org, is dedicated to improving the quality of life for elderly people and individuals facing end-of-life situations, as well as their caregivers. After almost two years of extensive research, CareForTom.org aims to provide accessible resources, support, and innovative technological solutions to ease the elder care and end-of-life journey. CareForTom.org will fill a critical gap by offering accurate, unbiased, and continuously updated information.

Currently, there is no single, comprehensive, and unbiased online resource in the US for families to access elder care and end-of-life information. Existing resources are often influenced by profit-driven motives or advertising dollars, compromising the accuracy and impartiality of the information provided. CareForTom.org is committed to ensuring that the information we provide is accurate, unbiased, and solely in the best interest of the families we serve.

Let us walk this path together, offering a helping hand during your time of need. With CareForTom.org by your side, you are never alone in navigating the complexities of elder care.

Name: VIDA Care Opieka Domowa dla Seniorów
Description:
Mieszkasz daleko. Martwisz się codziennie.

W VIDA Care pozwalamy Ci być po prostu córką/synem, a nie całodobowym opiekunem i koordynatorem medycznym w jednym. Zdejmujemy z Ciebie ciężar walki o wizyty lekarskie, pilnowania leków i organizowania obiadów, abyś mógł/mogła skupić się na tym, co najważniejsze – na relacji.
Na swoim życiu i pracy, które nie znikają w obliczu starości rodzica. 

Nasz cel? Spersonalizowana, zorientowana na relację i rezultaty opieka dla Twojego rodzica i spokój ducha dla Ciebie. 

Osiągamy to nie przez improwizację, ale przez system. Stworzyliśmy autorski Protokół Złoty Standard – metodologię opieki domowej, która zastępuje chaos i niepewność absolutną niezawodnością.

Jak to robimy?

 ELITARNY ZESPÓŁ: Zatrudniamy 5% najlepszych kandydatów po wieloetapowej weryfikacji. Do naszego zespołu zapraszamy wyłącznie profesjonalistów, których weryfikujemy pod kątem charakteru, a nie tylko umiejętności.

 GWARANTOWANA NIEZAWODNOŚĆ: Działamy w oparciu o system, nie przypadek. Od procedur higieny po plan na wypadek kryzysu – każdy detal jest zdefiniowany, by eliminować Twój niepokój.

 PROAKTYWNA KOMUNIKACJA: Nie czekasz na informacje. My je dostarczamy. Dzięki regularnym, pisemnym raportom masz pewność, co dzieje się u Twojego bliskiego, bez konieczności ciągłego dzwonienia. Oddajemy Ci poczucie bycia na bieżąco, bez względu na odległość.

 INWESTYCJA W JAKOŚĆ ŻYCIA: Zapewniamy więcej niż bezpieczeństwo – inwestujemy w jakość życia. Nasz Protokół Aktywnego Dobrostanu to gwarancja, że każdy dzień Twojego rodzica będzie wypełniony sensem, rozmową i godnością, a nie tylko biernym oczekiwaniem.

VIDA Care to wybór dla rodzin, dla których kompromis nie jest opcją. Jesteśmy tu, by zdjąć z Twoich barków jeden z najcięższych życiowych ciężarów.

Zapraszamy do rozmowy o Twoim spokoju ducha.

Name: Nearish Support
Description:
Online bookings and referrals open soon! Subscribe for updates - https://nearishsupport.com.au/subscribe

Nearish Support provides support work, psychosocial recovery coaching, and social work - all delivered online, remotely across Australia. 

We offer flexible, person-centred support via text, email, phone, or video call, so people can engage in ways that fit their pace and energy. 

Our approach is trauma-informed, neurodiversity affirming, and grounded in professional expertise as well as lived experience. 

We believe support should be accessible, collaborative, and never require you to mask or push through to receive it.

We provide services to privately paying clients, and plan or self-managed NDIS participants typically with one or many of the following experiences/identities:
- Psychosocial disability
- Autism
- ADHD
- Chronic illness (including autoimmune conditions, ME/CFS, POTS and other forms of dysautonomia, MCAS, h/EDS, fibromyalgia, chronic pain, endometriosis, adenomyosis, migraine and more) 
- Trauma and complex trauma (including dissociation and multiplicity)
- Queer and/or gender diverse folk
- Disabled professionals working in the disability and mental health sectors 

Socials @nearish_support
Email  info@nearishsupport.com.au
Website  https://nearishsupport.com.au

Name: Michigan Statewide Independent Living Council (MiSILC)
Description:
The Michigan SILC works in partnership with Michigan Rehabilitation Services (MRS), Bureau of Services to Blind Persons (BSBP), Disability Network/Michigan, Michigan's statewide network of Centers for Independent Living (CILs) and other partner organizations to prepare the State Plan for Independent Living.

The Council promotes a philosophy of independent living. This includes consumer control, peer support, self-help, self-determination, equal access, and individual and systems advocacy to maximize the leadership, empowerment, independence, and productivity of people with disabilities. The Council promotes integration and full inclusion of people with disabilities into the mainstream of American society.

Name: Fight4Balance
Description:
Fight4Balance (F4B) is an Australian registered charity dedicated to improving the lived experience and wellbeing of adolescent youth with learning and perceptual disabilities and their families. F4B delivers programs centred on family prosperity, utilizing dynamic peer-to-peer buddy systems. All F4B programs are designed in consultation with our team of clinical health and training practitioners adopting an evidence-based platform, combining research evidence, expert opinion, and well-grounded experience. 

The community we serve includes adolescent youth with mild to moderate  learning and perceptual  disabilities associated with Autism Spectrum Disorder, Down syndrome, Acquired brain injury, Fragile X syndrome,  Klinefelter syndrome, and other undiagnosed learning and perceptual disabilities.

Our vision is to provide pathways for affected youth and their families to connect with mainstream sporting clubs, school-based community programs and vocational employment programs to foster a sense of belonging and strengthen the bonds between disabled and non-disabled communities.

You're in safe hands with the team at Blueassist

Our parent company, Blueforce, has been a leader in providing services for independent living and aged care for over 20 years, and we’re thrilled to be a part of their vision to offer even more dedicated customer experiences and quality services.

Our goal is to help individuals stay in their own homes for as long as possible, while also providing peace of mind to their loved ones. We offer a suite of products that can be tailored to your needs, whether it’s for a single home or a holistic emergency care solution for aged care and independent living providers.

We also offer a discrete duress solution for those who need an added layer of protection while working alone. And with our Australia-based monitoring centre operating 24/7, you can trust that we’ll always be there to assist you.

At Blueassist, we’re passionate about helping people feel secure and comfortable in their own homes. We value our existing relationships and are always excited to build new ones. We’re a friendly and approachable company, and we want you to feel safe and cared for when you choose us as your provider of personal medical alarms and lone worker devices.

So, if you’re looking for a company that prioritizes customer care and is dedicated to helping individuals stay in their homes for longer, you’ve come to the right place. We’re here to help, and we’d be delighted to work with you.

Name: Schoonderhage vzw
Description:
Schoonderhage vzw is een door het VAPH vergunde zorgaanbieder. Wij staan garant voor een brede waaier aan zorg en ondersteuning aan volwassen personen met een verstandelijke handicap: van intensieve woonondersteuning over dagbesteding tot begeleid werk.
Binnen Schoonderhage staat het welzijn van bewoners en medewerkers centraal. De begrippen solidariteit, kleinschaligheid en inclusie geven onze werking vorm. Door die kleinschaligheid kunnen we voor onze bewoners een warme woon-& werkomgeving garanderen.
In welke functie je ook werkt (begeleider, poetskracht, administratie, leidinggevende,...), jij vormt een onmisbare schakel in ons streven naar bijzondere zorg voor bijzondere mensen!
De verschillende entiteiten van Schoonderhage werken gedecentraliseerd in de regio Ninove-Aalst-Herzele, de administratieve diensten en directie werken overkoepelend vanuit de administratieve zetel in Pollare.

Schoonderhage is een organisatie in volle groei, waar wordt geïnvesteerd in medewerkers: kansen bieden en ontplooiing stimuleren zijn sleutelbegrippen. Er worden dan ook voldoende vormingsmogelijkheden aangeboden, zowel individueel als in groep.
Binnen Schoonderhage staan ervaren, geëngageerde en hulpvaardige collega’s klaar, die solidair samenwerken in een aangename werkomgeving. Solidair samenwerken, oprecht zijn en zorgzaam zijn, vormen de kerncompetenties voor alle medewerkers.

Name: Custom Employment Solutions

Custom Employment Solutions (CES) – Ticket to Work Employment Network

At CES, we help individuals receiving SSI or SSDI benefits return to work, increase their income, and move toward financial independence through the Social Security Ticket to Work Program.

As an authorized Employment Network, CES provides:
	•	Career Counseling & Job Placement – Explore opportunities that fit your skills and goals.
	•	Benefits Counseling – Understand how working impacts your Social Security benefits.
	•	Ongoing Support – Get the tools, training, and coaching you need to succeed on the job.

Our mission is to empower individuals with disabilities to achieve greater independence through meaningful employment. With over 18 years of experience serving the disability community, CES is committed to walking alongside you on your path to self-sufficiency.

Participation in Ticket to Work is free and voluntary for SSI/SSDI beneficiaries ages 18–64.

Name: Project Growth
Description:
Project Growth was established in Ballarat in late 2021 when three friends saw a gap in the lives of young males living with disabilities. At the time, Founders Sam, James, and Bryson supported young males transitioning from finishing school, where all social outlets had been stripped, to programs with wide but not specific goals or targeted interests. The boys had noticed during this transition period that many of their participants were struggling to find confidence and enjoyment within themselves and these programs.

Sam, James, and Bryson, all young males themselves, used their knowledge and passion to set up the Project Growth program to allow them to connect and form friendships with like-minded individuals in a meaningful, engaging, and inclusive environment. The Project Growth team takes great pride in their work, which has resulted in great success within the Ballarat community, resulting in over 60 participants partaking weekly and over a 98% retention rate. The confidence our participants have gained from our program has assisted many individuals in gaining employment, living more independently, getting out into the community, and growing and achieving as individuals.

Name: Divine Collective

Our focus is Support Coordination and Recovery Coaching because that’s where real change happens.
These roles allow us to walk beside participants, break down the complexities of the NDIS, and build the confidence and knowledge needed to take control.

We don’t take no for an answer — especially when the NDIA tries to take funding away from participants who need it most.
We advocate fiercely, educate clearly, and support consistently.
We work with participants and their support networks to make sure they’re informed, empowered, and living the life they choose, not the one they’ve been handed.
At Le Divine Collective, we don’t just support people — we back them, we fight for them, and we help them thrive.
We have a fabulous team of well experienced professionals ready with capacity.

Name: OPTIMUM REHABILITATION CENTRE
Description:
Optimum Rehabilitation Centre (ORC) provides rehabilitation services to persons with disability through four thematic areas including; Assistive technology to provide appropriate assistive devices, Community Based Rehabilitation (CBR), Clinic Based Rehabilitation and Health promotion to prevent Non-communicable diseases.
The long term plan for ORC is to extend rehabilitation services to northern Uganda which is still recovering from the effects of a long standing gorilla war which has left the region impoverished with little or no social services like education, healthcare, water, sanitation and hygiene. 
Currently ORC has its head office in Kawuku, Entebbe where they operate a rehabilitation workshop that produces assistive technology devices like wheelchairs, standers, special seats, crutches for PWDs. ORC is expected to grow and continue to provide rehabilitation services to PWDs all over the country and beyond through collaboration with like-minded organizations and individuals.

Description:
Relier les pointillés, des ateliers artistiques auprès de publics en situation de handicap

Diplômée d'un Master en Intervention Sociale, elle pratique le théâtre depuis 2020 et se forme aux médiations théâtrales. Elle a notamment travaillé en GEM (Groupe d'entraide mutuelle), en foyer pour travailleurs en situation de handicap ainsi qu'en milieu scolaire. 

Les ateliers artistiques/d'expression théâtrale peuvent être proposés en groupe ou en session individuelle, en établissement accueillant du public ainsi qu'à domicile. 

La médiation par le théâtre est particulièrement adaptée aux publics en situation de handicap ou en "difficulté" au sens large (précarité, insertion...). Elle permet d'offrir un espace d'expression et d'exploration, de (re)créer du lien en faisant groupe, de soutenir le développement des habilités sociales.

Des ateliers de création (arts plastiques) peuvent être proposés à certains groupes (fabrication de marionnettes ou de masques, par exemple). 

Les ateliers sont co-construits avec les participant(e)s afin de s'adapter aux besoins, envies et objectifs que nous déterminons ensemble

Our Level 3 Specialist Support Coordinator can help you apply for NDIS, understand your plan, link you to service providers and allied health, assist you in reviews and when your circumstances change, write reports to the NDIS, and keep track of your spending and budgets in your plan. 

Our owner has a diploma in Community Services, diploma in Counselling, and an associate degree in Community Sector Management. Además de ser un Centro de Tratamientos Interdisciplinarios, contamos con la Prestación de Apoyo a la Integración Escolar (SAIE), que favorece la inclusión de niños y adolescentes en el ámbito educativo, trabajando en conjunto con familias, docentes y equipos escolares para garantizar trayectorias educativas significativas y equitativas.

Contamos con un equipo de profesionales en: fonoaudiología, psicología, psicopedagogía, terapia ocupacional y musicoterapia, y taller de habilidades sociales.

En Puertas Abiertas creemos que cada niño y cada familia merece ser abordada, escuchada y acompañada desde múltiples miradas, construyendo juntos caminos de inclusión, desarrollo y bienestar.

Name: mUKs - mit Unterstützter Kommunikation sprechen
Description:
Unterstützte Kommunikation:
Unterstützte Kommunikation (engl.: Augmentative and Alternative Communication) bedeutet, fehlende Sprache zu ergänzen oder zu ersetzen, um die kommunikative Teilhabe von Menschen, die Schwierigkeiten im Gebrauch und/oder Verstehen von Sprache haben, zu verbessern. Dazu gibt es verschiedene Kommunikationsmöglichkeiten, wie beispielsweise Sprachausgabegeräte, Gebärden, Kommunikationsmappen und -tafeln mit Symbolen oder einzelne Bild- und Symbolkärtchen.

Unsere Angebote:
- Diagnostik und Förderung für Menschen mit eingeschränktem Kommunikationsvermögen und Bedarf an Unterstützter Kommunikation
- Beratung zum Thema Unterstützte Kommunikation 
- Fortbildungen zur Unterstützten Kommunikation für Angehörige, Fachpersonal und Interessierte 

Unser Ziel:
Ziel des Vereins ist es, für UK-Nutzer:innen eine geeignete und alltagstaugliche Kommunikationsform zu finden, sie in ihrem Kommunikationsprozess zu fördern und zu begleiten und ihnen so die Möglichkeit zu Selbstbestimmung und Mitsprache zu eröffnen.

Name: Humanus Haus
Description:
Das Humanushaus ist eine sozialtherapeutische Lebens- und Arbeitsgemeinschaft. Die Kernaufgabe besteht darin, Lebensräume für Menschen mit besonderen Bedürfnissen zu sichern und zu gestalten. Das Zusammenleben fördert die individuellen Bedürfnisse zur Persönlichkeitsentwicklung und zur selbstgestalteten Lebensführung. In das Humanushaus können Jugendliche und erwachsene Menschen mit unterschiedlichen Behinderungen und  Alter aufgenommen werden.

Die Freizeitangebote im Humanushaus umfassen neben künstlerisch-musischen Angeboten auch Sport und Bildung. Mit verschiedenen anthroposophischen Therapien werden die Bewohnerinnen und Bewohner mit einem salutogenetischen Ansatz in ihrer Entwicklung angeregt und ihre Gesundheit wird erhalten beziehungsweise gefördert. Das Humanushaus bietet Ausbildungsplätze in sozialen Berufen auf verschiedenen Stufen an.

Das Humanushaus ist als «Wohnheim mit Beschäftigung» konzipiert. Eine Gemeinschaft mit Dorfcharakter bildet die Grundlage für das Zusammenleben in den 12  individuell gestalteten Wohngemeinschaften. Die verschiedenen Werkstätten sind darauf ausgerichtet, den Menschen mit Behinderung angemessene Arbeitsformen zu ermöglichen, welche Sinngebung und Anerkennung im Sinne von Arbeit vermitteln.

Name: The Life Project (Bath)
Description:
The Life Project supports people with learning disabilities, their families, and carers through:

- Day Services: Day Services providing creative and purposeful activities for adults with learning disabilities in Bath and the surrounding area (currently delivered primarily at our Allotment and Barn facilities)
- Family Support: Support for Family Carers including a weekly drop-in and termly retreats for parents.
- Socials: A programme of social events for families, friends and volunteers.
- Discipleship: Discipleship and worship opportunities for adults with learning disabilities.

The values of The Life Project (Bath) are at the heart of who we are and what we do – they are guided by a Christian ethos. These values have strong roots in the Christian teaching and practice, whilst also being accessible and meaningful for all – regardless of background. We believe that every person is unique – fearfully and wonderfully made (Psalm 139:4) – and is a valuable gift to the world and our community.

Our Values
The Life Project (Bath) has four key values – Creative, Inclusive, Enabling, Community – which are detailed below:

Creative: We refer to those who come along to our sessions at the Old Acorn Barn as ‘Makers’ to emphasise their role to come and be meaningfully, purposefully, and creatively engaged.

Inclusive: We recognise the intrinsic value of each individual and welcome into our Life Project community adults with learning disabilities as well as family, friends and carers from all backgrounds, all faiths and none.

Enabling: For us inclusion means enabling all people to have choices, to participate, and to undertake tasks whether these are considered ordinary or extraordinary.

Community: We believe we share the responsibility to support one another through community. We understand our true selves and reach our full potential when we share life with others – respecting and celebrating differences through healthy relationships.

High-Intensity Supports — Complex Bowel Care, Enteral Feeding, Dysphagia, Ventilator Support, Tracheostomy Care, Urinary Catheter, Subcutaneous Injections, Complex Wound Care, and Epilepsy/Seizure Support.

Daily & Community Supports — Personal care, household tasks, transport, and participation in community activities.

Capacity Building & Behaviour Support Implementation — Helping participants gain skills, independence, and quality of life.

What sets us apart:

Expertise: Led by Registered Nurses with 15+ years of clinical experience.

Fast Response: Referrals actioned in 24–48 hours for urgent needs.

Consistency: Skilled, reliable staff — not “fill-ins” or random rotations.

Partnership: Clear communication with participants, families, and Support Coordinators.

Our Unique Approach to STA

Breakaway Retreats provides two types of fully customised STA packaged services:

1. Breakaway Direct – Your package includes independent accommodation (e.g. resorts, hotels, Airbnb etc.) and suits participants looking for their own space.

2. Breakaway Connect – Your package includes high-quality hosted homestay accommodation provided through the Disability Homestay Network program. This option gives participants the opportunity to connect further with the community.

Commonly referred to as Respite, Short Term Accommodation (STA) is a way of providing much needed support for participants and their network to have time out, try new things and achieve their goals. Your Breakaway Retreats STA includes your accommodation, support, food and activities.

At Capable Minds, we are dedicated to empowering NDIS participants by providing comprehensive support services tailored to individual needs. Our mission is to enhance the quality of life for people with disabilities, promoting independence, choice, and control.

Our Services:

Support Coordination:
We offer expert support coordination to help NDIS participants navigate the complexities of the NDIS, connect with the right services, and achieve their personal goals. Our team works closely with participants to develop and implement individualised plans, ensuring access to the best available resources and supports.

Core Support:
Our core support services are designed to assist with daily living activities, promoting independence and enhancing the well-being of our participants. Whether it's personal care, household tasks, or community engagement, we provide reliable and compassionate support tailored to each individual's unique needs.

Why Choose Us:

Person-Centred Approach: We prioritise the preferences and goals of our participants, ensuring that they are at the heart of every decision.

Experienced Team: Our dedicated professionals have extensive knowledge of the NDIS and a deep commitment to supporting people with disabilities.
Comprehensive Services: From support coordination to core support, we offer a wide range of services to meet diverse needs.

Community Connections: We have strong relationships with a network of service providers and community organisations to offer the best possible support.

Join us on our journey to make a positive impact in the lives of NDIS participants. Connect with us today to learn more about how we can support you or your loved one in achieving greater independence and quality of life.

Todo ello bajo un enfoque de mejora continua y excelencia, aplicando la norma ISO 9001 y el modelo de calidad EFQM.

Nuestra trayectoria nos ha consolidado como una entidad de referencia en el sector, con un fuerte compromiso con la innovación y la atención centrada en la persona. En la última década hemos impulsado modelos avanzados de calidad de vida y reforzado la colaboración con entidades públicas y de economía social para el desarrollo de proyectos que respondan a las necesidades reales de las personas mayores.

At The Caring App, we are dedicated to empowering the elderly and vulnerable with cutting-edge solutions, working to improve their quality of life in their own home. We believe that technology is meant to enrich not only the lives of the young but also the elderly. With our roots deeply embedded in non-intrusive AI-driven solutions, we are committed to reshaping caregiving for at-risk individuals worldwide.

Leveraging electricity data from Smart Meters, our advanced algorithm discreetly monitors the individuals day-to-day activity. Our goal is to deliver support that guarantees the well-being of every person we serve.Be a part of our journey as we redefine elderly care. Description:
Kompass Community Inclusion is a proud South Australian, NDIS-registered provider on a mission to empower people with disabilities to live adventurous, connected, and meaningful lives.

We believe support should never be one-size-fits-all. That’s why we tailor everything to the individual — their interests, their goals, and the way they want to live. Whether it’s building daily living skills, accessing the community, trying new things, or simply having a laugh and a great time, we walk alongside our participants with care, consistency, and creativity.

Name: Entidad Tutelar Mare de Deu dels Desamparats
Description:
La Fundación Mare de Deus de los Desemparats es una entidad sin ánimo de lucro cuya misión tiene claramente una vocación de ayuda, apoyo, acompañamiento y atención integral de la persona. Sus actuaciones han ido cambiando con el nacimiento de los diferentes proyectos y con la evolución del entorno, pero siempre ha mantenido su principal propósito; la ayuda y atención a los más necesitados. Siguiendo las palabras que siempre ha recogido el Mossén Valentín y a las cuales hace referencia en su libro Desemparats entre vies i campanes; “La Fundación siempre estará al servicio de todos los jóvenes y de las familias, de los ancianos, enfermos y necesitados de esta Comunidad”.  Por todo ello, en el año 2014, con la visión de poder ampliar su propósito de ayuda y asistencia decide crear una Entidad Tutelar con el objetivo de aumentar su campo de actuación y su incidencia social. La misión de esta Entidad Tutelar sería proteger y velar por los intereses de personas en situación de vulnerabilidad que hayan sido incapacitadas judicialmente o en proceso de modificación de la capacidad, facilitando las condiciones necesarias para garantizar una atención adecuada y protección de sus derechos. Nuestro objetivo es mejorar la calidad de vida de nuestros asistidos, orientándoles a la promoción de su autonomía y desarrollo de sus capacidades en todo lo que establece la correspondiente resolución judicial.

Desde la Entidad Tutelar la Torrassa, se pretende dar un nuevo enfoque al modelo de intervención, conectando de una forma más directa con el usuario, las familias y las personas que necesiten de nuestro apoyo o conocimientos a través de un nuevo lugar de encuentro.

Name: ANOEH
Description:
l'ANOEH est une association composée d'humains engagés auprès des personnes en situation de handicap et qui ont les conviction que l'ostéopathie a un rôle majeur à tenir en matière de qualité de vie et de prévention. 

 

Les intérêts de l'ostéopathie dans l'accompagnement des personnes en situation de handicap sont multiples. L'ostéopathie est une médecine manuelle reposant sur 3 grands principes que sont : 

La capacité d'autorégulation du corps 
Le corps a une capacité d'autorégulation et d'adaptation. Cette notion est centrale dans la question de handicap. Le travail de l'ostéopathe par définition, n'est donc pas de soigner le patient mais de permettre au corps de récupérer, de maintenir ou d'améliorer sa capacité d'autorégulation en le libérant des contraintes mécaniques faisant obstacle à celle-ci.

Le lien entre une structure et sa fonction 
Une structure en dysfonction (non pathologique mais dysfonctionnelle) perdra en mobilité. Une structure en perte de mobilité dysfonctionnera. Ainsi l'ostéopathe établit un diagnostic ostéopathique grâce à des tests de mobilité précis.

L'unité du corps 
Toutes les structures du corps sont liées entre elles. En conséquence, l'origine d'une douleur ou d'un trouble peut être très éloignée d'elle. La localisation de la douleur n'est donc pas un élément indispensable à l'établissement d'un diagnostic ostéopathique. L'ostéopathie est donc une pratique de choix dans la prise en charge de la douleur des personnes ayant des troubles de la communication et ne pouvant verbaliser clairement la localisation d'une douleur.

"Dans la communication non verbale, le corps est un lieu d'échange privilégié". 

Le travail manuel a également un impact sur la corporalité, le sentiment d'inclusion sociale.

Did you know that there are no 2 Zebra stripe patterns the same in the world? Much like the human fingerprint. And here at Zebra Care, we also believe that no 2 people are the same. That is why we provide a unique personalised service, so that our participants feel seen, heard and supported.

Whether we are your first NDIS plan manager, or you are looking to switch to a new one, let Zebra Care help manage your National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) funding, so you can spend more time on what’s important… you!

Zebra Care is a nationally registered NDIS provider in Plan Management, ready to assist Participants nationwide. Our team will help you by paying provider invoices and managing the financial responsibilities of your plan.

At Zebra Care we pride ourselves on the personal, client-focused, and high-quality service that we provide. We follow the NDIS Practice Standards and Quality Indicators to maintain excellent results for both you and us.

We have a wealth of business and bookkeeping experience, and draw on our strengths of efficiency, individualised customer service and understanding that everyone is unique.

Name: REACH Columbus, Inc.

Empowering Abilities, Enriching Lives

At REACH Columbus, Inc., we create meaningful experiences for adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities. Through our engaging Day Program, participants build friendships, develop employment & life skills, explore fun activities, connect with the community, and embrace healthy habits—making every day an opportunity to grow!

We also support families through Participant Assistance & Care (PAC) services, offering a personalized, “friends and family” approach. Families choose their PAC support person, and we handle the training, payroll, and goal tracking—ensuring the best care for their loved ones.

As an Indiana Medicaid provider for Day Program & PAC services, we also welcome self-pay individuals. Whether you're looking for a supportive program or a trusted partner in care, REACH is here to help!

 Together, we thrive.

Name: 3rd World Congress of Age Friendly Cities and Communities
Description:
Major global events play a pivotal role in assessing progress and shaping the future of the age friendly movement. These gatherings provide essential platforms for stock taking, knowledge exchange, and strategic alignment.

The first two global congresses on age friendly cities and communities, held in Dublin, Ireland (2011) and Quebec City, Canada (2013), were foundational in building momentum for the global age friendly movement. They brought cities and communities together to exchange knowledge, align on shared priorities, and formally commit to action through initiatives such as the Dublin Declaration. These early conferences also enabled WHO to collect insights from early adopters, refine its age friendly cities and communities  (AFCC) framework, and expand the Global Network. They helped establish a tone of solidarity, innovation, and collaborative ambition that continues to define the movement.

The third Global Congress in San Sebastian, Spain (2026) will offer a timely and important opportunity to reflect on progress since the Global Network’s creation and during the first half of the UN Decade of Healthy Ageing (2021–2030). It will focus on our changing world, identify emerging opportunities including intergenerational connectedness, digital inclusion, caring and resilience in communities in the face of climate changes, conflict and pandemics as well as identify other gaps.

 Its setting will reinforce the importance of the Spanish-speaking community and engagement in the age friendly agenda, already one of the most spoken languages in the Global Network.

Name: Australian Care Solutions
Description:
At Australian Care Solutions, we don’t believe in one-size-fits-all.
We believe in listening first. We believe in showing up. We believe in building support around the whole person - not just their NDIS plan.

We understand that disability support is deeply personal. That’s why we work alongside each participant with care, cultural awareness, and respect - taking the time to understand their story, their strengths, and what a good day looks like for them.

Because that’s what matters.

We support people from all walks of life, across different cultures, languages, and lived experiences. Whether it’s helping someone gain independence at home, find confidence in the community, or simply feel more seen and supported - we're here for the everyday moments that build a better life.

Our services include:

•  Plan Management – taking care of the admin so you can focus on living.
•  Day Centre & Group Activities – creating safe spaces to connect, grow and thrive.
•  Assistance with Personal Activities – supporting independence, always with dignity.
•  Therapeutic Supports – tailored guidance for physical, emotional, and developmental wellbeing.
•  Community Participation – getting involved, staying connected, and building confidence.
•  Support Coordination – helping you navigate the NDIS with someone in your corner.
•  Personal Care & Safety – ensuring comfort, dignity, and protection in everyday living.
•  SIL Accommodation – supportive independent living in safe, comfortable environments.
•  Assistive Travel & Transport – helping you get where you need to go, safely and confidently.

We’re not here to manage you.
We’re here to walk beside you - with warmth, transparency, and practical support that fits who you are.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: AGB Care
Description:
AGB Care, established in 2022 in Findon, South Australia, is a trusted NDIS service provider committed to offering personalised care that empowers individuals with disabilities to achieve independence and improve their quality of life. 
Our Services:
1. Disability Accommodation: We provide specialised accommodation tailored to the needs of each participant, fostering independence and inclusion.
2. Assisted Transport: Our transport services ensure participants can safely and comfortably attend appointments, social events, and daily activities. 
3. Day Care: We offer engaging day care services that help participants develop social skills and form meaningful relationships.
4. Community Participation: We facilitate opportunities for participants to engage in community activities, promoting inclusion and social connections.
5. Life Skills: Our life skills programmes assist participants in building confidence and independence by developing essential everyday skills.
6. Supported Independent Living (SIL): We support participants to live independently while receiving the assistance they need to manage daily tasks and engage in their community.
Our Core Values:
1. Empathy: We listen and understand the unique needs of each participant, providing compassionate care.
2. Quality Care: We are committed to delivering high-quality services that meet participants’ expectations. 
3. Integrity: We operate with transparency and ethical practices, building trust with our participants and their families.
4. Empowerment: We support participants in making informed decisions about their care, promoting autonomy.

AGB Care is proud to be a trusted NDIS provider, offering person-centered services that make a meaningful difference in the lives of the people we support.

Name: Bee-Able Accessibility Services
Description:
Bee-Able, zet zich in voor een toegankelijke en inclusieve samenleving. We geloven dat iedereen, ongeacht hun beperkingen of achtergrond, de kans moet hebben om volledig deel te nemen aan de maatschappij.

Naast onze consultancy diensten voor bedrijven, hebben we ook een app ontwikkeld die de toegankelijkheid inzichtelijk maakt. Deze app geeft gebruikers informatie over de toegankelijkheid van openbare gebouwen, zoals restaurants, winkels en theaters. Zo kunnen mensen met beperkingen gemakkelijk locaties vinden die geschikt zijn voor hun behoeften.

We zijn ook trots om aan te kondigen dat we alle openbare invalideparkeerplaatsen in Nederland in kaart hebben gebracht. Dit helpt mensen met een handicap om gemakkelijker geschikte parkeerplaatsen te vinden en geeft hen meer onafhankelijkheid en vrijheid.

Ons team van ervaren professionals biedt maatwerk-oplossingen voor onze klanten. We helpen hen bij het voldoen aan de wettelijke vereisten voor toegankelijkheid, maar we gaan verder dan dat. We werken samen met onze klanten om een inclusieve cultuur te creëren die de diversiteit en unieke bijdragen van alle medewerkers omarmt.

We zijn vastbesloten om een positieve verandering teweeg te brengen en zijn altijd op zoek naar manieren om onze impact te vergroten. Als je geïnteresseerd bent in ons bedrijf en onze diensten, neem dan gerust contact met ons op. Samen kunnen we werken aan een toegankelijke en inclusieve samenleving.

Doel van Schildwijk dagbesteding is om zorg op maat te bieden in een huiselijke en warme sfeer,
zodat gasten zich thuis voelen en het vertrouwen in zichzelf vergroten. 
Hierbij willen we het “normale leven” nastreven, waardoor gasten vaardigheden behouden en zo mogelijk ook uitbreiden. 

Naast het bieden van ondersteuning aan mensen met dementie, bieden we ook specifieke plaatsen voor ouderen die te maken hebben met eenzaamheid en sociale isolatie. Onze programma's zijn ontworpen om deze doelgroep te ondersteunen en hen een veilige en stimulerende omgeving te bieden waarin ze sociale interactie, betrokkenheid en ondersteuning kunnen vinden. Met empathie en begrip richten we ons ook op het helpen van ouderen die worstelen met deze uitdagingen, zodat ze zich gehoord, gezien en verbonden voelen.

Schildwijk dagbesteding sluit aan bij de identiteit en de belevingswereld van de gasten , die helaas in toenemende mate aanvullende hulp nodig heeft. Iedere gast verdient ons respect, want elke gast heeft zijn of haar eigen levensgeschiedenis, en iedereen is Uniek.
Wij staan ervoor om aandacht voor elkaar te hebben op een eerlijke en respectvolle manier,
waardoor wij een fijne plek kunnen bieden aan mensen die structuur, regelmaat en/of een nuttige invulling van hun tijd zoeken.
Voor de partner of mantelzorger is het bij Schildwijk dagbesteding de mogelijkheid om de zorg even te delen en uit handen te geven; op die manier dragen we samen de zorg.

Name: Javela
Description:
Social isolation means having few social relationships or infrequent social contact with others. For senior citizens, this often means they have limited interactions with family, friends, or community members, leading to physical separation and psychological/emotional detachment from their social networks.

Lack of social interaction deprives us of cognitive stimulation, which is vital for maintaining brain health. Social isolation can lead to cognitive decline, increased risk of dementia, and mental health issues such as depression, negatively impacting the quality of life for elderly individuals.

Social engagement is active participation in conversations and interactions that foster connections with others. Social engagement provides cognitive, emotional, and physical benefits. For the elderly, social engagement is crucial for maintaining mental health, cognitive function, and overall well-being. Research such as the I-CONECT study shows that social engagement, including online conversation via video chat, can help mitigate the effects of social isolation, such as cognitive decline and increased risk of dementia.

Javela Friends uses video chat technology with a super-simplified tablet interface to connect seniors with conversation companions. This provides regular social engagement and cognitive stimulation, which helps to combat the effects of social isolation. The Javela tablet interface is designed to be very easy to use, minimizing the technological barriers that can prevent many seniors from using common video chat platforms.

Seniors won’t need to find an app, sign in, initiate a call, or grant camera/microphone permissions. They’ll just need to keep their tablet turned on and charged. It’s that simple.

Javela Friends are a global community of people of all ages, from many different places and walks of life. They’re trained in how to engage in cognitively stimulating and socially engaging conversation. And they love to meet new people & make new friends!

Description:
Our mission is to use live theatre to provide performing, vocational and life skills opportunities for adults (18+) with disabilities: cognitive and/or physical and/or neurodivergent. Unlabeled Theatre creates opportunities for actor-artists with disabilities, volunteers, and community members to experience what people with disabilities can do. Our work allows people to truly be seen, and be genuinely included.

The full scope of Unlabeled Theatre Company work includes:

1. Our inclusive theatre performances, featuring artist-actors with disabilities

2. Meaningful employment for adults with disabilities across our organization; also intentionally hiring freelance artists with disabilities for photography, graphic design, layout, artwork, etc.

3. Daily arts-centered classes designed to increase life skills in community involvement, personal independence, health & wellness, vocational abilities and more.

4. Developing and marketing inclusive theatre programming to high schools and colleges across the United States

5. Using our (eventual) space as a resource to help other disability-focused organizations (fundraising galas; concerts and events; speakers and conferences, etc.)

As the largest 'minority' group in the world, disability is ‘the great equalizer.’ Regardless of race, class, geographic location, gender, or language, disability lives within all of our communities. Working with volunteer partners in performances, audience members, and partners, we work to expand general awareness around disability inclusion and expand accessible arts communities for all.

Name: Hulp Waar Nodig
Description:
Als twee zussen zijn wij jarenlang mantelzorgers voor onze moeder geweest. De laatste jaren van haar leven 24/7 met een Persoonsgebonden Budget (PGB). Dit hebben wij met heel veel liefde gedaan! En we konden het gelukkig samen doen. Toch miste we af en toe praktische hulp. Er is genoeg hulp te vinden, maar niet echt toegepast op wat wij op dat moment nodig hadden. Vaak zit er een abonnement aan vast, is de hulp duur, is de hulp niet beschikbaar wanneer het ons uitkomt of zijn de werkzaamheden die de hulp mag doen zeer beperkt.

Daarom zijn we op 1 april 2016 begonnen met Hulp Waar Nodig. Dat was erg spannend en tegelijkertijd een geweldige uitdaging.


Het ontstaan van Hulp Waar Nodig
Het concept was makkelijk: hulp bieden aan senioren en mantelzorgers.

Dus was de naam ook makkelijk: Hulp Waar Nodig.

Onze kernwaarden hadden we ook zo bedacht: Flexibel, persoonlijk, klantgericht, betrokken, betrouwbaar, betaalbaar en klantvriendelijk.

We zijn toen aan de keukentafel begonnen met het maken van een website en een flyer. Daarna zijn we zelf de flyers gaan verspreiden. We hebben contact gezocht met gemeentes, huisarts- en tandartspraktijken en vele anderen om ons bekend te maken. Ondertussen ook intakegesprekken gevoerd met heel veel potentiële hulpen aan dezelfde keukentafel. Na niet al te lange tijd konden we de eerste match maken!

Inmiddels is het werkgebied van Hulp Waar Nodig uitgebreid en hebben we ons gespecialiseerd in Personal Organizing voor senioren.

We houden erg van afwisseling dus zijn we ook Hulp Waar Nodig voor ZZP en MKB. Daar bouwen en ontwerpen we bijvoorbeeld websites en flyers voor.

Dienstverlening met een hart! 
Waar we na al die jaren nog voor staan en willen blijven staan is het persoonlijke, de betrokkenheid. We zijn en blijven regionaal. Zo blijven we ook onze klanten en hulpen kennen en kunnen we ook de juiste match maken.

Gespecialiseerd in hulp voor senioren!

Il s’agit donc de respecter chaque personne, quelles que soient ses difficultés en la considérant avant tout comme un Être exprimant ses désirs et besoins et pour qu’elle soit reconnue en tant que citoyen aux yeux de la société.

Description:

We also provide Short and Medium Term Accommodation for participants needing respite or awaiting long-term housing, offering safe, supportive environments aligned with their NDIS goals.

After working in remote and rural schools as a Special Education teacher and executive, Eisha recognised the lack of access to behaviour support and services for families of children with special needs in these locations. To address the situation, Eisha and the Lime Rainbow team work with those in remote and rural areas of Australia in some of the most isolated communities in the world such as the APY Lands in South Australia, Coober Pedy, Oodnadatta, Ceduna and more.
As ardent supporters of Disability, Diversity and Inclusion, Eisha and the Lime Rainbow team work with people of all ages and various needs such as autism, ADHD, sensory processing disorder, anger, Psycho Social and other behaviours, assisting their ability to thrive in home, school and social environments

Description:
Pakistan Alive People Association - PAPA, a non-governmental organization dedicated to empowering individuals with disabilities. 

Our founder, Rana Mudassar, is a visionary leader who, despite facing his own challenges with osteogenesis imperfect disease, has not let his disability define him. Instead, he has used his experiences to drive his passion for making a difference in the lives of others.

At PAPA, we believe that everyone, regardless of their abilities, has the right to live a fulfilling life and contribute to society. 

That's why our mission is to help in education, health services, and career opportunities to individuals with disabilities. 

We believe in creating an inclusive society where people with disabilities are given equal opportunities to succeed, and we are committed to raising their voices to fight for their rights.

Our education program focuses on providing special education services to individuals with disabilities to ensure they receive the best possible education. 

Our health program aims to provide medical services and support to individuals with disabilities, ensuring they receive the care they need to live a healthy life.

We understand that people with disabilities often face discrimination and have limited career options. 

That's why we are dedicated to providing digital marketing skills training to empower individuals with disabilities to participate in the digital world and pursue their career dreams.

We believe that everyone has the potential for self-growth and empowerment, and we aim to provide resources and support to help individuals with disabilities achieve their full potential.

At PAPA, we believe that with the right support and resources, people with disabilities can play a vital role in society and contribute to its growth. 

We invite you to join us on this journey and help us create a brighter future for individuals with disabilities in world. Together, we can make a difference.

Name: Harlawhill Day Care Centre
Description:
Harlawhill Day Care Centre | Prestonpans, East Lothian

Since 1992, Harlawhill has been at the heart of Prestonpans, supporting older people, many living with dementia, Parkinson’s, stroke or frailty, to stay independent, connected and confident in later life.

We are a Scottish Charitable Incorporated Organisation (SC020282) and open 50 weeks a year, welcoming up to 16 members each weekday. Our adapted minibus collects people from their doors and brings them safely home, removing transport barriers that often cause isolation.

Inside our homely Centre, members enjoy nourishing meals and evidence-based activities such as reminiscence music, gentle exercise, creative arts and day trips that spark joy. Our dementia-friendly reminiscence room, designed by our in-house Dementia Ambassador, helps reduce agitation and encourage positive memories.

Our impact goes beyond daily activities. Staff build trusted relationships that allow us to spot early health issues, link families with GPs and NHS partners, and reduce unnecessary hospital admissions. This approach can cut emergency admissions by up to 50 percent while easing pressure on family carers and other health professionals.

We also provide signposting to benefits advice, energy-cost support and other local charities, keeping households stronger for longer. In 2021 our small charity was officially recognised as Top Team of the year at the Scottish Health Awards.

Our vision: a community where growing older never means surrendering independence or social connection.
Our values: Dignity • Compassion • Community • Prevention first

We are now seeking forward-thinking partners and investors to help expand our reach, funding vital projects like a new specially adapted minibus and enhanced dementia therapies. Together we can prove that community care, early intervention and neighbourly solidarity keep independence alive.

Since 1975, Binder & Binder has stood as America's premier Social Security Disability advocates, championing the rights of disabled Americans nationwide. Founded by brothers Harry and Charles Binder in New York, our firm began with a simple yet powerful mission: to help deserving individuals navigate the often overwhelming Social Security Disability system and secure the benefits they've earned through years of work and contribution.

What started as a small practice has grown into the nation's leading Social Security Disability advocacy firm, with a presence in all 50 states and a dedicated team of advocates who share our founding commitment to compassionate, personalized representation.

Imagine a career where you change lives every day and directly impact families regaining stability, independence, and peace of mind. At Binder & Binder Social Security Disability Advocates, we fight for vulnerable clients, ensuring they receive the disability benefits they deserve. Our nationwide team blends expertise, compassion, and determination to guide clients through complex processes. If you’re driven to make a difference—whether as an advocate, SSDI case manager, or support specialist—join us to transform lives and build a rewarding career with a trusted leader in disability advocacy.

We give you:
•	Potential for career growth
•	Intensive training to perform a vital role
•	The stability of working with a long-standing organization
•	Friendly management that makes you smile
•	A chance to work with a close-knit team
•	Staff longevity with many with us over 10 years
•	A successful company, winning thousands of cases since 1975

To apply for a Client Service Associate role, please email a resume to: BDitta@binderandbinder.com. An EOE m/f/d/v.

Our Services

NDIS Support Services:

We offer a wide range of NDIS-funded services designed to empower people with disabilities:

- Personal Care & Daily Living – Assistance with personal hygiene, dressing, and mealtime support.
- Therapeutic Supports – Access to experienced therapists including physiotherapists, occupational therapists, and psychologists.
- Community Participation – Support to engage in social, recreational, and community-based activities.
- Employment & Skill Development – Help with finding and maintaining meaningful employment or further education.
- Supported Accommodation – Options including Supported Independent Living (SIL) and Short-Term Accommodation (respite).
- Assistive Technology – Provision and training in mobility aids, communication tools, and home modifications.
- Support Coordination – Helping you manage your NDIS budget and connect with trusted service providers.

Aged Care Support Services:

For older Australians, we provide caring and professional support to maintain independence and comfort at home or in residential settings:

- Home Care Services – Assistance with daily tasks like cleaning, cooking, and personal care.
- Allied Health Services – Access to health professionals such as physiotherapists and podiatrists to maintain wellbeing.
- Residential & Respite Care – Full-time residential care or temporary respite for families and carers.
- Dementia and Palliative Care – Sensitive, specialised care tailored to complex health needs.
- Transport & Social Support – Help with attending appointments, social outings, and staying connected with the community.

Name: A C McArthur Services
Description:
I am a one-woman show. 
A late diagnosed Autistic ADHD mother of two boys, one of which is also autistic. This is not up for debate, or discussion.

I also share common co-existing conditions that I quite often see cross my desk for those I work with - CPTSD, PMDD, OCD #iykyk

I'm abruptly honest about how difficult navigating life while living in a neurotypical world is, and how even more difficult navigating NDIS is - because why would they make it easy for their target cohort?

While my books are closed currently for ongoing support coordination, I can also help with:
- Access requests for NDIS
- Plan reassessments (from application, gathering reports, and sitting in those meetings)
- Questions about provider conduct and how to navigate a complaint
- Advice on neurodivergent experience within NDIS and supports

My long term goal is to become an advocate.
I am currently studying my Diploma in Community Services and my Cert IV Mental Health Peer Work to expand my knowledge in this space and offer more.

I am based in the Hunter Valley NSW, however I work Australia wide remotely.

I am also a loud, passionate survivor of sexual violence who has recently found her voice to share her story. The path of multiple assaults that began at the age of 4, and ended at the age of 22.

I was once conditioned to sit still, look pretty - be minimal, don't be too loud.

I'm done with that now - one day, I'll be that person on the stage where my boys can point, and proudly say "that's my mum".

Name: Odd Fellows Home Of California
Description:
A proud emblem of California's rich history and commitment to community care, the Odd Fellows Home of California stands as a beacon of dedication to the elderly and handicapped. OFHC, a California Public Benefit Nonprofit Corporation, proudly owns and oversees two premier Continuing Care Retirement Communities (CCRC) – the Saratoga Retirement Community and the Meadows of Napa Valley. 

Both communities radiate the values and ethos that the Odd Fellows, a historic fraternity rooted in community service, have upheld for centuries. The Saratoga Retirement Community, nestled amidst the scenic landscapes of Saratoga, and the Meadows of Napa Valley, enveloped in the picturesque vineyard regions, exemplify commitment to offering unparalleled care. 

Recognizing the importance of specialized care and community management, OFHC has enlisted the expertise of Pacific Retirement Services (PRS) to operate and manage these communities. Yet, the oversight and vision remain deeply connected to the roots, with the OFHC Board of Directors supervising PRS's functions.

Guided by our Articles of Incorporation, our mission isn't just an organizational mandate; it's a testament to our commitment. Established under the California Nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation Law, OFHC was formed with a charitable purpose. While the primary focus remains to serve the elderly and handicapped members of the Independent Order of the Odd Fellows, we open our doors to the broader public when space permits, extending our mission of care and service.

By fostering an environment that intertwines heritage with cutting-edge care facilities, OFHC is not just a home; it's a legacy of compassion, commitment, and community service.

Name: CuidadIA
Description:
English / Español
CuidadIA es el primer servicio de monitoreo diario de personas mayores basado en llamadas impulsadas con inteligencia artificial.
Mitiga la soledad no deseada en las personas mayores y chequea las acciones diarias para un estilo de vida saludable mediante llamadas diarias de control, reportando la conversación a los familiares encargados de los mayores o de las personas en situación de dependencia.
Realiza llamadas diarias para hacer un control de la persona dependiente o en soledad no deseada, seguidamente llama al familiar encargado para resumir la llamada con los puntos clave y a la vez manda un correo electrónico con la transcripción completa de lo hablado en la conversación.

CuidadIA is the first daily monitoring service for seniors based on artificial intelligence driven calls.
It mitigates unwanted loneliness in the elderly and checks daily actions for a healthy lifestyle through daily monitoring calls, reporting the conversation to the family members in charge of the elderly or dependent person.
It makes daily calls to check on the dependent person or unwanted loneliness, then calls the family member in charge to summarize the call with the key points and at the same time sends an email with the complete transcript of the conversation.

Name: REACHability Columbus
Description:
REACHability provides hands-on training for employment and/or volunteer opportunities for adults with disabilities. We believe that every individual has potential. Given the right tools and hands on training, adults with disabilities can enhance their quality of life and find meaningful employment and/or volunteer opportunities. Every person desires to have a sense of pride, involvement, and the ability to make a difference in their community; being born differently abled does not lessen these desires.

Our hope and desire is to build a positive relationship between employers and persons with disabilities by helping them both succeed. We will educate employers on the benefits of hiring persons with disabilities and train our participants to fulfill employment and/or volunteer roles. A successful outcome would enhance the quality of life for adults with disabilities and provide a positive hiring outlook among local employers. 

Participants can benefit from REACHability if they choose to put in the work. We expect participants to be involved in their job search process and to believe they have skills which are valuable to businesses.

Utilizing each participant's individualized employment strategy, we will work with their family, friends, and support team to help them REACH their full potential, goals and live their best life.

Name: PossAbilities
Description:
Mission
At PossAbilities, we create opportunities for children and adults with disabilities to contribute to and connect with the community.

Vision
People with disabilities are thriving, valued, and engaged community members. Our community is vibrant, inclusive, and advocates for all of its members. PossAbilities is an exceptional agency of caring leaders and experts providing innovative, person-centered supports and services for people with disabilities.

History
PossAbilities was founded in 1961 by three mothers in Olmsted County. The mothers were looking to create a better life for their children, who had developmental disabilities. Through their efforts, the Minnesota Legislature established nine pilot Day Activity Centers (DACs). One of the first pilot DACs opened in Rochester in 1962. At the time, the center was just structured for children with developmental disabilities to have community-based education, but within a year, programming was added to include adults with developmental disabilities.

The success and community support of the Day Activity Center in Rochester blossomed into what it is today, PossAbilities of Southern Minnesota.

Our Services include the following: 

Respite:
This is for clients desiring to have a temporary change of environment in the home of another family away from their main residence.

Living Support:
This is provided in group home setting and are supervised 24/7 by qualified support staff. They assist in goal setting, skill development, activities of daily living, and leisure.

Supportive Roommate:
This provides opportunity for clients to receive customized residential support services within family environment that fits the client's specific needs and lifestyle.

Adult Relief:
This service is offered on a planned or emergency basis and it is beneficial for families or caregivers who are desiring a temporary break from the routine of caregiving daily activities.

Community inclusion: 
This program allows clients to socialize and become an inclusive part of the larger community. The day program is designed to fit each client's specific goals.

Transition to adulthood support:
This program provides young adults with their preferred targeted services based on their envisioned goals. Additional services are provided to support young adults’ requirements to effectively transition into adulthood.  
Our values are summarized with the ICEA acronym- Inclusion, Client-centric, Empathy, and Accountability.

Name: Memory Keeper
Description:
Memory Keeper is a human-centered AI platform that helps elderly individuals preserve their life stories through guided digital interviews. It was created to address two challenges—memory decline and loss of family history. As people age, details of their past fade; their lessons, struggles, and triumphs are often lost. Memory Keeper keeps those stories alive.

Through a friendly, conversational interface, users engage in guided storytelling sessions with an empathetic AI interviewer. The AI asks thoughtful, personalized questions about childhood, work, love, challenges, and key life moments. Every response is recorded, transcribed, and transformed into beautifully written narratives that capture both facts and feelings.

Each story is organized chronologically and thematically, then curated by editors to maintain authenticity and emotional tone. Users can add photos, voice notes, or videos, and the system automatically matches them to the relevant memories. The result is a deeply personal biography—available digitally or as a printed keepsake book—preserving a legacy that families can treasure for generations.

Beyond storytelling, Memory Keeper functions as a “mental gym” for the elderly. Recalling memories and describing them in detail activates cognitive pathways, improving recall, attention, and emotional health. The process itself becomes a form of gentle brain exercise that strengthens mental resilience and combats loneliness.

Ultimately, Memory Keeper is more than an app—it’s a bridge between generations, transforming fading recollections into timeless life stories, ensuring no wisdom is ever lost to time.

Description:
FitStrong is an NDIS-registered provider specialising in inclusive personal training and movement programs for children, teens, and adults living with disability.

 What Makes FitStrong Different

NDIS-registered and 100% compliant with all quality and safeguarding standards.

Trainers with lived experience and additional training in inclusive fitness.

Flexible delivery — home, community, park, or gym.

Fast onboarding — participants can usually start within 1–2 weeks.

No lock-in contracts, no surprises.

 Who We Work With

NDIS Support Coordinators & Plan Managers

Allied Health teams (OTs, Physios, Psychs)

Families seeking safe, reliable fitness supports

 Our Mission
To create a truly inclusive fitness network where every participant — regardless of ability, age, or background — has access to quality, compassionate personal training that improves strength, confidence, and independence.
