 to learn how we can help your participants get moving safely and confidently.

Name: The Arc of Macomb County
Description:
The Arc of Macomb County is a 501(c)3 non-profit membership association composed of people with intellectual and developmental disabilities, their families, their friends, and the professionals involved in supporting them. The Arc was founded in 1953 by parents with a common concern. The goals of the founding members continue to be reflected in our mission statement:

The Arc of Macomb County exists to ensure that people with disabilities are valued and that they and their families can participate fully in, and contribute to, the life of their communities. We are committed to securing, for all people with disabilities, the opportunities to choose and realize their goals and dreams regarding where and how they learn, live, play, and work.

We embrace the vision that:

The Arc of Macomb County will be a center of excellence in supporting anyone associated with The Arc. We will work until we achieve a society in which every person with an intellectual or developmental disability is able to sustain their own experience of companionship, connectedness, and community. We envision that every person will be treated with dignity and respect. We will engender a sense of safety for all persons with disabilities by providing them with the emotional, mental, and financial skills necessary to achieve their dreams.

The Arc's emphasis is on community inclusion through employment, independent living, and community participation so that each person served is a valued member of the community and will be able to achieve their greatest potential. In keeping with this basic philosophy, The Arc offers person-centered services and supports regarding an individual's vocational, social, educational, and residential needs and desires.

Personalized activity plans that adapt to client needs

Guided prompts and scripts for easy, engaging implementation

Scalable tools that reduce caregiver workload and increase client satisfaction

We believe that aging well means living well—every day. Thrive Guides is here to make that possible.

Description:
About Us:

Senior Care of Sacramento is a premier geriatric care management firm dedicated to enhancing the quality of life for seniors and providing peace of mind to their families. Our founder, Therese Johnson, is a seasoned Gerontologist, educator, author, and advocate for eldercare, bringing a wealth of experience and passion to senior care.

Our Mission:

Our primary goal is to maintain the independence and in-home living of aging clients for as long as possible. When necessary, we offer free referrals to a network of personally vetted and approved Residential Care Facilities for the Elderly (RCFEs), Assisted Living Communities, and Board and Care homes. We are committed to delivering customized caregiving solutions tailored to the unique needs of each senior and their family.

Services We Provide:

Medical Assessment and Long-term Senior Care Planning
Medical Advocacy and Asset Protection
Veterans’ Benefits and Medi-Cal Consulting
Memory Care Consulting, Referrals, and Placement
In-Home Care Consulting & Referrals
Geriatric Care Management

Why Choose Us?

With a comprehensive approach to senior care, we assess medical, psycho/social, and living conditions to create a formal customized care plan. Our team serves as a liaison and advocate between physicians and families, ensuring that healthcare needs are met and providing expert referrals to a wide range of services. We are dedicated to optimizing the health and wellness of seniors, enabling them to live in dignity and enjoy their final years.

Get in Touch:

If you or a loved one are in need of compassionate and professional senior care services, do not hesitate to reach out. Call us at 916-877-6904 for a free consultation, or fill out our contact form to get started. We are here to provide the support, resources, and care that you and your family deserve.

Welcome to La Gratitude Newcastle, Northern Natal’s friendliest retirement and senior living community. Our priority is to uphold the rights of our residents at all times while providing a safe and secure environment for you to live your senior life gracefully.

At La Gratitude, we offer multiple care levels and facilities to choose from, ranging from independent living to frail care. Our Frail Care Facility provides specialized services that include Primary Care, Palliative Care, Respite Care and Rehabilitative Care for Dementia and Alzheimer’s. Our multi-disciplinary team continually assesses your health status to provide you with the most suitable care regime and development plan.

Enhance your retirement years with our comfortable accommodation options such as flats and cottages. We provide specialized medical care to ensure that you have everything you need to live comfortably. We also believe in staying socially responsible within the community and are actively involved in numerous outreach and awareness programmes in rural and urban areas throughout the Amajuba District.

Learn more about our professional staff members who dedicate their time to making La Gratitude a home for those in need. You can also read through a collection of newspaper articles regarding the events we host and hear what our happy residents have to say.

As a non-profit organization, we depend on contributions from people like you to continue our work. Please consider making a donation to support our cause. We are fully compliant with all the national legal requirements, and you can view copies of our registrations and certificates on our website.

Choose to live your best senior life possible at La Gratitude.

Our experience and dedicated team provide quality support to the NDIS participant in their own home and community for self-care activities, personal and domestic support, community participation, support coordination, community nursing, access to social and recreational activities and support for skill development. Name: Embracing Arms
Description:
Our Story:
Embracing Arms is founded by two sisters who want to help change the narrative around disability work. Despite being seen as a job to many, we see it as a privilege to help enrich the lives of each individual by empowering their abilities. This stems from our own personal experience of having two brothers who were diagnosed with autism and an intellectual disability. Navigating through the challenges of finding the right support services to cater to our brother’s needs, has helped us understand what is required in providing adequate care. Throughout the past 18 years, we have acquired a wealth of knowledge and have a desire to initiate change in this industry.

Our Mission:
At Embracing Arms, our focus is on building meaningful relationships with our participants and their families. We believe in creating a supportive environment where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered. We work closely with families, caregivers and healthcare professionals to develop customised care plans that promotes independence. At Embracing Arms, we are committed to making a difference. We are passionate about what we do, and we strive to make a positive impact on the lives of those that we serve.

Nossa jornada tem sido marcada por dedicação e excelência, construindo um ambiente acolhedor e carinhoso para os idosos que escolhem chamar a Residência Lar Carioca de "lar".

Na Residência Lar Carioca, valorizamos cada indivíduo e buscamos criar um ambiente onde todos se sintam respeitados, valorizados e cuidados. Oferecemos atividades enriquecedoras, cuidados de saúde personalizados e refeições balanceadas. 

Ao longo dos anos, construímos uma comunidade baseada no afeto, na compreensão e no apoio mútuo. Somos mais do que uma casa de repouso; somos uma família estendida. Continuaremos trabalhando incansavelmente para garantir que nossos residentes desfrutem de uma vida digna, confortável e repleta de alegria.

Description:
NexAble was founded by two friends who know firsthand what it's like to navigate a world that isn’t always built with disabled people in mind. One of us is a wheelchair user, the other is autistic — and both of us have spent years searching for technology that actually meets our needs. Tired of coming up short, we decided to create a solution ourselves.
 
At NexAble, we specialise in sourcing and supplying accessible tech products that make a real difference. From smart doorbells and deaf-friendly fire alarms to big-button phones and safety hob systems, every item in our collection has been carefully chosen for its reliability, ease of use, and positive impact on daily life. If we wouldn’t use it ourselves, we won’t stock it.
 
But NexAble is about more than just products — it’s about people. We’re a disabled-led company, and we’re proud to be part of a wider community pushing for greater inclusion, independence, and accessibility. We understand the challenges because we’ve lived them, and we’re committed to offering practical, empowering solutions that support safer, more connected lives.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Dara Employment
Description:
Partner with DARA Employment to Build a More Inclusive Workplace 

DARA Employment is a supported employment programme that helps people with disabilities access meaningful, long-term employment within their communities. We work directly with inclusive employers like you to ensure the recruitment process is simple, supported, and successful for everyone involved.

Our service is completely free of charge and designed to meet the specific needs of your business. We take the time to understand your organisation and match you with skilled, job-ready individuals who are eager to contribute. From the first interview to long-term retention, we’re here to support you every step of the way.

A dedicated Job Coach works alongside you and your team — helping identify suitable roles, assisting with training, and providing practical on-site support. We also offer disability awareness training to promote understanding and confidence among your staff. Support doesn’t end after hiring; we continue to provide assistance as long as needed to ensure a successful outcome for both the employee and your team.

Employers who partner with us often experience improved morale, increased productivity, and a more positive work culture. Inclusive hiring isn’t just the right thing to do — it also makes business sense. A diverse workforce fosters innovation, broadens your customer base, and reflects the values of today’s communities.

Financial supports are also available to employers hiring people with disabilities — and we’ll help you access and apply for them.

When you partner with DARA Employment, you’re not just hiring — you’re making a difference.

Contact us today to explore the possibilities.

Our offering spans four areas:
Comfort and Safety
Mobility
Emergency and Security
Assistance/ help at home

At the centre sits a trusted service brand that helps customers understand what they need, when they need it, and where to find it without having to piece things together alone. 

In October 2025, as a first move, we acquired Chiltern Care Chairs, a Buckinghamshire-based business focused on the first of those areas. We are currently in talks with a bathroom refit company and in 2026 we will build out our mobility offering. 

Our acquisition model is flexible and depends on the maturity of the business and the life stage of the owners. Our first acquisition was 100% buyout. Our second will be an initial minority investment leading to a staged buy out. The deal structure is less important than the group offering we are aiming to build.

Name: Your Therapy SA
Description:
Erika, a dedicated, compassionate and highly experienced physiotherapist, began her professional journey in 2008 after graduating from the University of South Australia. Throughout her career, she worked in various industries across South Australia, constantly striving to support individuals in leading fulfilling lives despite facing significant challenges. Erika's approach has always been rooted in kindness, support, and empathy, as she firmly believes in the power of these qualities to impact people's lives.

Erika's commitment to the field extended beyond her clinical practice. For several years, she served as a clinical educator at the University of South Australia, where she took great pleasure in guiding students and helping them become therapists. Erika focused on empowering students to recognize their strengths and contribute to shaping the future of therapy. Her passion for education and mentorship left a lasting impression on the aspiring therapists she worked with.
In addition to her teaching role, Erika worked many years as a physiotherapist at a prominent tertiary hospital in Adelaide. Her specialization revolved around complex discharge planning, particularly on NDIS clients who either acquired a disability or experienced a progression of their existing disability while in the hospital. Erika dedicated herself to assisting these individuals in transitioning from the hospital to the community, ensuring they received the necessary support to lead fulfilling lives despite challenging circumstances.

In 2021, Erika transitioned into working within the community, leveraging her expertise to provide compassionate and empathetic care to people with various disabilities. This marked the beginning of her business, initially known as Your Physio SA. As demand for her services grew, Erika expanded her practice to encompass a broader range of therapeutic disciplines, rebranding her business as Your Therapy SA.

Name: CarePatrol Scottsdale
Description:
Our mission at CarePatrol of Scottsdale is to help Seniors and Families obtain safer living options with a focus on, providing the best quality of life for their loved ones. 

Finding Independent Living, Assisted Living, or Memory Care options for Yourself or a loved one so they can "Age In Place" should not be a stressful or frustrating experience. 
As a family-run business, we help to streamline the placement process, reduce stress and are a FREE SERVICE to our clients (we are paid by our provider partners).

How Does the Process Work?
We start with a detailed Care Discovery Assessment to gain insight on our clients health history, current and future care needs, budget, hobbies, and any amenities they would look for in a new home. As the experts on all Care Homes and Communities within our territory, we are confident that our recommendations will provide the level of care, are within budget, meet most of the requested amenities and are within the desired location. 

All of the Care Providers (Care Homes & Communities) we contract with, have been personally interviewed by us and we always check the care and violation history of every provider we recommend.

We then schedule and join you in touring the selected Care Homes or Communities so you get a feel for the lifestyle, levels of care and can help ask the right questions. 

We Are Not Just About Placement. 
In addition to those services, we offer a wealth of industry resources including: Medical/Non-Medical Home Care, Hospice, VA Accredited Agents to help with Veterans benefits, Senior Real Estate & Moving Specialists, Elder Law Attorney's and Mobile Medical Equipment Resources. 

We truly are a one stop shop.

Nous faisons du bien-être de nos résidents notre priorité.

L'EHPAD Pierre Laroque est un établissement privé associatif à but non lucratif géré par l'ADPEP 66 (Association Départementale des Pupilles de l'Enseignement Public des Pyrénées-Orientales), organisation reconnue pour son engagement dans les secteurs médico-social et éducatif depuis plusieurs décennies. Notre siège social est situé à Toulouges.
Niché au cœur de la commune de Saint-Paul-de-Fenouillet, dans le magnifique territoire des Fenouillèdes, notre établissement accueille 50 résidents répartis comme suit :
35 résidents en hébergement traditionnel
15 personnes en unité protégée adaptée aux troubles cognitifs
6 personnes en accueil de jour
Notre établissement propose exclusivement des chambres individuelles équipées d’une salle d’eau avec douche à l’italienne, garantissant ainsi intimité, tranquillité et confort à chaque résident. L'architecture et les aménagements ont été spécialement conçus pour accueillir des personnes en situation de handicap, assurant une accessibilité optimale.
30% de nos chambres sont habilitées à recevoir des résidents au titre de l'aide sociale du Département des Pyrénées-Orientales, témoignant de notre engagement pour un accueil accessible à tous.

Name: Borealis Care (Registered NDIS Services Provider)
Description:
Borealis Care began with a simple idea: that support should feel human. Not transactional. Not rushed. Not shaped by red tape. At the heart of what we do is a belief that every person deserves to be seen, heard, and treated with dignity no matter where they come from or what challenges they face.

We exist to walk beside people as they build the life they want, in the way they want to live it. Whether that means help at home, support getting out into the community, or just someone they can rely on through daily routines we are there, quietly and consistently.

Our work is not about ticking boxes. It is about building trust over time. It is about showing up, not just when things are easy, but especially when they are not. It is about helping people move through life with confidence, knowing someone has their back. Our role is not to lead or direct it is to listen, understand, and support people to stand strong in their own choices.

We do not believe in one-size-fits-all solutions. We take time to know each individual their story, their rhythms, and what matters to them. From that place, we shape support that fits. Gently. Honestly. And always with respect.

Our team cares deeply about the work, the people we serve, and doing things the right way. We invest in relationships first, because real support is built on trust, not speed. Every conversation, every visit, every small moment matters.

At Borealis Care, we welcome people from all walks of life including those with lived experience, cultural understanding, or different paths to this work. What matters to us is your heart, your intent, and your willingness to walk beside others with care and consistency.

We are proud to serve communities across New South Wales and continue to grow without losing the heart that started it all. The journey is personal, and so is our care one person, one relationship, one day at a time.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Locus Services
Description:
As a dedicated Support Coordination provider, we assist individuals with disabilities in navigating the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) to maximize their plans and access the right services. Our goal is to empower participants by providing tailored guidance, ensuring they build capacity, exercise choice and control, and achieve their personal goals.  

We offer three key services:  

- Support Coordination – Helping participants understand and implement their NDIS plans, connect with service providers, and develop the skills to manage their supports independently.  
- Specialist Support Coordination – Providing expert assistance for individuals with complex needs, ensuring they receive specialized support to address barriers and achieve positive outcomes.  
- Recovery Coaching – Supporting participants with psychosocial disabilities by promoting resilience, self-management, and recovery-oriented approaches.  
Our team is committed to delivering person-centered, flexible support through both face-to-face and virtual options, ensuring accessibility and convenience for all Self-Managed and Plan-Managed participants.

Name: Il Sogno di una Cosa
Description:
Nasciamo nel 1982 come gruppo di operatori socialmente motivati, impegnati nella deospedalizzazione delle persone provenienti dai reparti dell’ex Ospedale psichiatrico di Collegno. 
In pochi anni, grazie alla legge Basaglia del 1978 e al lavoro con il professor Agostino Pirella, suo allievo e collaboratore, siamo riusciti a restituire alle persone che ci erano state affidate una dignità, una casa, un contesto sociale in cui vivere fuori dall’istituzione manicomiale. 
In quegli anni abbiamo realizzato sul territorio comunità alloggio, gruppi appartamento, servizi di autonomia, centri diurni, servizi progettati per favorire il superamento dell’ex O.P. e consentire agli ex degenti disabili di essere accolti in un clima familiare e dignitoso. 
Da quel momento ci siamo lanciati in nuove sfide, in diversi territori, investendo in un continuo percorso di ricerca nel campo socio-assistenziale ed educativo-culturale.
Oggi gestiamo complessivamente 13 servizi rivolti a persone con disabilità, in due diversi poli territoriali: Torino e hinterland e Valle di Susa.
Collaboriamo con quattro A.S.L. (TO1, TO2, TO3 e TO5), con il Comune di Torino, con i Consorzi Intercomunali di Nichelino (C.I.S.A. 12), Rivoli, Rosta, Villarbasse, Collegno e Grugliasco (C.O.S.) e della Valle di Susa (Con.I.S.A.).
Aderiamo alla LEGA delle Cooperative, al Consorzio di cooperative sociali N.A.O.S., al Fondo di mutualità integrativa sanitaria Solideo e alla Società di Mutuo Soccorso Solidea.
Siamo sede, nei nostri servizi, del Servizio Civile Universale.

Name: SEQ Disability Support
Description:

We address the frustration caused by a lack of communication and transparency by providing clear, consistent, and timely updates. Our focus is to ensure services align with participants' needs and proactively resolve any issues before they impact outcomes.

What Sets Us Apart?

Proactive Support Coordination: We stay actively engaged with participants and service providers, conducting regular follow-ups to keep services aligned with goals.

Tailored Plans & Continuous Monitoring: We carefully track and adjust plans to ensure they meet participants' evolving needs for the best outcomes.

Clear Communication & Timely Responses: We prioritize fast, direct communication to keep things on track and minimize delays.

Comprehensive Record Keeping: Our organized, up-to-date records ensure transparency and simplify budget approvals, compliance, and reporting.

Genuine Relationships: We foster supportive, hands-on relationships to ensure no detail is overlooked.

With my background in corporate business, experience working in an NDIS agency, and firsthand caregiving experience, I understand the struggles families and plan managers face. Partnering with us means working with a team that cares deeply about streamlining the NDIS process and making it more impactful for everyone involved.

Description:
Somos 𝗘𝗾𝘂𝗶𝗽𝗼 𝗩𝗲𝗿𝗶𝘁𝗮, la familia y la fuerza impulsora detrás de Vera! Vera es una niña con parálisis cerebral severa y profunda, y su vida es la inspiración de nuestra misión. Con una comunidad activa de más de un año en 𝗜𝗻𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗴𝗿𝗮𝗺 (@equipoverita) y 𝗧𝗶𝗸𝗧𝗼𝗸 (@equipoverita), ahora extendemos nuestra voz y acción a 𝗟𝗶𝗻𝗸𝗲𝗱𝗜𝗻.

Nuestra experiencia nos ha enseñado a transformar vivencias en acciones positivas. En esta plataforma, compartiremos la inspiradora trayectoria de Vera, celebraremos sus progresos y ofreceremos una perspectiva dinámica y real sobre la discapacidad y la accesibilidad. Buscamos ser un faro de información útil y motivación.

Nuestros objetivos principales en LinkedIn son,
 𝗔𝗣𝗢𝗬𝗔𝗥: Brindar herramientas y soluciones prácticas en accesibilidad a familias, personas con discapacidad y adultos mayores.
 𝗖𝗢𝗡𝗘𝗖𝗧𝗔𝗥: Fomentar una comunidad vibrante de colaboración, aprendizaje mutuo e intercambio de ideas con profesionales, organizaciones y todos los interesados.
 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗠𝗢𝗩𝗘𝗥: Impulsar activamente la inclusión real, los derechos, el diseño universal y el merecido reconocimiento a la labor de los cuidadores.
 𝗔𝗖𝗧𝗨𝗔𝗥: Establecer alianzas estratégicas para catalizar un cambio significativo y tangible en la sociedad.

Creemos en el poder de la comunidad y la acción informada. Queremos que este espacio sea un motor de cambio, donde las experiencias se conviertan en soluciones y las conexiones en oportunidades.

 𝙏𝙚 𝙞𝙣𝙫𝙞𝙩𝙖𝙢𝙤𝙨 𝙖 𝙨𝙚𝙜𝙪𝙞𝙧 𝙖 𝙀𝙦𝙪𝙞𝙥𝙤 𝙑𝙚𝙧𝙞𝙩𝙖. Conectemos, colaboremos y construyamos juntos un futuro donde la accesibilidad y la inclusión abran puertas a infinitas oportunidades para todos. Súmate a nuestro equipo e impulsemos juntos este movimiento!

Name: Elite Disability Care (NDIS Registered Provider)
Description:
At Elite Disability Care 
We have a passion for providing excellent NDIS Support for people of all ages and our aim is to provide you with the best possible service. 
We are very proud to to work with the NDIS and uphold the NDIS code of conduct. We will,
 
• Act with respect for individual rights to freedom of expression, self-determination, and decision-making in accordance with relevant laws and conventions
• Respecting the privacy of people with disability provide support and services in a safe and competent manner with care and skill.
 
• Act with integrity, honesty, and transparency
 
• Promptly take steps to raise and act on concerns about matters that might have an impact on the quality and safety of support provided to people with disabilities.
 
• Take all reasonable steps to prevent and respond to all forms of violence, exploitation, neglect, and abuse of people with disability.
 
• Take all reasonable steps to prevent and respond to sexual misconduct.

Support coordination: We can help individuals navigate the NDIS system, understand their funding, and access the services they need.

Transportation: We can provide transportation to appointments, errands, and social activities.

Medication reminders: We can help individuals remember to take their medications on time.

Sleepover support: We can provide overnight support for individuals who require assistance during the night.

High care support: We can provide specialized support for individuals with complex needs.

Skill development: We can help individuals develop new skills and abilities to improve their independence.

Community nursing care: We can provide nursing care in the community for individuals with medical needs.

Mentoring: We can provide guidance and support to help individuals achieve their goals.

Specialist disability accommodation (SDA) and supported independent living (SIL)

Name: Intervento Società Cooperativa Sociale
Description:
Il nostro obiettivo è migliorare la qualità della vita
delle persone in difficoltà,
e di conseguenza dei loro familiari,
facilitandone il rapporto con la società.
Siamo un’equipe di specialisti e da più di 30 anni seguiamo le più moderne teorie nell’ambito delle scienze mediche, psicologiche e dell’educazione applicandole alla nostra metodologia di intervento.

Ci occupiamo di persone con disabilità, difficoltà o disagio personalizzando il nostro approccio per ogni utente , in quanto crediamo che ogni patologia o disturbo debba essere affrontata a partire dalla persona che abbiamo di fronte: con il suo carattere, le sue abitudini, le sue capacità e le sue passioni.

La Famiglia è al centro di tutti i nostri interventi, in quanto sono le persone che quotidianamente stanno vicine a chi è in difficoltà che per prime, con i giusti suggerimenti ed indicazioni, possono facilitare la loro vita.

Aderiamo costantemente a programmi di ricerca e sviluppo, anche in collaborazione con istituzioni pubbliche o private per essere sempre aggiornati sulle nuove procedure e tecniche di lavoro.

La nostra attività ha lo scopo di:
Migliorare la qualità della vita delle persone in difficoltà
Supportare ed aiutare le famiglie sulla base dei loro bisogni, risorse e limiti
Diffondere la conoscenza acquisita negli anni attraverso seminari e corsi di formazione
Promuovere la cultura nella società nell’ambito della diversabilità
Siamo una cooperativa sociale, non a scopo di lucro, composta da operatori, genitori e volontari
iscritta all’Albo regionale delle cooperative sociali del Veneto al numero 401/VE/A e all’Albo Cooperative al numero A125699.

Wij verzorgen laagdrempelige beweeglessen op een stoel (en/of staand) van 45 minuten binnen uw verzorgingstehuis, zorginstelling, zorgvilla of woongroep in regio van Rotterdam, Gouda, Dordrecht, Den-Haag, ‘s-Hertogenbosch, Breda en Tilburg.

Onze lessen zijn persoonlijk, geheel op maat en worden gegeven door ervaren trainers. Onze lessen zijn zowel staand- als zittend op een stoel te volgen. De lessen worden op (bekende) muziek gegeven, wat extra stimulans en sfeer geeft.

Name: Brandenburg Liftsysteme
Description:
Brandenburg Liftsysteme steht für sichere, verlässliche und individuelle Lösungen rund um barrierefreie Mobilität in den eigenen vier Wänden. Unser Schwerpunkt liegt auf der Planung, Lieferung, Montage sowie Wartung und Reparatur von Treppenliften, Plattformliften, Senkrechtliften und weiteren Liftsystemen – immer mit dem Ziel, Menschen ein selbstbestimmtes Leben in ihrem vertrauten Zuhause zu ermöglichen.

Als herstellerunabhängiger, regional verwurzelter Fachbetrieb begleiten wir unsere Kundinnen und Kunden von der ersten Beratung bis weit über die Inbetriebnahme hinaus. Wir hören zu, analysieren sorgfältig die baulichen und persönlichen Gegebenheiten und entwickeln Lösungen, die technisch sauber, wirtschaftlich sinnvoll und langfristig tragfähig sind. Dabei übernehmen wir Verantwortung – für die Sicherheit der Nutzer, für die Qualität unserer Arbeit und für die Verlässlichkeit unserer Zusagen.

Was Brandenburg Liftsysteme ausmacht, ist die Verbindung aus technischer Kompetenz, ehrlichem Handwerk und echter Nähe zum Menschen. Unsere Monteure, Techniker und Berater arbeiten eng zusammen, denken ganzheitlich und handeln lösungsorientiert. Gerade bei anspruchsvollen Einbausituationen, individuellen Anforderungen oder besonderen baulichen Herausforderungen zeigt sich unsere Erfahrung und unser Qualitätsanspruch.

Unsere Mission ist klar: Mobilität darf keine Frage von Alter, körperlichen Einschränkungen oder baulichen Hürden sein. Wir schaffen Lösungen, die Sicherheit geben, den Alltag erleichtern und Lebensqualität zurückbringen. Dabei setzen wir auf transparente Beratung, klare Kommunikation und nachhaltige Beziehungen – zu unseren Kunden, Partnern und Mitarbeitenden.

Brandenburg Liftsysteme versteht sich nicht als Verkäufer von Technik, sondern als langfristiger Begleiter. Wir sind da, wenn es darauf ankommt – heute, morgen und in Zukunft. Denn im Mittelpunkt unseres Handelns steht immer der Mensch.

Name: MOLINS de MAR
Description:
“MOLINS DE MAR” és una Associació sense ànim de lucre que neix amb l’objectiu d’ajudar a totes aquelles persones que tinguin qualsevol tipus de diversitat funcional, als seus familiars i cuidadors, estudiants i persones amb rendes molt baixes, que puguin accedir a realitzar activitats relacionades amb el mar.

Es presenta com a un projecte de consciència, inclusiu, accessible, sostenible i educatiu. 
Les principals activitats de l’associació “MOLINS DE MAR” es centren en realitzar sortides al mar, ja sigui sortir a navegar, com recollir plàstics, banyar-se al mar, busseig i fotografia submarina, paddlesurf, ... i moltes més que s’aniran incorporant al catàleg d’activitats de l’associació.

Una part molt important de l’activitat de l’associació “MOLINS DE MAR” es centra en l’educació i la conscienciació sobre la necessitat de preservar els ecosistemes marins, assistint a xerrades, ponències, conferencies, museus i d’altres activitats relacionades amb l’activisme ecològic.

L’Associació “MOLINS DE MAR” compta amb el suport de Federacions oficials, empreses del sector nàutic i clubs esportius per a la realització de les seves activitats assegurant els principis de que totes aquestes activitats han de ser INCLUSIVES i ACCESSIBLES.

Com a activitats inclusives entenem que són totes aquelles activitats que pot realitzar QUALSEVOL PERSONA independentment de la seva condició física i/o intel·lectual. 
Com a activitats accessibles entenem que són totes aquelles activitats que pot realitzar QUALSEVOLPERSONA independentment de la seva condició econòmica, prioritzant a les persones amb major situació de vulnerabilitat, i sense excloure’n a aquelles amb majors rendes.

La prioritat de l’Associació “MOLINS DE MAR” és generar un entorn de cohesió social, per fomentar el creixement personal individual i col·lectiu i acompanyar a totes aquelles persones a realitzar activitats relacionades amb el mar, que per el motiu que sigui, fins ara, s’han vist privades de poder-les dur a terme.

Description:
Access, Inc is committed to supporting individuals with disabilities in reaching goals, according to personal needs and choices, at home, work, and in the community. We work tirelessly to live out this mission. Assistance and support from community members such as yourself is vital for us to continue to support individuals to reach their goals and dreams and to expand these
services.
Access, Inc assists individuals with disabilities to live in the most integrated setting appropriate to their needs and to be an active, included member of their community. These services are based upon the needs, choices, and desires of the individual served, allowing for them to gain greater independence and quality of life. Members are supported in their homes and
community through various modalities including our day habilitation and residential services.
Day Habilitation is based out of our Hampton office and currently serves forty individuals from Franklin and neighboring counties. Day Habilitation services take place Monday-Friday 8:30am-2:30pm. Members enjoy the various community activities, volunteer opportunities, and skill-building activities that they can choose from.
Access, Inc currently has six community-based living homes: two homes in Hardin County and four homes within Franklin County serving a total of twenty-four individuals. The members who live within these homes have staff support available to them seven days/week up to twenty-four hours/ day depending on their individual wants and needs. Many of these members also participate in our Day Habilitation program.
Our intermittent supported community living program currently serves twenty-six individuals within Franklin, Hardin, Wright, and Cerro Gordo counties. These services continue to fluctuate based upon need and location. These services are held within each member’s home or community. The service hours are based upon the need of the individual.

Name: AgingWELL Senior Home Safety
Description:
90% of seniors 65+ report they want to live in their own home, but for seniors to age in place, their physical environment.

AgingWELL SHS Certified Safety Specialists perform a room by room 240-point affordable home safety inspection, so you or your aging parent(s) can feel more confident and at ease living at home. 
We specialize in:
*240 -point senior home safety inspection 
*Fall prevention and customized safety plan
 *Alzheimer's/Dementia safety considerations
* Home safety toolkit-Delivered
*Crime prevention education 
*MEDICAL GUARDIAN- Emergency medical alert solutions 

90% of Seniors report they want to age in the comfort of their own home. 
Every 11 seconds, an older adult is treated in the emergency room for a fall. Falls are the leading cause of fatal injuries and the most common cause of non-fatal trauma-related hospital admissions among older adults. (hip fractures and head trauma). AgingWELL Senior Home safety will uncover all areas of concerns and make on-the-spot safety modifications during our walk-thru. After our safety assessment is complete, we will consult with each client (and their family members) to review , offer recommendations and create a customized plan for larger projects and suggestions for assistive devices and home safety hardware. 

Having an annual safety home evaluation is one of the most important things you will ever do if you plan to age in place!  Our Senior Home Safety Inspection can make a tremendous difference in reducing fall risks and the quality of life for our seniors. 
 
Safety for seniors with Alzheimer's/Dementia:
People with dementia are four to five times more likely to fall than older people who do not have a cognitive impairment. If you're caring for a loved one with Alzheimer's/Dementia, we can help assist the family with safety precautions and fall prevention considerations. 

For more information and to request a safety assessment quote, visit: www.agingwellseniorhomesafety.com

Name: Adelant
Description:
Verandermanagement
Wil je jouw verandertraject tot een goed einde brengen? Zien jouw medewerkers niet het belang in van verandering? Zijn je medewerkers verandermoe? Of ervaar je weerstand tot veranderen bij je medewerkers? Alleen als je draagvlak hebt, kun je succesvol en blijvend veranderen.

Adelant biedt een gestructureerde aanpak voor verandermanagement. Hierbij houdt Adelant een passende balans tussen de resultaatkant en de menselijke kant van de verandering.

Ik begeleid bestuurders, managers en teams bij complexe transities in de zorg. Een verandering succesvol implementeren doe ik door aandacht te besteden aan zowel de boven- als de onderstroom van een verandering. In de bovenstroom werk ik aan de hand van een helder veranderplan, denk aan de visie, strategie, doelstellingen, te behalen resultaten, duidelijke verantwoordelijkheden en actieplannen en werk ik samen met degenen die hier verantwoordelijk voor zijn.

In de onderstroom heb ik aandacht voor de minder zichtbare facetten van de organisatie en de verandering. Denk aan cultuur, (team)dynamiek, commitment, weerstand en sfeer. Door aandacht te hebben voor dat wat er speelt, problemen op tafel te krijgen en bespreekbaar te maken, kan ik samen met de organisatie zoeken naar oplossingen. Tijd en aandacht voor de menselijke kant van veranderen, is essentieel om een verandering succesvol en blijvend te implementeren. Mijn werkwijze kenmerkt zich door het voortdurend schakelen tussen doelgericht en relatiegericht handelen (balans van mijn talent resultaatgerichtheid en mijn verbindende kant).

Wil je meer weten over mijn werkwijze, dan sta ik je graag te woord (06-50562898).

We’re not a home care agency. We don’t employ caregivers—we empower families who do.

Whether you’re planning for long-term care or seeking immediate support, Ethos offers a simplified, personalized approach to caregiving at home.

Caring for Seniors, Supporting Families, Building Community

At Team Senior Referral Services, we are committed to guiding families through one of the most significant transitions in life—finding the right care and support for their aging loved ones. As Southern Oregon’s trusted senior care advisors, we provide compassionate, personalized assistance to help families navigate the complexities of senior living options, from assisted living and memory care to independent living and in-home care services.

Description:
We started Aging with Grace Housing Initiative due to an understanding and commitment to supporting low-income seniors, understanding that the challenges of aging are not just physical, but emotional and mental as well. We believe that every senior deserves a home that not only meets their physical needs but also provides a strong sense of community and emotional support.

Through our communities, we offer more than just a place to live, we provide an environment where seniors can truly thrive. We know how important it is for older adults to feel connected and engaged, and that’s why we offer therapy sessions, support groups, and activities designed to keep the mind active and spirits high. By focusing on creating a warm, welcoming space for seniors, we hope to give them not only the comfort of a home, but the confidence that they’re living in a place that supports their well-being. Name: Audible Traders, Inc
Description:
Audible Traders is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit making financial markets accessible through patented audio technology.

Our innovation, MaMoo, converts stock market data into musical notation in real-time—enabling blind individuals to trade professionally while creating valuable tools for all traders.

 THE PROBLEM:
700,000 blind Americans are excluded from financial markets. Not because they lack capability—because every trading platform assumes you can see.

 THE SOLUTION:
MaMoo transforms market data into sound. Prices become melodies. Patterns become rhythms. Trading becomes something you can hear, not just see.

 OUR PROGRAM:
We're launching the world's first trading education program for blind musicians (Q2 2026). Five weeks, 15-20 participants, professional instruction, zero financial risk, 100% free.

 THE IMPACT:
When we design for blind users with sophisticated audio requirements, we create better products for everyone—sighted traders seeking an auditory edge, aging investors with declining vision, and anyone who processes information better through sound.

Founded: September 2025
Patent: U.S. Patent No. 11922501
Founder: Anthony T. McCabe Sr.
Website: audibletraders.org
Technology: mamoo.io

Economic opportunity doesn't require sight. It requires skill.

Name: HAPPY CARE DISABILITY SERVICES
Description:
Happy Care | Your Partner in NDIS Success

Navigating the NDIS isn’t easy, and at Happy Care, we keep things simple and focused. We take on the complexities, so you can focus on what matters: living your life your way.


 What We Do 



1 Therapy Services


Our team includes licensed psychologists, speech therapists, occupational therapists, dieticians, physiotherapists, and counselors, each dedicated to delivering support that’s practical, effective, and fully covered by the NDIS. Sessions are tailored to fit specific needs—whether it’s communication, mobility, mental health, or daily skills.



2 Respite Homes and Care


Caregiving takes time and energy. We offer respite options for short-term stays, day care, and overnight support. This means loved ones receive quality care in a safe, comfortable environment while caregivers get the time they need.



3 Support Coordination


NDIS applications, plan reviews, and provider connections can be overwhelming. Our experienced coordinators help make sense of the system, so you can use your plan to its fullest.



4 Group Activities


Building skills and social connections is important, so we provide activities in art, fitness, life skills, music, and more, all in inclusive settings for all ages and abilities.



 Our Approach


Our approach is straightforward—each service is designed to address real-life challenges, improve quality of life, and give clients practical tools to achieve their goals. We get to know each client in an initial free consultation and create plans that fit their specific situation and needs.


 Why Choose Us


Happy Care was founded by people who understand the NDIS landscape inside and out, and we’ve brought together a team dedicated to offering consistent, practical support. Our team works in close coordination with each client and family member, ensuring that support is tailored to fit their NDIS goals and day-to-day life.


 Reach out today

Name: Socialgood Social Enterprise
Description:
Social Good Enterprise Co., Ltd. is a certified social enterprise in Thailand committed to creating sustainable employment and economic opportunities for people with disabilities.

Founded in 2013 with a registered capital of THB 5,000,000, the company bridges corporate compliance with disability employment law  of the Persons with Disabilities Empowerment Act) and meaningful social impact.

 Chief Executive Officer (CEO): Ms. Tanwarin Jantrprapangkul

 Our Mission

“Empowering persons with disabilities through skills, jobs, and dignity – while helping organizations turn compliance into impact.”

We believe that persons with disabilities are not just beneficiaries but valuable contributors to society. Our mission is to transform mandatory corporate spending into opportunities that generate measurable social value (SROI), strengthen corporate reputation, and foster inclusive communities.

 What We Do
• Employment Solutions: End-to-end services for companies to comply with disability employment law through vocational training and employment projects.
• CSR & Tax Benefits: Projects that enable organizations to gain tax deductions while achieving genuine CSR/ESG results.
•	Social Enterprise Products: Souvenirs, premium gifts, and corporate merchandise produced by people with disabilities.
• Training & Skill Development: Certified vocational training programs that enhance employability and income for persons with disabilities.
• Upcoming Platform Development: Building a nationwide digital commerce platform to promote and sell products made by persons with disabilities.
• Future Workforce Collaboration: Seeking partners to train and employ persons with disabilities in Data Labeling, AI Training, and Healthcare AI Support — empowering them to contribute to healthcare data management and medical AI solutions.

 Our Partners
Trusted by leading organizations across industries, including:
IBM Thailand, TQM Insurance Broker, Sangfah Construction, Yuasa Battery Thail

Name: UniqueAble
Description:
UniqueAble is a social impact e-commerce marketplace that bridges artisans with disabilities from India and Indonesia to conscious consumers in Germany. We showcase and sell handcrafted, ethically-sourced products while empowering artisans through fair trade practices and sustainable income opportunities.

Our platform distinguishes itself through:

1. Direct Impact: An ethical profit-sharing model that ensures 30% of margins go directly back to artisans, creating sustainable livelihoods and economic independence.

2. Artisan Partnership: We partner with over 50 skilled artisans, providing them not just a marketplace, but also training, quality control support, and market insights to help them create products that resonate with German consumers.

3. Product Authenticity: Each product tells a story - of traditional craftsmanship, personal triumph, and cultural heritage. Our artisans specialize in handwoven textiles, sustainable home decor, and traditional craft pieces.

4. Tech-Enabled Impact: Our platform leverages data analytics to optimize product-market fit, while our transparent supply chain ensures customers can trace their purchase's journey and impact.

5. Market Access: We've successfully reached over 500 German consumers who value both product quality and social impact, creating a sustainable bridge between artisan communities and conscious consumers.

Through UniqueAble, we're not just selling products; we're cultivating a marketplace that celebrates ability, creativity, and economic inclusion. Our mission is to transform the narrative around disability through economic empowerment and sustainable commerce.

Description:
Nous sommes une association de consultance pour l’amélioration de la participation sociale des personnes qui vivent des situations de handicap et des restrictions de leurs droits.
Nous sommes une association sans but lucratif qui a pour objectif de promouvoir les droits des personnes qui vivent des situations de handicap.
Nos actions :
La formation des professionnels de l’accompagnement des personnes en situations de handicap
Le soutien-conseil pour améliorer les interventions afin que la personne puisse être conceptrice et actrice des projets qui la concerne
Nos actions s’inscrivent dans le cadre de la mise en œuvre de la Convention relative aux Droits des Personnes Handicapées -CDPH- adoptée en 2006 par les Nations Unies et ratifiée par plus de 180 pays
Nos activités se déroulent en Europe, en Afrique et au Québec
GRAVIR est également le Réseau Belge sur le Processus de Production du Handicap (RBPPH) et, à ce titre, un partenaire du Réseau International sur le Processus de Production du Handicap (RIPPH) dont le siège est à Québec. Le Modèle de Développement Humain et du Processus de Production du Handicap (MDH-PPH) est le cadre conceptuel sur lequel s’appuie nos activités ainsi que la Convention relative aux droits des personnes handicapées (ONU 2006) ratifiée par la Belgique le 2 juillet 2009.
Nos valeurs : 
L’accessibilité  
L’égalité des chances
La non-discrimination
L’égalité entre les hommes et les femmes
La participation et l’intégration pleines et effectives à la société
Le respect de la différence et l’acceptation des personnes handicapées comme faisant partie de la diversité humaine et de l’humanité
Le respect de la dignité intrinsèque, de l’autonomie individuelle, y compris la liberté de faire ses propres choix, et de l’indépendance des personnes
Le respect du développement des capacités de l’enfant handicapé et le respect du droit des enfants handicapés à préserver leur identité

Name: Visionary Independence Program
Description:
Welcome to Visionary Independence Program, a trusted family-owned agency with over two decades of unwavering commitment to enhancing the lives of individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities.  At Visionary Independence, we are not just an agency; we are a community-driven force committed to empowering lives through Individualized Living Support (ILS) and Supported Living Services (SLS). Our philosophy revolves around recognizing the unique potential within each individual, and our dedicated team is passionate about fostering an environment where abilities flourish.

With a focus on person-centered care, our programs are tailored to meet the specific needs and aspirations of those we serve. At the heart of our mission is a commitment to enhancing the quality of life for individuals with developmental disabilities by promoting self-determination, community integration, and skill development.

What sets Visionary Independence apart is our unwavering dedication to creating a supportive, inclusive, and compassionate space. Our team of skilled professionals is here not only to provide assistance but to collaborate with individuals, their families, and the community, ensuring a holistic and empowering approach to independent living. Our rich history is a testament to the relationships we've built and the positive impact we've made on countless lives.

Join us on this transformative journey, where Visionary Independence Program becomes a partner in realizing dreams, celebrating achievements, and fostering a future filled with possibilities. Together, we're building a community where everyone, regardless of ability, can thrive and lead a life of purpose and fulfillment.

Name: Sky's the Limit Disability Services
Description:
At STLDS, we are dedicated to providing personalised support to individuals living with disabilities across SA. Our mission is to empower each person we serve by offering tailored services that enhance their independence, wellbeing, and quality of life.
With a commitment to inclusivity and respect, we deliver a range of support options designed to meet the unique needs and goals of every individual. Whether through assistance with daily living, access to community activities, or support in achieving personal goals, our team is here to make a positive impact.
Our services include:
Personal Support: Tailored assistance with daily tasks to enhance independence.
Community Participation: Encouraging engagement in social, recreational and community activities.
Skill Development: Providing opportunities for learning and growth to build new skills.
Advocacy and Guidance: Offering support and advice to navigate services and resources.
As a leader in disability support, STLDS is proud to be at the forefront of accessibility and inclusion. We work closely with families, caregivers, and other stakeholders to ensure that our services are effective, empathetic and aligned with the best practices.
Our team is led by Tim Haggis, a dynamic leader and social entrepreneur with over 20 years of experience in customer service and disability support. Tim, who is legally blind, uses his personal and professional experience to drive positive change and advocate for improved accessibility and inclusion.
We are always looking to connect with like-minded individuals and organisations in the disability sector who share our values of integrity, inclusivity and social impact. If you are passionate about making a difference and want to collaborate on creating a more inclusive future, we invite you to connect with us.
Discover how STLDS can help you or your loved ones achieve their fullest potential. Together, we can create a world where everyone has the opportunity to thrive.

Name: McKenzie Care
Description:
McKenzie Care is a values-driven NDIS provider supporting individuals and families across Ipswich and Toowoomba. Founded by Melinda McKenzie, who brings over 13 years of experience with UnitingCare (Blue Care), McKenzie Care has a strong foundation in disability and aged care services and is committed to providing consistent, person-centred support.

Having delivered support under the QCSS program for the past two years, McKenzie Care is now expanding into NDIS services - transitioning existing clients and welcoming new participants. The team offers support coordination and frontline services, including:

- Community access
- Personal care
- Daily living assistance
- Light household support

Our focus is on supporting plan-managed and self-managed NDIS participants with high-quality, reliable care from a team who truly understands the local community.

McKenzie Care is also home to McKenzie Clean, a separate brand offering professional domestic cleaning services across Brisbane, the Gold Coast, Sunshine Coast, Toowoomba, Gatton, and the Lockyer Valley. These services are delivered by a team of reliable, often Cert III-qualified cleaners and support workers.

In early 2026, McKenzie Care will launch YellowTree Respite Farm - a wheelchair-accessible day program and short-term respite service on a working farm near Rosewood. The farm will provide enriching, hands-on experiences for NDIS participants, from animal encounters to life skills like cooking and gardening. It’s just one more way we’re putting our values into practice by creating joyful, inclusive experiences that support growth and independence.

At McKenzie Care, we believe in Real Support. Life Done Well.

Based in Rosewood, Queensland
For referrals or collaboration opportunities, message us here on LinkedIn or email: support@mckenziecare.com.au.

• Assistance with Daily Activities and Personal Care: Personalized support with essential tasks, ensuring comfort and well-being.
• Short and Medium-Term Accommodation (STA): Safe, welcoming housing solutions designed to foster growth and self-sufficiency.
• Social and Recreational Activities: Helping participants connect socially, build friendships, and engage in community life.
• Complex Care: Specialized services for individuals with high support needs, including clinical care and comprehensive health management plans.
Our approach to care goes beyond the basics. We respect each individual’s choices and promote dignity in every service we provide. By tailoring our supports to align with personal goals, we empower participants to lead more independent, rewarding lives. If you’re looking for a supportive, dedicated team ready to make a positive difference, we’re here to help.

Name: Independent Support Worker Network of Ontario
Description:
We are a non-profit organization for independent support workers (ISWs)/private support workers who are self-employed and often hired through individualized funding in developmental services (Passport, Special Services at Home) or other individual funding sources to support community participation, life skills development, person-directed planning, or respite services for children youth and adults labelled with a developmental, cognitive, physical disability or Autism.
For the ISW, we offer networking, mentorship, and educational opportunities to develop best practices and provide meaningful and quality support for those who hire them.
We help new and existing ISWs explore how to create service agreements, consider liability, insurance, and taxes, and create safe, person-centred, and person-directed services as self-employed ISWs.  We also help navigate the confusion between being an "independent contract worker (self-employed ISW) or an employee of a person/family as per the Canada Revenue Agency guidelines through available resources.
ISWs may work a few hours a week in this role or have many contracts with various individuals that make up a full-time workload. Regardless of how much the ISW works in self-employment, we have information that can help.

Description:
Understandings - Your Guide to Optimising NDIS Funds and Inclusion in Education

At Understandings, we are dedicated to helping individuals and families navigate the complexities of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) with confidence. Founded by Catherine O’Farrell, a highly regarded specialist in NDIS, education, and community services, our mission is to bridge the gap between NDIS funding and its application in educational and community settings.

With years of experience in senior roles at the NDIS, including positions such as Senior Planner for the Complex Support Needs Pathway, Catherine leads our team with a deep understanding of the NDIS framework. We use this expertise to ensure our clients receive the best possible outcomes, whether it’s accessing an NDIS plan, preparing for planning meetings, or securing additional support for educational inclusion.

Our services include guiding you through NDIS access, planning meetings, and reviews, with a focus on ensuring your plan and funds management strategy align with your individual needs and goals. We also specialise in assisting parents to understand how NDIS funds can be used to support disability inclusion in the education sector.

At Understandings, we pride ourselves on building awareness and connecting our clients with the best NDIS outcomes. We work closely with parents, support coordinators, educators, community service providers, and businesses to create inclusive environments where individuals with disabilities can thrive.

Let us help you unlock the full potential of your NDIS plan and support your journey towards greater inclusion and empowerment.

Name: Zoe Phillips Plan Management
Description:
Hi, Zoe here!
A little about me… I’ve been in the financial sector for a couple of decades now and established my business, Zoe Phillips Bookkeeping back in 2019. 
Since then, I've been working directly with local businesses to help them gain control of their finances and to help their businesses to thrive.

I've always been passionate about community and noticed that dedicated, personalised NDIS plan management services were significantly lacking in our area. This is why I embarked on our new venture - to contribute more to our community and to support the thriving of all our members, not just businesses.

We're Dubbo NSW locals through and through, deeply connected to our community. Our mission?... To assist folks right here in our area, including those in the nearby regions who rely on NDIS support. 

We offer that personal touch with plan management that you won't find elsewhere, and when you need us, we're right here for face-to-face chats. No middlemen here – just genuine local support, tailored to our community's needs.

Just like the major plan management agencies, we handle everything from managing funding allocations to processing claims and paying invoices, so you can focus on living your best life. Utilising NDIS-approved software, we provide you with live access to approve your requests anytime, ensuring a fast turnaround time of no more than 48 hours. With us, you retain full control over your NDIS plan, empowering you to make informed decisions about your support and services.

When you choose us, you get a local partner dedicated to your success. We work closely with you to manage your NDIS plan efficiently and effectively. With us by your side, you can confidently navigate the NDIS, knowing we're just a drive, walk, or phone call away, ready to assist you every step of the way!

Zoe

Name: Empowering Ability Journey
Description:
Empowering Ability Journey offers a range of support services to individuals living with a disability. Our professional teams strive to empower individuals and foster independence through our services and resources. Our vision is to see you reach your full potentials and achieve your goals.  

Our founders, Kawala and Nhi, are experienced Registered Nurses who have dedicated a combined total of over 10 years working in hospitals. Throughout their time working in hospitals, they have witnessed the challenges faced by patients when trying to find a suitable NDIS Provider to assist them with nursing care at home after discharge. They were deeply concerned by the negative impact on patients' well-being and quality of life, as many were forced to remain in the hospital longer than necessary.

Motivated by their observations and driven by their shared vision, Kawala and Nhi recognized the urgent need for a dedicated NDIS Provider that could provide comprehensive and personalized nursing care to NDIS participants. They envisioned a service that would leverage their expertise, skills, and nursing knowledge to provide support to participants within the comfort of their own homes.
 
With a deep understanding of the complex needs of patients transitioning from hospital to home, Kawala and Nhi founded Empowering Ability Journey. They set out to create a compassionate and inclusive environment that empowers participants, fosters independence, and enriches quality of life for every participant in their lifelong journey.

Name: Fondazione Pietro Zoncada Onlus
Description:
La Fondazione Ing. Pietro Zoncada Onlus si impegna da circa 15 anni per soddisfare le esigenze delle persone anziane. Attivo dal 2003, l'ente valuta accuratamente le esigenze di ogni anziano per offrire la soluzione abitativa più adatta alle loro esigenze in base a condizioni di salute, location della struttura, budget disponibile ed eventuali preferenze. 
La fondazione con sede a Borghetto Lodigiano garantisce la massima disponibilità e professionalità offrendo alloggi adeguati e con assistenza sanitaria anche notturna
Ogni residenza per anziani in provincia di Lodi proposta da Fondazione Ing. P. Zoncada Onlus e' in grado di offrire un'assistenza su piu' livelli per supportare gli anziani nel corso delle attivita' quotidiane e soddisfare le loro esigenze mediche e riabilitative.
Assistenza di base: supporto per l'igiene personale quotidiana e il bagno settimanale, cura di abbigliamento e dieta, pulizia degli ambienti, attivita' di mobilizzazione, animazioni e socializzazione, assistenza 24 ore. 
Servizio medico: presso la residenza per anziani e' a disposizione un medico dell'ente e viene garantita reperibilita' 24 ore per urgenze.
Assistenza medica specialistica: la Fondazione mette a disposizione numerosi specialisti come fisiatra, geriatra, psicologo, chirurgo e urologo. Direttamente presso la residenza per anziani si possono inoltre eseguire esami diagnostici strumentali quali elettrocardiogramma, ecografia internistica, ecocolordoppler.
Infermeria
Riabilitazione: 54 ore di terapia settimanali tenute da professionisti accreditati secondo il programma prescritto dal medico fisiatra dell'ente.
Locali attrezzati al recupero funzionale: presenti all'interno della residenza per anziani in provincia di Lodi
Servizio ricreativo e occupazionale.

Description:
Feet on the Ground Care Services: Compassionate Elder Care Solutions

About Us
Feet on the Ground Care Services specializes in bridging the gap for families caring for elderly loved ones from afar. With expertise in social work and elder care, our mission is to ensure seniors live their golden years with dignity, comfort, and happiness.

What We Offer
- Attending medical appointments for effective communication and coordination.
- Assistance with advanced directives, ensuring seniors' wishes are respected.
- Regular well-checks for ongoing health and wellness monitoring.
- Liaison services for out-of-town families, maintaining strong connections.
- Comprehensive case management post-hospital discharge.
- Financial management and oversight for seniors’ financial well-being.
- Placement assistance and resource connection for living arrangements, estate planning, and Medicare.

Our Commitment
We are committed to personalized, respectful, and professional care. Our team is dedicated to making a positive impact in the lives of seniors and providing peace of mind to their families, regardless of the distance.

Name: Joseph Gerard Foundation
Description:
Established in 1989, our organization offers accommodation for 36 elderly individuals in a densely populated area of Gauteng. Recognizing the lack of services for early childhood development in Alexandra, we began our preschool program in 1992 to provide a safe learning environment for children. Despite government subsidies, additional funding is necessary to sustain our efforts.

Our mission is to safeguard the rights and enhance the quality of life for children, the elderly, and the impoverished, tailored to their specific needs. We prioritize aiding the most vulnerable, including abandoned, neglected, and deprived individuals, to help them live with dignity.

For our Home for the Elderly, our objectives are to provide round-the-clock care, holistic support services, psychosocial assistance, and advocacy for improved care services and continuity. We also empower elderly residents for self-advocacy.

In our Pre-School/Crèche established in 1989, we accommodate 150 children from Alexandra and its environs, addressing the community's needs. St. Martin's Preschool in Wynberg and Alexandra seeks financial aid for operational costs, programs, and activities to ensure its smooth functioning.

Our preschool objectives include ensuring children's safety, providing early education, offering family support services, and advocating for educational resources and care access.

Name: COMPARTIM
Description:
En COMPARTIM somos un centro especializado en el acompañamiento y tratamiento de personas con Trastorno del Espectro Autista (TEA), Trastorno por Déficit de Atención e Hiperactividad (TDAH), atención temprana e integración sensorial. Nos dedicamos a proporcionar un espacio seguro y de apoyo donde niñas, niños, adolescentes y sus familias puedan desarrollarse, crecer y progresar.

En COMPARTIM creemos que el progreso se logra a través del compartir. Por ello, fomentamos una colaboración estrecha y constante entre profesionales, familias y todas las personas que participan en el proceso terapéutico. Sabemos que cada persona es única, y trabajamos de manera personalizada para atender sus necesidades específicas, siempre respetando su ritmo y proceso individual.

Nuestro enfoque es integral y cercano, y busca no solo abordar los desafíos en el aprendizaje y desarrollo, sino también promover el bienestar emocional, social y físico de quienes acompañamos. Fomentamos la participación activa de cada persona en su proceso, creando un entorno donde puedan sentirse valoradas, comprendidas e incluidas. La convivencia y la interacción son esenciales para el crecimiento, por lo que promovemos espacios de integración donde se respetan las diferencias y se potencian las fortalezas de cada individu@.

Name: PathLink
Description:
PathLink is committed to enriching and fulfilling the lives of adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD) in our community. Our vision is to grow into a financially sound organization that provides exceptional person-centered care.

The lack of ethical, compassionate HCBS providers creates a gap into which PathLink aims to step. The 200,000+ individuals living with IDD in Ohio deserve caring, high-quality services, but some agencies prioritize profits over people. PathLink was founded to remedy this problem. We believe that every individual deserves to lead a rewarding life surrounded by people who genuinely care about their well-being. Our goal is to empower individuals through respect, community and innovation.

By focusing on honesty, integrity and doing what is right, PathLink creates an experience where individuals feel valued and cared for. When given the choice between 125 providers, many families choose PathLink because of our stellar reputation and commitment to excellence. As we grow, PathLink will work to address the systemic issues that prevent many individuals from receiving high-quality care. Our vision is a future where every person has access to ethical, compassionate services. By starting in Stark County, PathLink aims to ignite wider change across the state of Ohio and beyond.

It aims to provide a New Model of Homecare Services for the frail elders by integrating technology. The Project provides services to the elders such as "Tele-Health ", "Tele-Rehabilitation ", "Online Activities", "Health & Entertainment Videos", and "Smart Schedule", etc. through a self-developed tablet application. 

The App has been used for over 191,000 hours by more than 2000 frail elderly users aged 81 on average in Hong Kong, which helping elders improve health habits and social connectedness. On average, our elderly users measured their vital sign 1.7 times per day, performed 0.7 exercises per day and underwent 16 minutes of cognitive training per day.

Name: KC Independent Support Coordinator
Description:
With more than 15 years’ experience across disability, aged care, and community services, I’ve built my career on one clear belief:

The NDIS should work for participants, not against them!

As a Level 3 qualified Support Coordinator, I specialise in complex cases and situations that my participants often find overwhelming. I've worked as a provider in frontline roles through to senior leadership and executive positions, and have seen how easily things can fall through the cracks. I use that insight to bring clarity, structure, and practical solutions that deliver real results for participants.

Alongside this professional experience, I also bring my own lived experience navigating the NDIS. I know what it feels like to face red tape, to hit barriers, and to need someone who genuinely listens. That perspective fuels my passion for this work and drives me to make sure every participant feels heard, respected, and supported.

My expertise lies in: 
 Complex case management — untangling challenges, fixing what others can’t 
 Goal-focused planning — keeping participant needs at the centre of every decision 
 Detail & accountability — committed to getting things right

I don’t just “coordinate supports.” I partner with participants to cut through the noise, fix what’s broken, and create lasting change. My experience is backed with persistence, energy, and genuine care for those I'm supporting.

My goal is simple: to make the NDIS work for you, every step of the way.

Name: DivyanGO: Move Freely
Description:
DivyanGO, is an innovative platform offering the information and services for differently abled users to live life to the full. This sophisticated app will be a one-stop lifestyle platform for PwDs, from accessibility-related information, jobs, matrimonials, social media, medical e-commerce, consultancy, advocacy, training and certifications. Currently, no other platform is offering a complete lifestyle platform for the differently abled community.

On one side, DivyanGO will offer the following services to PwD registered users:

- Interactive map of Bharat with accessibility ratings and user reviews, so that Divyangjan know where they can go out safely

- Job search portal for Divyangjan

- Matrimonial portal for Divyangjan

- Medical e-commerce marketplace for Divyangjan to find discounted specialist medical equipment

- Social media platform for Divyangjan to connect and message each other

- Consultancy and advocacy services for Divyangjan who need disability-related legal support

The other side of DivyanGO, for registered businesses, will offer:

- DivyanGO training and certification services (offering government-compliant guidelines, assessments and final grading for businesses to make their outlets more accessible and inclusive)

For businesses and service providers, DivyanGO is a specialised directory giving them access to the niche customer base of PwDs and their loved ones

As of now, there is no standardised effort across India to unify the built environment according to the guidelines set out in the PWD Act (2016). DivyanGO aims to change that by becoming the industry leader in accessibility standardisation and assessment.

Name: SDR Progression
Description:
SDR PROGRESSION
SDR Progression offers therapy that is focused specifically for your child's capabilities and needs. This service is also extended to teenagers and adults. Therapy is offered to those suffering from Cerebral Palsy, and also those whom have had SDR (Selective Dorsal Rhizotomy) surgery which attempts to remove spasticity, allowing children to have the ability to achieve their goals.

SDR Progression provides;
* Long term commitment
* Flexible courses
* Motivation and inspiration
* Continual assessment and evaluation
* Progression Programmes
* Video analysis and feedback for you and your child
* Recommendation of other affiliates
* Correspondence with your child's other health proffesionals to ensure consistency.
SDR Progression was set up initially to focus on working with children pre and post SDR Surgery. As a result of this i have come across and currently treat children that have Cerebral Palsy but SDR Surgery is not suitable or not an option. For this reason i have also extended the service to the all that suffer from Cerebral Palsy so that i can advise and help more families.

Strength and conditioning is an important part of the child's progress, however educating the child in how to harness and apply this to functional movement(s) is SDR Progression's focus.
Regardless of how much strength and  ability a child has, this process will be applied during each session(s) in order to create  good habits.

My ethos still stays the same;
* Bespoke service 
* One to one consultations

Name: Allai AB
Description:
Allai is a Swedish healthtech company built on the belief that AI should build relationships, not replace them — and that even a short conversation can make a big difference.

We develop human-centered voice assistants that enhance safety, independence, and well-being in everyday life.

Our flagship service, The Companion (www.trygghetssamtalet.se), is a virtual voice that calls older adults every morning to ask how they’re feeling, offer meaningful conversation, and promote daily well-being. The calls are short but significant — powered by AI trained to listen, respond, and communicate with warmth, empathy, and understanding.

If a call goes unanswered or something seems wrong, the service immediately alerts family members or caregivers — providing peace of mind for both the user and their loved ones.

Many seniors today live with limited social contact or unwanted loneliness. The Companion acts as a trusted daily presence — a friendly voice that brings reassurance, connection, and care, especially for those living alone or with limited support.

The service is developed in close collaboration with SPF Seniorerna, Sweden’s leading senior organization, together with families, caregivers, and municipalities. This partnership ensures that our solutions are technically advanced yet simple to use — always with people at the center.

Name: MM-EVO
Description:
MM-EVO est un bureau d'études engagé depuis 2017 dans le secteur de la rénovation de l’habitat, avec une expertise initiale dans l’accompagnement des personnes en situation de fragilité énergétique. Forte de cette expérience, elle a progressivement recentré son activité principale autour de l’adaptation des logements pour les personnes âgées ou en situation de handicap, répondant ainsi à un enjeu sociétal majeur : le maintien à domicile en toute sécurité et autonomie.

Une mission dédiée à l’autonomie et au bien-être
MM-EVO accompagne les seniors et les personnes en perte d’autonomie dans leur souhait de continuer à vivre chez eux, en adaptant leur logement à leurs besoins évolutifs. Le bureau d'études se distingue par une approche personnalisée et un service clé en main, comprenant :

Un diagnostic approfondi : Identification des aménagements nécessaires, tels que la transformation d’une baignoire en douche sécurisée, l’installation d’un monte-escalier ou l’adaptation des toilettes.

Un accompagnement administratif complet : Aide dans les démarches pour obtenir des subventions comme MaPrimeAdapt’ ou d’autres aides publiques, facilitant ainsi le financement des travaux.

Expertise reconnue et solutions sur mesure
En tant qu’assistant à maîtrise d’ouvrage (AMO) habilité par l’Anah, MM-EVO garantit un suivi rigoureux tout au long du projet d’adaptation. L’entreprise s’appuie sur son savoir-faire pour simplifier un processus parfois complexe, offrant aux bénéficiaires un environnement adapté et sécurisé.

Un engagement durable depuis 2017
Depuis sa création, MM-EVO a su évoluer pour répondre aux besoins spécifiques des personnes vulnérables. Après avoir œuvré dans la rénovation énergétique, elle met désormais son expertise au service du maintien à domicile. Cette transition reflète son engagement pour une société inclusive où chacun peut vivre chez soi de manière autonome et épanouie.

Avec MM-EVO, « votre habitat s’adapte à vous, pas l’inverse ».

Name: HAARAN- Horn of Africa Aid and Rehabilitation Action Network
Description: Horn of Africa Aid and Rehabilitation Action Network was formed in 2012 in Lewiston, Maine. The founders are Abdi Noor Matan, Executive Director, Bashir Ali, Treasurer, and Christina Feller, Technical Resource Consultant. We are still working as a Team 10 years on to fulfill the mission of the organization, which is to "create safe havens for immigrants and refugees to use the resources around them to rebuild their lives, reach their potential, care for their elders and those who live with disabilities, and find new ways of rebuilding a peaceful society." The organization relocated to St. Peter in 2017, and since then, we have been carrying out a number of important projects, all underwritten by grants or by Government Reimbursements. HAARAN has an outpatient mental health program, and East African Elderly Healthy Eating Program that includes providing nutritious meals, a community liaison program improving parent interactions and communications with schools; we are part of the ARMHS (Adult Rehabilitation Mental Health Services) program. HAARAN successfully carried out a caregiver training program in 2021 funded by the CARES Act. HAARAN has become an integral part of the community of caregivers, whether paid or volunteer, because we are trusted providers of important information about how to better manage and handle their roles and responsibilities as caregivers. That was a very successful project, so successful that caregivers have requested that we become Caregiver Consultants and have continued to seek out our advice about maintaining health and safety standards during this time of COVID and the variants.
