Objectifs de l'association :
1 - Éduquer et qualifier les personnes en situation de handicap et favoriser leur intégration socio-économique.
2 - Contribuer à l'amélioration des conditions de scolarisation des enfants en situation de handicap.
3 - Intégrer les personnes en situation de handicap dans l'environnement général (social, sportif, intellectuel, artistique, économique).
4 - Renforcer les capacités intellectuelles, sensorielles et les compétences des personnes en situation de handicap.
5 - Préparer les personnes en situation de handicap et créer un esprit d'initiative pour servir les causes nationales.
6 - Développer l'esprit de solidarité, de travail collectif et de volontariat entre les personnes en situation de handicap et les personnes valides.
7 - Défendre les droits des personnes en situation de handicap (santé, scolarisation, accessibilité, emploi...).
8 - Créer une banque de données sur les statistiques des personnes en situation de handicap dans la province et classifier les types de handicap.
9 - Lancer des initiatives provinciales et régionales concernant la réhabilitation communautaire, la formation professionnelle et le soutien aux idées productives et entrepreneuriales pour les personnes en situation de handicap.
10 - Créer un atelier pour la fabrication et la réparation d'appareils et de prothèses médicales pour les personnes à mobilité réduite.
11 - Créer un club sportif et s'engager dans des compétitions nationales et internationales.
12 - Organiser des programmes d'animation touristique, culturelle et artistique au niveau national et international.

Name: Veterans Guide
Description:
VeteransGuide.org is an initiative founded by the Disability Advocate Group, LLC, a Delaware incorporated entity, dedicated to championing the cause of U.S. armed forces veterans. Comprising a nationwide team of veterans and allies, we provide comprehensive educational materials and direct access to a panel of expert counselors and legal professionals in collaboration with the National Advocates for Veterans Disability Assistance, LLC (NAVDA).

We are acutely aware of the challenges veterans face when attempting to access government resources. Similarly, we understand the skepticism that can arise from the content provided by private websites, particularly those associated with law firms or other organizations. 
To ensure credibility and trust, we are steadfast in our commitment to offer content and resources that are either prepared by people with direct experience or meticulously researched and backed by reliable sources. One of our support members is Linda Cosme, a United States Army Veteran who is passionate about helping veterans get disability benefits.

Our ultimate objective, whether through our self-serve content or via our association with NAVDA, is to support veterans in achieving an accurate VA disability rating.

In addition to resources for veteran disability, VeteransGuide.org offers links to thoroughly vetted legal professionals and law firms, along with self-paced learning materials.

Disability Advocate Group, LLC is a Delaware organization with a team spread out across the country, collaborating across time zones. Our team members are proud to be representing every corner of the United States.

Name: Activate Consultants
Description:
Life isn’t easy at the best of times - but when the bombshell of a severe disability explodes into a family’s life the implications cannot all be taken in at once. A few images of what life might involve (maybe wheelchairs and spoon-feeding) are often outnumbered by the things that will now NOT happen.

So when we meet parents for the first time, it is often to these shattered dreams that they are still clutching - while they face a future full of unknowns, a life full of people and experiences that they probably will have never seen before.

So how can we help?

* We listen and assure families that there is a fulfilling life ahead of them.
* We help them navigate the various statutory services.
* We offer an insight into the principles and science behind Postural 
  Management.
* We advise on the choice of Wheelchair/Buggy Provision - protecting
  families from unscrupulous providers.
* We advise on Travel Safety - the type of vehicle and the method of restraint.
* We advise on Architectural Design for High-Care living spaces - avoiding 
  expensive mistakes.
* We assist with the Planning Process for Housing Development - using our 
  associate Occupational Therapists and Educational Psychologists to create a 
  compelling case for approval.
* We oversee the Build process - to ensure the necessary adaptations are not 
  affected by the application of Building Regulations.

Through all of this, we help families to strike their own balance, whilst drawing on our experiences with hundreds of other families to help them to avoid making the same mistakes.

Description:
My Brother and I founded MyLegacyCare in mid-September of 2025, with an aim of providing lasting support for all when it matters most.

LegacyCare – "Ensuring your love never forgets, even when memory fails."
Transform your most precious moments into lasting gestures of love and care. LegacyCare empowers you to pre-record heartfelt messages, schedule meaningful gifts, and plan celebrations years in advance—so your loved ones feel remembered on every birthday, anniversary, and special occasion, whether you're there or not.
But LegacyCare goes beyond life.

Name: Upgrading Standards
Description:
According to research, there are millions of people around the world who live with physical challenges that require wheelchairs, canes, and walkers to keep them mobile; population analytics show a significant annual growth in this demographic.
What once was considered adequate accessibility in buildings, existing and new constructions, and other environments are not truly user-friendly and impact the lives and safety of this demographic daily.

Before Upgrading Standards, there were no companies that provided consulting services for:
- small businesses - medical facilities/systems
- architectural and development firms - and beyond …
that analyze and convey the needs, gaps, and accessibility oversights in individual businesses from the consumer perspective with methods to develop feasible and effective plans of change.

Our services collaborate with companies who understand the need for thorough analyses and actionable solutions that fill gaps experienced by physically impaired clients/consumers.

Acknowledging the need for specialized assessments and commitment to deliberate re-configurations to establishments, buildings, environments, and elements of services are the first steps for serving people of all abilities.

Collaborators are chosen by their willingness to revisit practices, plans, perceived barriers, and have an open mind to implement changes based on evidence-based assessments. This service is beneficial for any business, but we choose to work with those who have the humility to listen and the enthusiasm to act. Going beyond what is required and prioritizing the customer experience of those with physical challenges is important to us and we want to join forces with others who share this desired outcome.

Elle offre une véritable plateforme de valorisation pour des œuvres artistiques et des produits artisanaux créés par des personnes en situation de handicap, en mettant en lumière leur talent, leur créativité et leur singularité.

Au-delà de cette vocation de diffusion et de commercialisation, La Galerie Solidaire développe également des activités et prestations à destination des publics accompagnés, en proposant des ateliers d’expression artistique et de relaxation. Ces temps sont pensés comme des espaces de bien-être, de réflexion et de développement personnel, favorisant l’expression des émotions, l’estime de soi et l’autonomie, tout en s’adaptant aux besoins et aux capacités de chacun.

Chaque œuvre présentée raconte une histoire unique et témoigne d’un travail méticuleux, offrant aux visiteurs et aux clients la possibilité d’acquérir des créations de qualité tout en soutenant une démarche profondément humaine et solidaire.

La Galerie Solidaire représente ainsi bien plus qu’un simple lieu d’exposition ou de vente : c’est un espace d’expérimentation sociale, où l’art devient un levier d’inclusion, de participation et d’impact social, au service des personnes accompagnées comme du grand public.

Description:
At Power Unleashed, we’re not here to tick boxes—we’re here to create real change.

We empower young people with disabilities to build confidence, skills, and independence through hands-on capacity-building. Our programs go beyond traditional support models, focusing on real-world experiences that help participants grow, thrive, and take control of their future.

What We Do:
 Practical Learning – Skills that actually translate into everyday life, work, and independence.
 Confidence & Mindset Growth – Helping participants build self-belief and take on new challenges.
 Support Without Limits – A program that adapts to individual goals, not just funding categories.

We know that one-size-fits-all doesn’t work. That’s why Power Unleashed is designed to meet each participant where they are and support them in building the life they want.

If you’re a Support Coordinator, Allied Health Professional, or parent, we’d love to connect and explore how Power Unleashed can be the right fit for the people you support.

 Let’s start the conversation—follow us to stay updated!

Description:
Inclusive Endeavors is a nonprofit organization dedicated to advancing social and labor inclusion for people with disabilities. We believe that everyone deserves access to meaningful employment and community participation, regardless of physical, cognitive, or sensory differences.

Our mission is to create equal opportunities by partnering with businesses, governments, and civil society to build inclusive ecosystems. Through customized training, employment readiness programs, accessibility consulting, and strategic inclusion frameworks, we help organizations and individuals thrive together.

We support companies in becoming more inclusive employers by providing tools and knowledge to attract, hire, and retain talent with disabilities. We also empower people with disabilities to strengthen their skills, grow their confidence, and gain access to sustainable employment and entrepreneurship.

From awareness-raising campaigns and public policy advocacy to direct capacity-building projects, Inclusive Endeavors is a catalyst for inclusive change across Latin America and beyond.

We envision a world where diversity is not only accepted, but celebrated—and where inclusion drives innovation, dignity, and shared prosperity.

Join us on our journey to build a more inclusive future for all.

Name: Pró60+ Feira de Equipamento Serviços e Soluções de Atendimento a Idosos
Description:
A PRÓ 60+ será a primeira feira de fornecedores para estabelecimentos especializados no atendimento de pessoas 60+.

Atualmente, nosso país conta com mais de 30 milhões de brasileiros com 60 ou mais anos de idade. Segundo estimativas, esse número deve chegar a 32 milhões em 2025 e a 68,1 milhões em 2050. De acordo com o IPEA (Instituto de Pesquisa Econômica Aplicada) mais de 85.000 idosos vivem em Instituições de Longa Permanência de Idosos (ILPIs), são cerca de 110.000 leitos em todo o país com ocupação de cerca de 90%.

Acreditamos que esse mercado tenha muito potencial de crescimento nos próximos anos e que as instituições e empresas especializadas no atendimento a idosos possam se beneficiar da aproximação e conhecimento de novos fornecedores para agregar valor aos seus serviços.

A PRÓ 60+ será realizada entre 24 e 26 de Abril de 2025 no Santos Convention Center. A feira contará com a visita de proprietários, gestores e profissionais de estabelecimentos especializados no atendimento a idosos, principalmente Instituições de Longa Permanência de Idosos (ILPIs), além de empreendedores, profissionais da saúde e empresas que queiram se aperfeiçoar no atendimento a este público.

Name: ΛΕΩΝΙΑ ΘΕΣΣΑΛΟΝΙΚΗΣ
Description:
Η Πρότυπη Μονάδα Φροντίδας Ηλικιωμένων ΛΕΩΝΙΑ στη Θεσσαλονίκη, βρίσκεται στο Κάτω Σχολάρι, στον Δήμο Θέρμης και ξεκίνησε τη λειτουργία της, τον Ιούνιο του 2022.

Στη Μονάδα απασχολείται εξειδικευμένο και άρτια εκπαιδευμένο προσωπικό, που αποτελείται από κορυφαίους γιατρούς, φαρμακοποιούς, νοσηλευτές, φυσιοθεραπευτές, ψυχολόγους και εργοθεραπευτές και το οποίο υποστηρίζει πλήρως τις υπηρεσίες φροντίδας που παρέχουμε. Με περίσσια αγάπη προς τον άνθρωπο, αφουγκραζόμαστε κάθε ανάγκη του και φροντίζουμε για την βελτίωση της καθημερινότητας της τρίτης ηλικίας, με την παροχή εξατομικευμένης προσέγγισης στους αγαπημένους σας από το ιατρικό και νοσηλευτικό προσωπικό μας.

Στόχος μας, να γίνεται πραγματικότητα το όραμά μας και να επιτυγχάνεται η αποστολή μας καθημερινά με τον καλύτερο δυνατό τρόπο.
Πρόκειται για μία υπερσύγχρονη γηριατρική μονάδα, που έχει δημιουργηθεί σύμφωνα με ευρωπαϊκά πρότυπα και προδιαγραφές και στεγάζεται σε ολοκαίνουργιο κτήριο που κατασκευάστηκε για αυτόν τον σκοπό. Η ΜΦΗ Λεώνια Θεσσαλονίκης εξειδικεύεται στη φροντίδα ηλικιωμένων που υποφέρουν από Άνοια ή τη νόσο Alzheimer και το μοντέλο φροντίδας που ακολουθεί είναι δυναμικό, προσφέροντας απασχόληση, κινητοποίηση και όχι μόνο παθητική φροντίδα.

Διαθέτει άριστες ξενοδοχειακές εγκαταστάσεις και υλικοτεχνικό εξοπλισμό, με ευρύχωρα μονόκλινα, δίκλινα και τρίκλινα δωμάτια, ενώ παράλληλα με την βοήθεια του έμπειρου εξειδικευμένου προσωπικού μας, παρέχουμε υπηρεσίες φροντίδας, νοσηλευτικής και ιατρικής υποστήριξης υψηλού επιπέδου, εξαιρετικές συνθήκες διαβίωσης, ασφάλειας και αισθητικής, με στόχο πάντα την καλύτερη ποιότητα ζωής των περιθαλπομένων μας.

Name: mysc.com.au
Description:
MYSC functions as a trusted partner, working with participants, their families, and other stakeholders to understand their unique needs and ambitions. They apply their expertise of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) to develop individualised support plans that align with the aims of participants. 
MYSC’s major job is to organise and administer these support plans, ensuring that participants have access to the services and support they need.  They work diligently to connect individuals with appropriate service providers and help them navigate the application and approval processes. 

Moreover, MYSC serves as a dedicated advocate for participants, ensuring they receive the best possible care and support. They strive to empower individuals by building their capacity to manage their plans, make informed decisions, and exercise choice and control over their support journey. 

MYSC’s role extends beyond administrative tasks. They provide ongoing assistance, monitor the progress of the support plan, and make adjustments as necessary. They also collaborate with a network of service providers and community organisations, facilitating connections and referrals to enhance participants’ overall well-being. 

In summary, MYSC (Support Coordinator) is a compassionate and knowledgeable professional who acts as a guide and advocate for individuals seeking support services. Their expertise in the NDIS framework, personalised approach, and commitment to empowering participants make them invaluable resource in optimizing the support journey.

Name: De OpStapBus Capelle aan den IJssel
Description:
In de loop der jaren kunnen vriendschappen veranderen of zelfs vervagen. Ouderen worden vaak geconfronteerd met een afname van sociale activiteiten, gepaard gaande met het verlies van dierbaren door verhuizing, gezondheidsproblemen of overlijden. De levendige sociale kringen van toen transformeren soms tot stille ruimtes.

Het verlies van sociale contacten heeft diepgaande gevolgen voor het welzijn. Sociale isolatie kan leiden tot eenzaamheid, een gevoel dat niet alleen mentaal, maar ook het fysieke welzijn beïnvloedt. Het gevoel van verbondenheid, dat gedurende vele jaren is opgebouwd, kan plotseling veranderen, waardoor een leegte ontstaat die moeilijk op te vullen is.

Dit is precies de reden waarom z’n 30 vrijwilligers van de OpStapBus zich het hele jaar door inzetten om uitjes te organiseren voor kwetsbare ouderen. Een dagje naar een museum, een ritje naar de kringloop, bijpraten met oude bekenden en nieuwe mensen ontmoeten. Een dagje weg kan écht het verschil maken.

Om uitjes te organiseren hebben we steun nodig. Je kunt ons helpen door een financiële donatie te doen. Ook kun je ons helpen met een dienst of product. Koffie voor in de bus, een tank benzine, een onderhoudsbeurt aan onze bus, een rondleiding door jouw organisatie, een lunch, noem maar op.
Alle hulp kunnen we heel goed gebruiken!

Het geluk van een ander, ligt ook in jouw handen Ouderdom of eenzaamheid lijkt misschien ver van je af te staan. Maar het is dichterbij dan je denkt. Een vader, moeder, opa of oma, buurvrouw op de hoek…

De bewoners blijven in principe hun verdere leven in hun appartement wonen. Ook als hun gezondheid achteruit gaat, kunnen zij rekenen op de juiste begeleiding en de zorg van de medewerkers die hen zo goed kennen.

Name: Clinica Martinelli - Casa de Repouso
Description:
A Clínica Martinelli - Casa de Repouso em Jundiaí é a principal referência em tratamento geriátrico e gerontológico na região. Oferecemos uma vasta gama de serviços de saúde com uma equipe multidisciplinar altamente qualificada e especializada em geriatria e gerontologia. Nosso objetivo é assegurar o bem-estar dos nossos residentes e proporcionar tranquilidade às suas famílias, promovendo qualidade de vida através de abordagens terapêuticas personalizadas.

Nossos espaços são organizados de acordo com as necessidades e diagnósticos específicos de cada paciente, criando um ambiente seguro e adequado para:

Idosos Independentes: Para pessoas com mais de 60 anos que, apesar de lúcidas, não se sentem seguras em suas casas e preferem não morar com familiares.

Cuidados com Demência: Especializados em cuidar de pessoas com doenças neurodegenerativas como Alzheimer, Demência Frontotemporal e Demência por Corpos de Lewy.

Recuperação Pós-Hospitalar: Suporte para aqueles em recuperação após internações, como pós-operatório de fratura de fêmur, que ainda não podem voltar para casa.

Reabilitação: Assistência para pessoas em processo de reabilitação após AVC ou outros eventos, necessitando de recuperação de capacidades físicas e motoras.

Cuidados Paliativos: Cuidados paliativos para pacientes com mais de 60 anos sem perspectiva de cura, incluindo cuidados a pacientes com câncer, acamados em coma ou com algum nível de consciência.

Na Clínica Martinelli - Casa de Repouso em Jundiaí, cada detalhe é cuidadosamente planejado para oferecer um ambiente acolhedor e seguro, garantindo que nossos residentes recebam o cuidado e a atenção que merecem. Nossa missão é maximizar a qualidade de vida, respeitando a individualidade e as necessidades de cada um.

Name: PALS LLC
Description:
PALS has been providing quality services to adults with developmental disabilities for over 17 years. Established in 1998, Pals has been dedicated to assisting individuals to live independently in their own homes. PALS specializes in personally designed services that are tailored fit to suit the individual's needs. Located in Whittier and Indio, PALS serves clients of the Regional Center who reside in Los Angeles County and the Coachella Valley.

PALS is designed to assist individuals in achieving their goals and objectives in all areas of life. The aim of PALS is to respectfully and responsibly develop each consumer's program design to suit their individual needs, goals, and aspirations. 

PALS does not operate as a special needs program in which consumers are categorized or grouped according to disability (within the organization and outside community), but rather, supports a true supported living philosophy. PALS faithfully adheres to the premise that individuals with disabilities, regardless of the nature of severity, can be successfully integrated into their surrounded community through supports (professional and natural), one-to-one instruction, and effective training techniques. Such supports are always age appropriate and directly related to a client's successful participation in community life.

Description:
Wir freuen uns, die Eröffnung des Infoase Vereins bekannt zu geben! Unser Verein befindet sich in der Hauptstrasse 56, 4132 Muttenz und bietet eine einzigartige Kombination aus Beratung, Café und Blumengeschäft, speziell zugeschnitten auf die Bedürfnisse von Senioren und Pensionierten.

Was ist Infoase?

Infoase ist mehr als nur ein Ort – es ist ein Zufluchtsort für Senioren, wo sie in einer freundlichen und unterstützenden Umgebung Rat und Hilfe finden können. Unsere Dienstleistungen sind effizient, direkt und vor allem kostenlos.

Unser Angebot:

Beratung: Wir unterstützen Senioren bei allen möglichen Anliegen, sei es bei technischen Problemen mit dem Handy oder der Organisation von Transportmöglichkeiten. Unser Ziel ist es, schnell und unkompliziert Lösungen zu bieten.
Café: Senioren können jederzeit kostenlos Kaffee und Kuchen geniessen, in einer Atmosphäre, die zum Verweilen und Entspannen einlädt.
Blumen und Pflanzen: Um die Umgebung zu verschönern und eine angenehme Atmosphäre zu schaffen, verkaufen wir auch eine Auswahl an Blumen und Pflanzen.
Infoase ist eine Oase für Senioren, ein Ort, an dem sie sich wohlfühlen und gut aufgehoben wissen können. Wir laden alle Senioren und Pensionierten herzlich ein, uns zu besuchen und unsere Angebote zu nutzen.

Besuchen Sie uns und erleben Sie die Infoase!

Name: Golden Bridges Senior Care Services
Description:
At Golden Bridges Personal Care Home, we believe that everyone deserves a golden home during their golden years. Our mission is to provide compassionate, personalized care that promotes the dignity, safety, and well-being of every resident. We strive to create a home-like environment where each individual feels valued, supported, and empowered to live their best life, surrounded by comfort, respect, and a true sense of community.

We are committed to delivering exceptional care that enhances the quality of life for our residents and their families. Our team offers 24-hour care and supervision, ensuring that every need is met with professionalism and kindness. Residents benefit from medication management and assistance, as well as access to physical, occupational, and speech/language therapy to maintain and improve their well-being.

Our services also include personal laundry and linen services, as well as comfortable private and semi-private rooms with climate-controlled living spaces to ensure maximum comfort. Residents enjoy three daily home-style meals and snacks, with special dietary accommodations available. For added convenience, we offer monthly in-house doctor visits and respite care stays for families who need short-term support.

At Golden Bridges, we believe that quality of life extends beyond physical care. That’s why we offer a wide range of engaging activities, from BINGO and arts & crafts to music and more. Residents can relax in our outdoor sitting areas and courtyards and enjoy socializing in a safe, supportive environment.

Whether you’re looking for short-term or long-term care options, or live-in caregivers for more dedicated attention, Golden Bridges Personal Care Home provides a nurturing space where residents feel truly at home. We also offer home health care services for those who need personalized care in the comfort of their own residence.

At Golden Bridges, we are here to support you and your loved ones every step of the way.

Name: Melodies As Medicine
Description:
Melodies as Medicine, Inc., was born to link the healing power of music with seniors, worldwide, who may not have the ability or access to attend music performances in person and collaborate with many global organizations promoting this mission. 

Scientific studies demonstrate the health benefits of listening to and playing musical instruments on brain health and the central nervous system. Exposing seniors to musical journeys to their past, helps them reconnect with themselves, as well as explore the unlocked doors to the special memories in their life.

As one of the first completely student-run 501(c)(3) nonprofit organizations focused on bringing music to seniors and sharing the benefits of music therapy, we are a compassionate group of teens who are determined to utilize our passion for music and science as a bridge to reaching seniors and vulnerable populations worldwide. Our mission is to provide a platform for young people to channel their creative energy towards improving the lives of seniors and vulnerable populations. We facilitate opportunities for our members to engage directly with the communities they strive to assist and to develop creative fundraising opportunities and awareness/education campaigns through social media.

Our journey began with self-managed HCP clients who wanted something more personal—care that feels like family, not just a service. That inspired us to create a provider that truly listens, builds lasting relationships, and treats people with dignity and respect.

We now proudly support both NDIS and HCP participants with services such as:
 Personal care & domestic assistance
 Community access & transport
 Social companionship & wellbeing support
 Meal prep, shopping assistance & light household tasks
 Reliable, mobile care across Hobart

Whether it’s a short visit or ongoing care, our team is here to make everyday life easier, safer, and more meaningful.

Name: The Agilitas Foundation
Description:
The Agilitas Foundation is a nonprofit organization committed to restoring independence, dignity, and opportunity for individuals with disabilities by funding essential mobility products and solutions. At Agilitas, we believe that mobility is more than movement—it’s the key to freedom, self-sufficiency, and a life without limitations. 

**Our Mission**

Our mission is to empower individuals with disabilities by providing life-changing mobility solutions. Through fundraising and community support, we help fund products and services to help those in need. Together, we’re breaking barriers and restoring independence—because mobility is freedom. 

**Our Impact**

The impact of our work is profound, as mobility challenges affect a significant portion of the population:

- More than 1 in 4 adults in the U.S. (27%) have a disability, yet mobility equipment and adaptive solutions remain financially out of reach for many. 

- The average cost of a power wheelchair ranges from $2,000 to $30,000, depending on customization, while basic manual wheelchairs can cost $500 to $5,000. 

- Adaptive vehicles—including wheelchair-accessible vans—can cost $40,000 to $100,000, with conversions alone ranging from $10,000 to $30,000. 

- Only 15% of individuals who need mobility equipment receive full coverage through insurance or government programs, leaving 85% to pay out-of-pocket or seek alternative funding sources. 

By addressing these challenges, The Agilitas Foundation plays a crucial role in enhancing the quality of life for individuals with disabilities.

**Get Involved**

We invite you to join us in making a difference:

- **Donate:** Your financial support enables us to provide essential mobility solutions to those in need.

- **Volunteer:** Offer your time and skills to support our programs and initiatives.

- **Partner:** Collaborate with us to expand our reach and impact within the community.

Name: Lighthouse Professional Placement & Caregivers
Description:
Lighthouse Professional Placement and Caregivers offers free, knowledgeable support to families navigating senior care decisions across the Bay Area and throughout California. We understand the urgency and complexity involved in finding quality care for a loved one, and we’re here to simplify the process—quickly, compassionately, and at no cost to the family.

We assist with placements in licensed assisted living, board & care homes, memory care, and hospice communities. Every placement is guided by a personalized consultation to assess medical needs, personal preferences, and financial circumstances. We coordinate tours, schedule assessments, assist with discharges, and guide families through licensing standards, paperwork, and provider communication to ensure a smooth transition.

For families needing care at home, we provide access to dependable in-home caregivers who are trained to support a wide range of needs—including dementia, mobility limitations, hospice, and post-hospital recovery. Caregivers are experienced, background-checked, and many offer multilingual support to better serve diverse households. Whether care is needed hourly, overnight, or live-in, we coordinate services that are responsive, reliable, and aligned with each family’s preferences.

What sets Lighthouse apart is our responsiveness, follow-through, and the personal attention we give to every case. We don’t just offer lists—we stay involved, ask the right questions, and act as an advocate from the first call through placement or caregiver coordination. Our team is knowledgeable, efficient, and deeply committed to helping families feel informed, supported, and confident during one of life’s most difficult transitions.

Name: Code Care Services
Description:
Code Care services aims at empowering people with disabilities to be able to exercise their choice and control in their support services.
The services we provide are:
•	Assist travel and transport
We provide suitable transport for our participants to enable them to access services that they wish to access. These includes access to visit family and friends, recreational facilities, shopping, doctors’ appointments, specialists’ services appointments, and other intended objectives requiring transport.

•	Community Nursing 
Our experienced nurses will help you with medication administration, Wound management, Subcutaneous and intramuscular injections, Catheterization, Peg feeding, diabetic management and other, in the comfort of your home



•	Daily Living life skills 
Our experienced and dedicated support workers assist you to join community clubs, activities, and programs, learn employable skills, buy groceries, look after your health and fitness, support clients at work and when volunteering.

•	Participate Community
Our team assist our clients to connect with community groups, facilities, and social amenities around your area. They assist to gain confidence, skills and develop long lasting friendships. Participating in the community helps to engage with other social groups that support people with disabilities. 
•	Household tasks
Our dedicated support workers will assist our clients with cleaning your room or house, laundry services (where washing machines are available), washing dishes, cleaning windows
•	Assist in daily personal activities
The team will assist you in the following areas, personal hygiene, including showering, bathing, and personal grooming, toileting, and bowel management. They will assist with meal preparation and assist in the use of appliances such as aids and communication gadgets. Our dedicated staff will assist in safely transferring our clients from the bed to wheelchair vice versa using safe manual handling techniques.

Name: Multi Service Utrecht
Description:
Diensten: Grafonderhoud - Helpende Hand - Klussen - Computerservice - Tuinonderhoud - Multimedia

Grafonderhoud - Hulp bij het onderhouden en verzorgen van graven. Schoonmaken grafsteen of grafzerk, onkruid, mos en blad verwijderen. Planten of bloemen vervangen. Letters en graveerteksten bijwerken. 

Helpende Hand - Hulp en advies geven bij administratie, regel- en uitzoekwerk en bellen met instanties. Een luisterend oor zijn en als vertrouwenspersoon mensen mogen bijstaan, soms ook in moeilijke tijden, bijv. bij het verliezen van een partner.

Klussen - Hulp bij kleine klussen in en om het huis. Hulpmiddelen plaatsen, lamp ophangen, spullen opruimen, boodschap doen, schilderij ophangen, hulp bij verhuizen, wandje sauzen, kast in elkaar zetten, afvoeren grofvuil.

Computerservice - Hulp bij computeronderhoud, advies en uitleg nieuwe apparatuur, wegwijs maken in de digitale wereld. Backups maken, updates en installaties uitvoeren, accounts aanmaken, randapparatuur aansluiten, wifi instellen, email en internetbankieren instellen, veilig internetten. Digitale nalatenschap verzorgen.

Tuinonderhoud - Hulp bij tuinwerkzaamheden zoals snoeien, onkruid weghalen, bladruimen, gras maaien, planten vervangen, terras schoonspuiten.

Multimedia - Hulp bij audio en tv. Uitleg smartphone of tablet. Advies, aanschaf en aansluiten nieuwe apparaten.

Description:
Sahara Disability Services is a family-run disability support provider servicing Geelong and surrounding areas. Founded by two brothers-one with an allied health background and the other with direct experiences as a support worker-our business was built on a belief that genuine relations and purposeful support can change lives. 

We specialise in peer mentorship and capacity building, supporting participants to develop life skills, engage with their communities, and grow in confidence. Our approach is not just about assistance - its about empowerment, connection and progress. 

Operating under National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), we provide tailored participant-led supports designed around the individuals, their strengths and goals. Our Team values authenticity, consistency, and autonomy of our participants - and we walk alongside our participants every step of the way. 


What Sets Us Apart

Family-owned & Operated: Our clients become part of a close-knit, supportive network that values trust and relationships.

Allied Health Insight: Our leadership brings a deep understanding of holistic care and the value of support work in long-term outcomes.

Peer Mentorship Focus: We match clients with workers who can offer not only support but real-life mentorship and relatable experiences.

Capacity Building Approach: We aim to grow our participants independence and confidence - not their reliance.

Local Knowledge: We live and work in the communities we serve - Geelong and Surroudns - bringing local knowledge and connections to our support 

As we grow, our goal remains clear: To deliver honest, high-quality, and empowering support that truely makes a difference to the lives of the people we work with.

Name: Püre Home Care
Description:
When it comes to the “why” of Pure Home Care, we derive it from our firm conviction that each person has a right to a life that is dignified, independent and, as much as possible, fulfilling, despite any challenges they may have to overcome. We are motivated by a deep conviction that we are all different. Every individual has some special aspirations which should be pursued.

It is our conviction that dedicated services based on compassion are sufficient to help the vulnerable embrace their life and fulfil their potential. Our “why” is to be a beacon that illuminates the path, and enables people to make their own journey to lives full of purpose, joy, and autonomy.

For us, “why” is about our desire to impact the lives of those we are dedicated to in a meaningful way. We feel so strongly about what we do, and we have the courage to make a difference, starting with an individual. For us, Pure Home Care does not see “why” just as what we want to know; it’s the essence of our life; it’s what we always do and try to do better every day.

Name: Creative Chance
Description:
Creative Chance is an innovative project designed to break down social barriers between those with and without disabilities in Baltimore, while promoting, educating, and creating opportunities within the arts for people of all abilities. 

Project Specifics:
- implementation of street festivals and gallery style events in Baltimore communities
- exposure for an extensive network of artists within the disability 
community, including dancers, theater performers, musicians, visual artists, comedians, photographers, DJs, community activists and more 
- opportunities for vendors and artists to sell and promote
- live performances throughout the events 
- opportunities for personal interviews and education surrounding artists with disabilities 
- employment and recreational opportunities 
- goal of increased awareness and access to local businesses for people with disabilities 
- incredible social change within Baltimore communities 

Creative Chance is an original inspiration, mission and vision of Founder/Director Amanda Taylor. Ms. Taylor has been working with individuals with disabilities since 1999 in various roles, specializing in autism therapy, creative movement and expressive arts therapy techniques. Her background includes many years of performance, but her most recent roles included serving as a program director in a center for independent living where she designed and pioneered multiple programs and curricula in the Baltimore area, working as a services coordinator for individuals with neuromuscular diseases and most recently, for those with mental health diagnoses. She has extensive experience in nonprofit organizations, and it has been her dream to enter the nonprofit world as an entrepreneur, finally making Creative Chance a reality after many years of planning. 

Grants and additional funding mechanisms are needed to sustain Creative Chance, as we are in the process of formation of the organization as a nonprofit 501(c)(3).

Name: This Ability Support
Description:
At This Ability, we believe that every individual has unique strengths waiting to be celebrated. As a dedicated disability support service, we proudly serve the communities of the Gold Coast, Logan, Ipswich, and Tweed Shire Regions, offering tailored support that truly makes a difference.

Founded from firsthand experience within large support organisations, This Ability was born out of a desire to do things differently. We saw a gap where individuality was often overlooked, and participants were slotted into pre-existing programs that didn’t reflect their interests or aspirations. That’s why we created This Ability—to shift the focus from programs to people.

We don’t believe in one-size-fits-all. Instead, we take the time to listen, understand, and build support around what truly matters to each person. Whether it’s nurturing existing abilities or exploring new interests, our approach is as unique as the individuals we support.


Why Choose This Ability?

Individual - Centred Approach: Your goals, interests, and aspirations shape the support you receive.

Flexible & Adaptive: We evolve with you, offering opportunities to try new things and discover new passions.

Genuine Connections: We build meaningful relationships based on trust, respect, and understanding.

Local Focus: Proudly serving our local communities with heart and commitment.


At This Ability, we don’t just provide support—we embrace potential, celebrate abilities, and walk alongside you on your journey to a fulfilling life. Discover the difference of truly personalised support.

Name: Wichita Ability Expo
Description:
The Wichita Ability Expo is a first-of-its-kind community event designed to unite individuals of all abilities with the organizations, services, and solutions that support access, independence, and quality of life. This expo brings together families, caregivers, therapists, healthcare professionals, and businesses for a full day of connection, education, and discovery.

Attendees can explore adaptive equipment, assistive technology, therapeutic services, inclusive recreation, community resources, employment opportunities, and more—all in one accessible, welcoming space. Our goal is simple: to strengthen the bridge between the disability community and the providers committed to serving them.

100% of proceeds from the Wichita Ability Expo go directly to The Thrive Foundation, a nonprofit supporting individuals who have experienced a brain injury. The Thrive Foundation fills the critical gap where insurance falls short—funding items, services, and support that help survivors regain independence, participate fully in their communities, and rebuild their lives with dignity.

Whether you're a family seeking resources, a professional looking to connect, or a business dedicated to inclusion, the Wichita Ability Expo offers meaningful opportunities to engage, learn, and make a lasting impact.

Join us as we work together to create access, inspire independence, and build a more inclusive Wichita.

Name: Burden & Bloom
Description:
Having a disability or raising a child with a disability is often described as the most challenging and most rewarding experience anyone will ever have. Burden & Bloom embodies this paradox by helping individuals and families navigate the burden and celebrate the bloom. B&B seeks to aid in finding and understanding resources specifically designed for people with disabilities and their communities. They lack a centralized, user-friendly, transparent platform to access resources in their area, which adds to their challenges and prevents individuals from reaching their full potential.

We aim to hold agencies accountable with the goal of making them better to serve the communities they aim to help. Families, caregivers, and disabled individuals are modeled by relationships and the building of community. We aim to digitize this process and make it more accessible to people everywhere, publishing word-of-mouth communication and relaying information. B&B is committed to streamlining the process of joining this community, making it as seamless as possible. We honor the collective knowledge by recognizing that this resource is continually growing; we rely on the input and contributions of users to ensure that we have the most up-to-date, complete platform possible.

Name: Sunshine Care & Therapy Services
Description:

While some providers are busy ticking boxes, we’re focused on building trust, connection, and independence ,walking alongside you every step of the way.

Our Services Include:

   - Daily living support & household tasks

   - Life skills & capacity building

  -  Social engagement & community participation

   - NDIS Support Coordination & plan implementation

   - Social work advocacy and specialist support

   - Occupational Therapy (OT) assessments & services

   - Mental health coaching & wellbeing support

   - Flexible transport assistance

    Connecting you to the right providers and services

We advocate fiercely for every participant , making sure your goals drive every decision. Our social workers and OT professionals work closely with you to build confidence, choice, and control , because that’s what real care looks like.

We don’t just show up , we connect. Whether it’s helping you budget, plan meals, build social skills, or navigate the NDIS , we’re right there with you.

If you’re ready for support that’s warm, genuine, and built around you, let’s chat.

Get in touch today and take control of your future!

Description:
Die I:NÖ Leben gemeinnützige GmbH bietet professionelle Begleitung und Betreuung für Menschen mit Behinderungen in Wiener Neustadt, Bad Erlach und Lanzenkirchen. Unser Ziel ist es, selbstbestimmte Lebenswege zu ermöglichen und individuelle Potenziale zu stärken – immer mit dem Menschen im Mittelpunkt.

Unser Angebot umfasst:

Wohnen: Voll- und teilbetreute Wohnplätze mit individueller Unterstützung – angepasst an persönliche Bedürfnisse.

Tagesstätten: Strukturierte Tagesgestaltung mit kreativen, praktischen und lebensnahen Aktivitäten zur Förderung von Fähigkeiten und Selbstwirksamkeit.

I:NÖ Service: Gezielte Unterstützung beim Einstieg ins Arbeitsleben durch praktische Tätigkeiten im Bereich Haus- und Gartenservice.

Ferienappartements: Barrierefreie Erholung in der Nähe der Therme Linsberg Asia – offen für alle, mit und ohne Assistenzbedarf.

Eltern- & Angehörigenarbeit: Wir begleiten Familien mit Information, Gesprächen und Austauschformaten – transparent, offen und unterstützend.

Was uns leitet: Der Mensch wird in seiner Einzigartigkeit gesehen – mit seinen Fähigkeiten, dem individuellen Charakter und seinen Zielen. Unter dem Motto „Nichts über uns, ohne uns!“ steht die Selbstbestimmung unserer Kund:innen an oberster Stelle. Wir setzen auf partizipative Strukturen, kontinuierliche Begleitung sowie Stärkung der Kompetenzen aller Beteiligten – inklusive unseres engagierten Teams.

Wir arbeiten gemeinwohlorientiert, gefördert vom Land Niederösterreich.

Name: Tu día a día más fácil
Description:
Hola! Soy Paula , graduada en Bioquímica y emprendedora detrás de "Tu día a día más fácil".

Vivo con Ataxia de Friedreich , una enfermedad neurodegenerativa, y he transformado mi experiencia en una misión profesional: promover la inclusión, la accesibilidad y la calidad de vida para personas con discapacidad.

A través de mis plataformas, creo contenido que no solo inspira, sino que también ofrece soluciones tangibles. Comparto trucos de autonomía, reseñas de productos accesibles y mi perspectiva sobre cómo vivir una vida plena, sin importar los desafíos.

Mi objetivo es doble: por un lado, empoderar a la comunidad de personas con discapacidad mostrándoles que es posible alcanzar sus metas (como graduarme en la universidad!) y, por otro, colaborar con marcas y organizaciones para impulsar un cambio real y duradero.

Estoy convencida de que la verdadera inclusión se construye con empatía, innovación y productos diseñados para todos.

Si tu empresa busca entender y conectar con el público de la discapacidad, o si eres un/a profesional comprometido/a con la accesibilidad, me encantaría conectar y explorar cómo podemos colaborar.

Únete a la conversación y conseguiremos una sociedad más fácil para todos! 

Sígueme en mis otras redes!

 TikTok, Instagram, Facebook y YouTube: tudiaadiamasfacil

#tudiaadiamasfacil #discapacidad #inclusiónlaboral #accesibilidad #emprendimientosocial #bioquimica #ataxiadefriedreich #diversidadfuncional #liderazgoinclusivo

Name: Dad's Place, Inc.
Description:
When my father got to the age of needing assistance, he moved in with me and remained for 5 years.  As his needs grew bigger than I could manage alone, I started to look for nearby homes.  Unfortunately, I could not find what I was looking for.  He went into a "paid for" facility for Veterans, and it took him 2 years of misery to get to the point that he agreed to spend his money on a small home that would provide the care he so desperately needed.  Again, I could not find what I was looking for and settled for “OK” care in Granite Bay.  Very quickly we came up with the plan to start our own RCFE, and Dad's Place was born.  All my life's experiences had led to this moment.  All the skills and knowledge needed to care for the elderly and run a powerful organization led with compassion and proficiency were things I had worked towards my entire life.

The heart of every organization is most clearly defined by its caregivers.  We hire based on the person’s heart and passion for the elderly.  All skills can be taught if the person has the required dedication and affection.
Dad’s Place vision is to provide love to those needing care and do it in a setting as close to home as we can get.  I believe we have achieved that goal, and I am proud to pass this legacy on to my children and grandchildren.

We have 2 locations, one in Auburn and one in Loomis and both are 6 bed homes that feel like home, not an institution.

Name: Uplift Mentoring
Description:
Created a virtual based mentorship program (“Uplift”) with high school students as the mentors, and middle and elementary school students with conditions as the mentees. To clarify, in this context I use the term “conditions” to describe youth with medical (visible and/or invisible) and/ or mental disabilities/disorders. Youth with conditions typically feel isolated from their peers in certain aspects as there are certain things about transitions they will go through in life that abled peers typically do not understand. Uplift would connect them to a group of children with similar struggles. My goal is to create mentor/mentee friendships that are mutually beneficial. Prior to the start of meetings with their mentor, mentees will be able to design (in their application google form) what they want their typical mentor meetings to look like (whether that be having someone to vent with, ask advice about the high school process (typically for middle school aged mentees), help with homework, play a game, etc) so they can be matched with their ideal mentor.
Mentors (who may be abled or disabled) will be trained prior to meeting with their mentees on common issues youth with conditions face, how to interact with younger children, and how to steer conversations more positively and productively to avoid self depreciation, and improve the mentee's sense of self. 
As a cumulative project for the end of this year's program, mentors will help mentees create a biography about their condition, how it affects them, how they are separate from their condition (ie “my condition does not define me”) and what they hope for in their future. Mentors can space this out however they see fit as long as it is done by the end of the program.

Name: Snoez En Bulles
Description: Snoez En bulles c'est avant tout un concept,  un accompagnement des familles à leur domicile et en visio, en structure ou au sein des établissements scolaires. Beaucoup trop d'enfant, bien qu' inscrit auprès des établissements scolaires se retrouvent sans solution de prise en charge, ou ne bénéficient que d'une prise en charge partielle. Les enseignants, souvent peu formés au handicap se retrouvent confrontés à des situations complexes et ont du mal à adapter leur programme d'enseignement pédagogique. Les listes d'attentes pour les diagnostiques, l'accompagnement de nos enfants ne cessent de s'allonger. Une prise en charge précoce est très importante. Je suis apte à réaliser une Evaluation des compétences scolaire grâce à l'outils EDCS et à vous proposer un programme d'accompagnement de soutien scolaire spécifique basée sur la pédagogie Montessori adaptée. Je vous propose du coaching et des séances de  "guidance parentale" en vous aidant à planifier des listes de tâches, des séquentiels, je suis au plus près de vos besoins. Je vous donne des clés afin de mieux appréhender et comprendre les comportements "problèmes" de votre enfant. Je suis là pour vous aider à améliorer votre quotidien en vous proposant une restructuration et un réaménagement de vos espaces basée sur les techniques TEACCH. Je propose de me rendre aux domiciles des particuliers ou des structures (école, institution, etc) pour mettre en place des séances Snoezelen adaptées et individualisées,  des ateliers pédagogiques sensoriels, etc. Je vous oriente si besoin auprès de professionnels qualifiés Je me rend disponible pour travailler en équipe avec les professionnels qui suivent votre enfant afin de les aider dans leur approche et leur donner les clés concernant la prise en charge des enfants avec un trouble du spectre de l'autisme. Enfin,  mon accompagnement auprès des familles est élargie et concerne également les familles dont les enfant n'ont pas de handicap ou de besoin particuliers.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: Homeli Inc.
Description:
At Homeli, Inc., we believe families deserve better when it comes to finding safe, trustworthy elder and disability-friendly housing. Whether seeking assisted living, memory care, or board-and-care homes, families today face overwhelming stress, unclear information, and commission-driven referral services that prioritize profit over people.

Homeli is a Northern California-based startup building a free, transparent digital platform and app to change that. We empower families to search, compare, and connect directly with care providers — without sales pressure, hidden incentives, or biased recommendations.

What makes us different?
 Free for families — no hidden fees or commission-driven referrals.
 Transparent and unbiased — empowering families to make informed decisions on their own terms.
 Inclusive of small and mid-sized providers — giving care facilities affordable ways to increase visibility, not just the big players.
 Local roots, scalable future — starting in California with a vision to expand nationwide.

Founded by healthcare professionals with over 16 years of frontline experience, Homeli was built from firsthand insight into the pain points families face and the strain placed on overworked hospital systems and case management teams. We’re on a mission to redesign how care transitions happen — making the process easier, more equitable, and more human-centered.

If you’re a family looking for clarity, a care provider seeking visibility, or a partner ready to innovate in elder and disability care, we’d love to connect.

 Learn more at gohomeli.com

Name: Fair Care Idaho
Description:
Fair Care Idaho is a parent-led, lived-experience–driven nonprofit dedicated to strengthening Idaho’s disability and family care systems. We elevate caregiver voices, share real family stories, and bring clarity to complex policy changes that directly impact the lives of disabled Idahoans.

Our work is grounded in the belief that families thrive when they have access to stable supports, clear information, and a community that understands the realities of caregiving. 

Through education, storytelling, data collection, and statewide collaboration (non-lobbying), we help families navigate Idaho’s home and community-based services while ensuring their experiences inform conversations about the future of care.

We support a coalition of thousands of families, publish accessible policy guides and analyses, and host ongoing community-driven initiatives such as Walk in Our Shoes, a storytelling project that captures the lived experiences of disabled Idahoans, their families, and caregivers/providers.

Fair Care Idaho exists to make the system easier to understand, the path forward clearer, and Idaho families stronger, together.

Name: Disability Friendly
Description:
A small independent family company based in Northampton, that can provide a bespoke assessment to your business premises as seen from a disabled persons point of view highlighting areas of opportunity to enhance the experience for your less abled guests.

I am a bilateral leg amputee for over 10 years, a manual wheelchair and mobility scooter user. Due to injury I also have weakness in my upper limbs and problems with fine dexterity. I see and face challenges every day. 

I am mentally strong, determined and want to use what I have learnt in life to help improve the experiences of other less abled people.

Over the years I have seen so many things that able bodied people take for granted or do not even see, yet from a disabled persons point of view they can be a big challenge or obstacle.

Name: NewDirections
Description:
At NewDirections, our first and foremost priority is identifying the needs of caregivers in each employer’s workforce, finding solutions tailored to the employer and its workforce, and formalizing a clear plan of action. Employers who have formal policies convey support for family caregiving in the workplace. Family supportive policies broadly have been shown to encourage employee retention, increase recruitment of new employees, and improve worker productivity.

NewDirections provides expert information about managing family caregiving and caregiving resources, which can include employee assistance programs; onsite experts; and expert workshops, webinars, and guides. We provide transparency, education, and connection to your best and most appropriate options, all in an unbiased manner and without high-pressure commission sales tactics currently experienced with other solutions.
Primary Industry: Services for the Elderly and Disabled

Name: IE Support Coordination
Description:
Are you looking for a dedicated Support Coordinator to assist you to navigate and optimise your NDIS funding?

Name: Stripes Home Care
Description:
Stripes Home Care is a NDIS support services provider based in Canberra. We provide support to make a difference in the lives of people with disability. Our team is culturally diverse and always available to assist people with disability or supervise their execution of personal tasks during their day-to-day life that enable them to live as independently as possible. We provide support in different environments including the homes of people with disability or when in the community. We assist with:
•	personal hygiene, including showering, bathing, oral hygiene, dressing and grooming, toileting, eating and drinking
•	house cleaning and other household activities
•	attending appointments, use of aids and appliances, hearing and communication devices 
•	mobility and transferring, for example moving in and out of bed and on or off the toilet; or application of splints, basic first aid due to injuries sustained because of a participant’s disability.
•	preparation and delivery of meals
We also support people with disability to choose the home they want to live in and set up support in a way that best suits them. In the home we help or supervise them whilst doing daily tasks that enables them to live as independently as possible, building their skills including helping them with personal care tasks, or cooking meals.
We can also support people with disability to find short term accommodation, including respite, for a short time away from their usual home up to 14 days at a time. Clients might have a short stay with other people, or by themselves when their usual carers aren’t available, or for them to try new things.
We’re constantly training our team to positively impact the lives of people with disability.  Learn more at https://www.stripeshomecare.com.au and follow us on Instagram (@stripeshomecare) and Facebook (@stripeshomecare).

Name: Takiwātanga Ki Tāmaki Makaurau
Description:
Our mission is to create a community in Tāmaki Makaurau (Auckland) in a Te Ao Māori worldview; where Takiwātanga (Autism) is nurtured and celebrated.

Our kaupapa is based through Te Ao Māori and Tikanga Māori.

All tākiwatanga and whanau are empowered to determine their aspirations in all aspects of Hauora (Health) ; Taha Tinana (Physical), Hinengaro (Intellectual), Mauri (Values & Beliefs) and Wairua (Soul).

We want to shift the negative culture, whakamā and stigma associated with Takiwātanga and turn it into a more positive one, by recognising one’s strengths, gifts and uniqueness – just as our tupuna did.

Our Values:

Aroha – Is the foundation our kaupapa is motivated by. It is important to show kindness and compassion towards our whanau (families) in our community; this can be seen, felt and heard through the mahi we do.

Tino Rangatiratanga – Inspire and strengthen whanau (families) to become confident decision – makers while embracing their Takiwātanga identity.

Whanaungatanga – A connection to a community, whanau feel environment to promote a sense of belonging and citizenship within our community, with an emphasis on diversity, inclusiveness and accessibility.

Name: Disability Included
Description:
Facilitating the seamless integration and sustained employment of individuals with disabilities involves a comprehensive range of support services: 

Recruitment Assistance: We connect with organisations of persons with disabilities, schools, higher education institutions, and recruitment agencies to ensure a diverse and inclusive talent pool. 

Interview Support: We collaborate with both prospective employees with disabilities and employers, addressing accommodation needs, ensuring venue accessibility, and modifying testing and interview questions to create an inclusive and accessible process. 

Induction Training Assistance: We assist with accessibility of venues, training materials, and health and safety aspects during induction, ensuring that all employees feel welcome and accommodated. 

Workplace Preparations: Our services extend to disability sensitisation training for employers and colleagues. We provide practical and customised advice on suitable reasonable accommodations and assistive devices, in order to create an inclusive work environment. 

Training and Meetings Support: Whether it's providing Sign Language interpreting, note taking or assisting with seating arrangements, we ensure that all training sessions and meetings are accessible to everyone.

Name: Tippecanoe Senior Services
Description:
Tippecanoe Senior Services (TSS) is a 501c3 non-profit organization that provides senior citizens in Tippecanoe County with services and programs that promote overall wellness.

Formerly known as Tippecanoe County Council on Aging and The Center at Jenk’s Rest, we have been providing services to seniors since 1973. Today, we operate four programs designed to meet the needs of the senior population:

1) Tippecanoe Senior Center
We provide a place for seniors to enjoy activities and fellowship with their peers.  

2) Meals on Wheels
Meals on Wheels is a community-based senior nutrition program that addresses food insecurity and isolation among seniors and other home-bound persons. 

3) SHARP
Our Senior Home Assistance Repair Program (SHARP) provides home repairs to help seniors maintain a safe, accessible, and sanitary environment. 

4) TAP
Our Transportation Assistance Program (TAP) provides transportation to approved medical appointments utilizing professional, contracted third-party transport services. 

To learn more, visit our website tippseniorservices.org.

Name: Rwanda Vulnerable People Disability Organisation
Description:
Rwanda Vulnerable People Disability Organisation (RVPDO) is a non-profit organization dedicated to promote the inclusion of persons living with disabilities in the development of communities. We aim to advocate for their rights and empowerment, working towards building a more inclusive society. Through various programs and initiatives RVPDO aims to support and uplift the lives of individuals with disabilities, creating equal opportunities for their active participation in society.

Services and Programs:
1.	Rights advocacy: RVPDO actively works towards advocating for the rights of persons living with disabilities by raising awareness, lobbying and influencing policy changes. We collaborate with local, national, and international organizations to ensure disability rights are protected and enforced.
2.	Empowerment Programs: We develop and implement programs that empower persons living with disabilities to live independent lives. These programs include skill development workshops, training on assistive technologies, and providing resources for professional development. Our aim is to enhance their confidence, abilities and self-reliance.
3.	Community Integration: RVPDO strives to promote the inclusion of persons living with disabilities within their communities. We organize awareness campaigns, conduct educational workshops, and engage cultural activities to facilitate interaction and understanding between individuals with and without disabilities. Our goal is to break down the barriers and build inclusive communities.
4.	Support Services: We provide support services to persons living with disabilities, that is counseling, therapy, and access to assistive devices based on their specific needs. We collaborate with experts in various fields to ensure a holistic approach towards their well being and inclusive development.

Name: ElderBridge Consulting Group
Description:
ElderBridge Consulting Group - A division of SKYLOCK Solutions

At ElderBridge, we understand that for many seniors and their families, the world of technology and service providers can feel overwhelming and confusing. From managing cellular plans to navigating cable, internet, and telephone services, the sheer volume of options—combined with the fast pace of technological change—can cause unnecessary stress and uncertainty.

That’s where we come in.

ElderBridge offers more than just technical solutions. We provide personalized, compassionate care focused on the unique needs of each senior and their family. Our in-person support goes beyond basic troubleshooting—we take the time to listen, to understand, and to empower our clients so they feel confident and secure in their interactions with modern technology and essential service utilities.

With over 35 years of experience in IT and cybersecurity, our team brings deep technical expertise and a passion for protecting individuals from the risks of online threats. But our journey to founding ElderBridge was deeply personal.

After the loss of a parent, I witnessed firsthand the emotional and practical challenges my surviving parent faced in adapting to a digital world—from managing services to confronting online security concerns. What struck me most wasn’t just the complexity of the technology—but the emotional toll it took. The fear of scams, the anxiety of making mistakes, and the feeling of vulnerability during an already difficult time revealed a critical gap in the support available to seniors.

ElderBridge was born from that experience—with a mission to bridge the gap between technology and trust, offering seniors and their families the guidance, reassurance, and protection they deserve.

Description:
Welcome to Liberty Life Skills, a Supported Living Services agency with 
an unwavering commitment to enhancing the lives of individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities. At Liberty Life Skills, we are not just an agency; we are a community-driven force committed to empowering lives through Supported Living Services (SLS). Our philosophy revolves around recognizing the unique potential within each individual, and our dedicated team is passionate about fostering an environment where abilities flourish.

With a focus on person-centered care, our programs are tailored to meet the specific needs and aspirations of those we serve. At the heart of our mission is a commitment to enhancing the quality of life for individuals with developmental disabilities by promoting self-determination, community integration, and skill development.

What sets Liberty Life Skills apart is our unwavering dedication to creating a supportive, inclusive, and compassionate space. Our team of skilled professionals is here not only to provide assistance but to collaborate with individuals, their families, and the community, ensuring a holistic and empowering approach to independent living. Our rich history is a testament to the relationships we've built and the positive impact we've made on countless lives.

Join us on this transformative journey, where Liberty Life Skills becomes a partner in realizing dreams, celebrating achievements, and fostering a future filled with possibilities. Together, we're building a community where everyone, regardless of ability, can thrive and lead a life of purpose and fulfillment.

Name: Innergrowth Hub
Description:
At Innergrowth Hub, we are a registered NDIS provider offering a comprehensive range of support services for individuals living with disabilities. Our mission is to empower participants by enhancing their independence and improving their quality of life through tailored, person-centered care.

Our services include:

Activities of Daily Living (ADL): Supporting participants in maintaining independence with everyday tasks.

Supported Independent Living (SIL): Helping participants live independently in shared or individual living arrangements.

Personal and Complex Care: Offering specialized care such as PEG feeding, dysphagia management, and complex bowel care.

Community Access & Travel: Encouraging community participation and providing accessible transport solutions.

Short-Term Accommodation (STA) & Respite: Providing temporary care to give participants and their families a break.

24-Hour Care and Day Programs: Ensuring continuous support and engaging day activities that promote social skills and personal development.

At Innergrowth Hub, we work closely with participants, their families, and support coordinators to create personalised care plans that reflect each individual's needs, goals, and preferences. 

Our dedicated team is committed to providing high-quality care and support to help participants achieve their goals and live fulfilling lives.

Learn more about how Innergrowth Hub can support you or your loved ones by visiting our website or getting in touch with us today.

Name: Segues
Description:
Segues is a full-service, senior move management company. No matter where you are in your own downsizing, packing, moving, settling-in, or clearing-out process, we can lighten your load with:

As little or as much help as you need
Over a short or extended period of time
Now…in the future…or gradually over months

Among our many personalized services to help you downsize, move and settle in, and/or clear out Segues can:



Plan and coordinate your entire move
Stage your current home for resale
Professionally pack, move, and/or ship your possessions to specified destinations
Unpack and decoratively arrange things in your new home
Provide free boxes and supplies (for local moves)
Remove all boxes and packing materials
Arrange for donation, auction, and consignment sale of unneeded items
Clear and clean former residence
Provide other move or liquidation services

To help you decide what you need and want, Segues offers a FREE CONSULTATION. 

We’ll come to your home and discuss cost-effective options to make your move as physically and emotionally easy as it can be. 

While we're there, we’ll provide a written estimate for Segues services. And for any other services that subsequently we arrange on your behalf, we will bill them at provider rates with no additional markups. Learn how Segues senior move specialists can help YOU make a smooth move and easy transition.

Description:
Ruby, Betsy, and Milo Too Assistance Dog Training is a UK-based business led by Ruby Welsford, an openly Autistic Force Free Dog Trainer. Ruby is highly qualified and experienced, holding certifications from prestigious organizations such as IMDT, Pet Professional Guild, Dog Training College, Association of Animal Behaviour Professionals, and The Professional Association of Canine Trainers. 

Ruby offers seminars and training sessions nationwide, specializing in Disc Dog, Trick Dog, and Clicker Training. She also assists individuals in training their own assistance dogs and provides consultation to organizations and charities. Ruby's dedication to promoting force-free training methods and her innovative "Pay What You Can" intense real-life training days make her services accessible to responsible dog owners across the UK.

Name: Reignite Support
Description:
Welcome to Reignite Support, a dedicated NDIS Service Provider in Adelaide and regional South Australia. At Reignite Support people are at the heart of everything we do and we are committed to support, empower and assist them to achieve their goals and aspirations. Our team of skilled and experienced professionals is passionate about supporting people with disabilities and/or mental health challenges irrespective of gender, abilities, sexuality, age or any other differences. Reignite Support also has a good understanding of culturally and linguistically diverse communities and the issues they may face due to the language barriers. That’s why we aim to provide personalized living and care solutions that are as individual as the people we serve.

At Reignite Support, we also understand that there are thousands of ways that people’s brains process information, function and present behaviourally, therefore we embrace the concept of neurodiversity that simply indicates how diverse the human population is. Rather than thinking there is something wrong or problematic when some people don’t operate similarly to others, neurodiversity embraces all differences. At Reignite Support, restrictive practices are considered as a last resort, our experienced support team will work with our participant’s support networks to implement the restrictive practices and report them as per the NDIS Commission guidelines. We place high emphasis on the privacy of every participant and store documentation securely and retrieved it safely only by the authorised personnel.

Name: The Right Way Co
Description:
At The Right Way company, we exist to champion the rights and aspirations of individuals with disabilities in the NDIS landscape. Our family-owned and operated team has been working in the NDIS landscape since 2020, shaped by our personal experiences of growing up with family members facing disabilities. With a deep understanding of the challenges faced by participants, we witnessed the impact of service providers solely driven by growth, losing sight of what truly matters: doing things The Right Way for our clients.

We are a husband and wife duo who recognized a pressing need for a support service business that stays true to the fundamental principle of participant choice. We are driven by the belief that every individual has the right to determine their own path, deciding what services they require, when they need them, and how they need them, all in pursuit of their unique goals.

In our pursuit of our vision and mission, we remain committed to our core values and our clients’ goals and needs. Rather than aspiring to become a large company, our ambition is to remain small, agile, and intimately connected to our clients, ensuring that our services are deeply personalized, reliably delivered, consistently excellent, and always done The Right Way.

Together, we strive to create an environment where individuals with disabilities can flourish, where their voices are heard, and their choices are respected. By remaining true to our values and commitment to doing things The Right Way, we aim to make a meaningful impact on the lives of our clients and their families.

Name: Marado Health
Description:
Welcome to Marado Health. Our mission is to equip elder care providers with the resources needed to deliver responsible, compliant, and individualized care to their aging clients. By focusing on training, regulatory compliance, operational excellence, and resident-centered care, we help facilities and other elder care providers create thriving communities where seniors can live comfortably, securely, and with a sense of purpose.

Our team of health, compliance, and training experts provides services for assisted living facilities that enhance the quality of care. With an average of 20+ years of experience in their respective fields, our team members bring a diverse range of skills that are leveraged to provide a holistic approach, supporting both the business and resident care aspects of assisted living.

Our services address some of the most critical aspects of assisted living facility management. Currently, we provide:

State-mandated courses and certification for assisted living facilities and their managers in the state of Maryland

Operational training services that improve efficiency within assisted living facilities, enhancing the quality of life for their residents

Compliance management services that ensure facilities adhere to state and federal regulations, helping them avoid financial, operational, and legal penalties

Soon, we’ll expand these offerings to other states. Ultimately, we look forward to providing a broader suite of services and consulting solutions to elder care facilities, all designed to elevate industry standards and meet the unique needs of seniors and their families.

Join us in our mission to elevate the standard of elder care and make a meaningful difference in the lives of those we serve.

Aunt Ava is your friendly voice on the phone – here to help when things get overwhelming, confusing, or just a bit too hard to do alone.

We provide real-time, person-to-person assistance over the phone for seniors, disabled people, and anyone who prefers a bit of human support instead of a screen. 

Our service is accessible, judgment-free, and designed to empower. You don’t need to use a computer or smartphone – just pick up the phone and dial 0508 AuntAva (0508 286 8282) to speak to a real person who’s ready to help.

Aunt Ava was founded by a blind New Zealander who knows first hand how important it is to have support that’s patient, clear, and respectful.

That’s why we’re passionate about removing barriers and making everyday tasks easier for those who need a hand.

We help people who don’t use the internet with things like:

 Help with individualised funding
 Booking doctor or hair appointments
 Paying bills
 Ordering groceries or transport
 Filling out forms (benefits, funding, etc.)

 Just having someone to talk to!

Our services, including support brokerage, personal support, and community development, are designed to foster independence, inclusion, and meaningful community participation. Guided by our mission, “It Takes a Village,” we strive to create a supportive environment where every voice matters, enabling individuals to thrive and achieve their fullest potential.

Mission 
At Village Values LLC, we embrace the power of community and collective support, guided by our motto, ‘It Takes a Village.’ Our mission is to create an inclusive environment where every voice matters, empowering individuals to thrive in their communities.

Through personalized support and collaboration, we equip our members with the tools and resources they need to Venture, Interact, Learn, Leverage, Advocate, Guide, and Engage with their peers and community members. We believe that by fostering independence, inclusion, and fulfillment, we contribute to building a vibrant and supportive community.

Together, we unite as a community to profoundly impact the lives of those we support. Because, at Village Values LLC, we truly understand that ‘It Takes a Village’.
